source: “Eighteen years!” said the passenger, looking at the sun. “Gracious Creator of day! To be buried alive for eighteen years!”
domestication: “十八年啊！”那位乘客凝望着阳光说，“天哪！被活埋了十八年，这简直是天底下最惨的事儿！”
foreignization: “十八年！”乘客看着太阳说。“仁慈的白天的造物主！被活埋了十八年！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Eighteen years!” said the passenger, looking at the sun. “Gracious Creator of day! To be buried alive for eighteen years!”
“十八年啊！”那位乘客凝望着阳光说，“天哪！被活埋了十八年，这简直是天底下最惨的事儿！”

messages_foreignization.content:

source: It was but the inquiry of his first confusion and astonishment, the answer being obvious. If the impression were not produced by a real corresponding and sufficient cause, how came he, Jarvis Lorry, there? How came he to have fallen asleep, in his clothes, on the sofa in Doctor Manette’s consulting-room, and to be debating these points outside the Doctor’s bedroom door in the early morning?
domestication: 不过，这只是他最初的困惑和惊讶的疑问罢了，答案其实很明显。如果这印象不是由真正的、相应的、足够的原因产生的，那么，他，贾维斯·洛里，是怎么会来到这里的呢？他是怎么在曼尼特医生的诊所里，在沙发上穿着衣服睡着了的，又是怎么在清早的时候站在医生的卧室门外思考这些问题的呢？这一切如果没有一个合理的原因，实在让人费解。
foreignization: 这只不过是他最初的困惑和惊讶的询问，而答案显而易见。如果这一印象不是由一个真正的相应且充分的原因产生的，那么他，贾维斯·洛里，是怎么来到这里的呢？他是如何在医生曼尼特的诊疗室的沙发上穿着衣服睡着，然后在清晨在医生卧室门外辩论这些观点的呢？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was but the inquiry of his first confusion and astonishment, the answer being obvious. If the impression were not produced by a real corresponding and sufficient cause, how came he, Jarvis Lorry, there? How came he to have fallen asleep, in his clothes, on the sofa in Doctor Manette’s consulting-room, and to be debating these points outside the Doctor’s bedroom door in the early morning?
不过，这只是他最初的困惑和惊讶的疑问罢了，答案其实很明显。如果这印象不是由真正的、相应的、足够的原因产生的，那么，他，贾维斯·洛里，是怎么会来到这里的呢？他是怎么在曼尼特医生的诊所里，在沙发上穿着衣服睡着了的，又是怎么在清早的时候站在医生的卧室门外思考这些问题的呢？这一切如果没有一个合理的原因，实在让人费解。

source: It was Stryver’s grand peculiarity that he always seemed too big for any place, or space. He was so much too big for Tellson’s, that old clerks in distant corners looked up with looks of remonstrance, as though he squeezed them against the wall. The House itself, magnificently reading the paper quite in the far-off perspective, lowered displeased, as if the Stryver head had been butted into its responsible waistcoat.
domestication: 史特莱弗有个很奇怪的地方，那就是他总显得太大，哪儿都容不下他。他实在太大了，放在泰尔森公司显得格格不入，连远处角落里那些老职员也会抬头看他一眼，好像他正在把他们挤到墙边。就算是整个泰尔森公司的大楼，看起来也有些不自在，就像它那尊贵的胸襟被史特莱弗撞得不舒服似的。
foreignization: 斯特莱弗最大的特点是他总是显得太大，超出了任何地方或空间。他对于泰尔森公司来说实在太大了，以至于远处角落里那些老职员抬头看着他，带着责备的神情，就好像他在挤压他们，逼迫他们贴近墙壁。这座房子本身，威严地阅读着报纸，远远地躺在视线的尽头，显出不悦的表情，仿佛斯特莱弗的头撞进了它那端庄的马甲。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was Stryver’s grand peculiarity that he always seemed too big for any place, or space. He was so much too big for Tellson’s, that old clerks in distant corners looked up with looks of remonstrance, as though he squeezed them against the wall. The House itself, magnificently reading the paper quite in the far-off perspective, lowered displeased, as if the Stryver head had been butted into its responsible waistcoat.
史特莱弗有个很奇怪的地方，那就是他总显得太大，哪儿都容不下他。他实在太大了，放在泰尔森公司显得格格不入，连远处角落里那些老职员也会抬头看他一眼，好像他正在把他们挤到墙边。就算是整个泰尔森公司的大楼，看起来也有些不自在，就像它那尊贵的胸襟被史特莱弗撞得不舒服似的。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was Stryver’s grand peculiarity that he always seemed too big for any place, or space. He was so much too big for Tellson’s, that old clerks in distant corners looked up with looks of remonstrance, as though he squeezed them against the wall. The House itself, magnificently reading the paper quite in the far-off perspective, lowered displeased, as if the Stryver head had been butted into its responsible waistcoat.
斯特莱弗最大的特点是他总是显得太大，超出了任何地方或空间。他对于泰尔森公司来说实在太大了，以至于远处角落里那些老职员抬头看着他，带着责备的神情，就好像他在挤压他们，逼迫他们贴近墙壁。这座房子本身，威严地阅读着报纸，远远地躺在视线的尽头，显出不悦的表情，仿佛斯特莱弗的头撞进了它那端庄的马甲。

source: The prison of La Force was a gloomy prison, dark and filthy, and with a horrible smell of foul sleep in it. Extraordinary how soon the noisome flavour of imprisoned sleep, becomes manifest in all such places that are ill cared for!
domestication: 拉福士监狱是一个阴森的监牢，黑暗潮湿，里面到处都有一股难闻的臭味，简直就是个污秽不堪的地方。真奇怪，没人照管的监狱，怎么都快得让人难以忍受的臭气弥漫其中呢。
foreignization: 拉福斯监狱是一个阴郁的监狱，黑暗而肮脏，里面散发着可怕的污秽睡眠的气味。很奇怪的是，在所有这些照顾不周的地方，被监禁的睡眠的令人作呕的气味竟然这么快就显现出来了！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The prison of La Force was a gloomy prison, dark and filthy, and with a horrible smell of foul sleep in it. Extraordinary how soon the noisome flavour of imprisoned sleep, becomes manifest in all such places that are ill cared for!
拉福士监狱是一个阴森的监牢，黑暗潮湿，里面到处都有一股难闻的臭味，简直就是个污秽不堪的地方。真奇怪，没人照管的监狱，怎么都快得让人难以忍受的臭气弥漫其中呢。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The prison of La Force was a gloomy prison, dark and filthy, and with a horrible smell of foul sleep in it. Extraordinary how soon the noisome flavour of imprisoned sleep, becomes manifest in all such places that are ill cared for!
拉福斯监狱是一个阴郁的监狱，黑暗而肮脏，里面散发着可怕的污秽睡眠的气味。很奇怪的是，在所有这些照顾不周的地方，被监禁的睡眠的令人作呕的气味竟然这么快就显现出来了！

source: “Now, don’t let my announcement of the name make you uncomfortable, Sydney,” said Mr. Stryver, preparing him with ostentatious friendliness for the disclosure he was about to make, “because I know you don’t mean half you say; and if you meant it all, it would be of no importance. I make this little preface, because you once mentioned the young lady to me in slighting terms.”
domestication: “喂，悉尼，你别因为我要宣布这个名字就感到尴尬啊，”斯特赖弗先生说，同时带着夸张的友好态度来缓和即将说出的事，“我知道你说的这些话并不是认真的，即便你是认真的，那也没什么大不了的。我提前说这些，是因为你曾经对那个年轻的女士有过轻蔑的评论。”
foreignization: "现在，不要因为我提到名字而让你感到不舒服，悉尼，” 斯特赖弗先生说，他带着张扬的友好态度，为他即将透露的消息做准备，“因为我知道你说的并不是你真正的意思；就算你是认真的，那也无关紧要。我有这个小小的前言，是因为你曾经对我提到过那个年轻的女士，用了一些轻蔑的词句。”

source: The Concord bed-chamber being always assigned to a passenger by the mail, and passengers by the mail being always heavily wrapped up from head to foot, the room had the odd interest for the establishment of the Royal George, that although but one kind of man was seen to go into it, all kinds and varieties of men came out of it. Consequently, another drawer, and two porters, and several maids and the landlady, were all loitering by accident at various points of the road between the Concord and the coffee-room, when a gentleman of sixty, formally dressed in a brown suit of clothes, pretty well worn, but very well kept, with large square cuffs and large flaps to the pockets, passed along on his way to his breakfast.
domestication: 在皇家乔治酒店，有一个很有意思的现象，总是会给乘坐邮车的客人安排康科德房间，而乘坐邮车的人一般都会从头到脚裹得严严实实。因此，尽管只有同一类型的人进入这个房间，但出来的人却形形色色，各式各样。这导致了另一名服务生、两个行李搬运工、几位女佣和老板娘都“碰巧”在康科德房间和咖啡厅之间的路上各处徘徊。当一位六十岁的绅士穿着褪色的棕色西装走过时，他的衣服虽然已有些磨损，但保养得非常好，袖口很宽大，口袋很大，他前往早餐室的路上吸引了众人的注意。
foreignization: 康科德卧室总是被分配给邮件乘客， 而邮件乘客总是从头到脚被厚厚地裹着， 这间房子对皇家乔治酒店有着奇怪的趣味，尽管只有一种男人被看到进去， 各种各样的男人却从里面出来。 因此，当一位六十岁的绅士身穿褐色西装，款式正式，虽然有些磨损但保养得很好，袖口很大，口袋很大，沿着路走向他的早餐时，另一位店员，两个门房，还有几位女佣和女老板，都恰巧在康科德房间和咖啡馆之间的道路上的不同点逗留。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The Concord bed-chamber being always assigned to a passenger by the mail, and passengers by the mail being always heavily wrapped up from head to foot, the room had the odd interest for the establishment of the Royal George, that although but one kind of man was seen to go into it, all kinds and varieties of men came out of it. Consequently, another drawer, and two porters, and several maids and the landlady, were all loitering by accident at various points of the road between the Concord and the coffee-room, when a gentleman of sixty, formally dressed in a brown suit of clothes, pretty well worn, but very well kept, with large square cuffs and large flaps to the pockets, passed along on his way to his breakfast.

source: Any one of these partners would have disinherited his son on the question of rebuilding Tellson’s. In this respect the House was much on a par with the Country; which did very often disinherit its sons for suggesting improvements in laws and customs that had long been highly objectionable, but were only the more respectable.
domestication: 这些合伙人有个共同的特点，那就是谁要是敢在重建泰尔森银行的事上提出异议，他们就会毫不犹豫地把自己的儿子赶出家门。在这方面，泰尔森银行和整个国家其实差不多。国家也经常会因为有人提出了修改那些已经存在很久、早就引起众人反感的法律和习俗而抛弃自己的子民。这些法律和习俗虽然早就应该被改进，但由于历史悠久，反而显得更加值得尊敬。
foreignization: 这些合伙人中任何一个人都会因为改建泰尔森银行的问题而将自己的儿子逐出继承权。在这方面，泰尔森银行与整个国家十分相似；这个国家经常会因为有人建议修改那些已经被反对很久但是由于时间长了反而更值得尊敬的法律和风俗而将其子女逐出继承权。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Any one of these partners would have disinherited his son on the question of rebuilding Tellson’s. In this respect the House was much on a par with the Country; which did very often disinherit its sons for suggesting improvements in laws and customs that had long been highly objectionable, but were only the more respectable.
这些合伙人有个共同的特点，那就是谁要是敢在重建泰尔森银行的事上提出异议，他们就会毫不犹豫地把自己的儿子赶出家门。在这方面，泰尔森银行和整个国家其实差不多。国家也经常会因为有人提出了修改那些已经存在很久、早就引起众人反感的法律和习俗而抛弃自己的子民。这些法律和习俗虽然早就应该被改进，但由于历史悠久，反而显得更加值得尊敬。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Any one of these partners would have disinherited his son on the question of rebuilding Tellson’s. In this respect the House was much on a par with the Country; which did very often disinherit its sons for suggesting improvements in laws and customs that had long been highly objectionable, but were only the more respectable.
这些合伙人中任何一个人都会因为改建泰尔森银行的问题而将自己的儿子逐出继承权。在这方面，泰尔森银行与整个国家十分相似；这个国家经常会因为有人建议修改那些已经被反对很久但是由于时间长了反而更值得尊敬的法律和风俗而将其子女逐出继承权。

source: He stooped a little, and with his tattered blue cap pointed under the carriage. All his fellows stooped to look under the carriage.
domestication: 他稍微弯了弯腰，用他那破旧的蓝色帽子往车底下一指。他的同伴们也都弯着腰去看车底下。结果，大家伙儿都低头往车底瞧。
foreignization: 他俯下身去，并且用他那破旧的蓝帽子指向马车底下。所有他的伙伴都俯下身去往马车底下看。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He stooped a little, and with his tattered blue cap pointed under the carriage. All his fellows stooped to look under the carriage.
他稍微弯了弯腰，用他那破旧的蓝色帽子往车底下一指。他的同伴们也都弯着腰去看车底下。结果，大家伙儿都低头往车底瞧。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He stooped a little, and with his tattered blue cap pointed under the carriage. All his fellows stooped to look under the carriage.
他俯下身去，并且用他那破旧的蓝帽子指向马车底下。所有他的伙伴都俯下身去往马车底下看。

source: The gentleman from Tellson’s had nothing left for it but to empty his glass with an air of stolid desperation, settle his odd little flaxen wig at the ears, and follow the waiter to Miss Manette’s apartment. It was a large, dark room, furnished in a funereal manner with black horsehair, and loaded with heavy dark tables. These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.
domestication: 泰尔森公司的这位先生也无计可施，只能抱着一杯酒，装出一副死不瞑目的样子，整理一下他那奇怪的小黄色假发，跟着服务员前往曼尼特小姐的住处。她的房间很大，光线很暗，黑色马鬃沙发和扶手椅摆放得满满当当，沉重的深色木桌上到处都是油迹。这些家具被擦拭得锃亮，但反而显得更加阴郁。房间中央的大桌子上点着两根高大的蜡烛，它们的影子被映射在每片叶子上，就好像它们被埋葬在黑色乌木的深坑中，除了等待被挖掘出来，否则别想从中得到任何光明。整个房间布置得像一个丧礼现场，令人感到十分压抑。
foreignization: 泰尔森公司的那位先生只好绝望地喝完酒杯里的酒，调整一下他那奇怪的小型亚麻色假发，将它整理到耳朵旁，然后跟着服务员去曼妮特小姐的房间。 这是一个又大又黑的房间，里面装饰的非常阴郁，到处都是黑色的马鬃，房间里摆满了沉重的黑色桌子。这些桌子被擦得油光锃亮，房间中央桌子上的两根高大的蜡烛在每一片叶子上都映照出了一种阴森的光芒；就好像它们被深埋在地下，深深地埋葬在黑红木制的坟墓中，一点光亮都没有，直到它们被挖出来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The gentleman from Tellson’s had nothing left for it but to empty his glass with an air of stolid desperation, settle his odd little flaxen wig at the ears, and follow the waiter to Miss Manette’s apartment. It was a large, dark room, furnished in a funereal manner with black horsehair, and loaded with heavy dark tables. These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.

泰尔森公司的这位先生也无计可施，只能抱着一杯酒，装出一副死不瞑目的样子，整理一下他那奇怪的小黄色假发，跟着服务员前往曼尼特小姐的住处。她的房间很大，光线很暗，黑色马鬃沙发和扶手椅摆放得满满当当，沉重的深色木桌上到处都是油迹。这些家具被擦拭得锃亮，但反而显得更加阴郁。房间中央的大桌子上点着两根高大的蜡烛，它们的影子被映射在每片叶子上，就好像它们被埋葬在黑色乌木的深坑中，除了等待被挖掘出来，否则别想从中得到任何光明。整个房间布置得像一个丧礼现场，令人感到十分压抑。

These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.

source: This answer had a happy effect upon the audience. Cries in exaltation of the well-known good physician rent the hall. So capriciously were the people moved, that tears immediately rolled down several ferocious countenances which had been glaring at the prisoner a moment before, as if with impatience to pluck him out into the streets and kill him.
domestication: 观众们听到这句话后，顿时变得欢呼雀跃。赞扬这位著名好医生的喊声响彻整个大厅，人群的情绪忽然发生了转变。刚才那些凶神恶煞的面孔，刚才还在怒目圆睁地盯着囚犯，仿佛迫不及待地想把他拉到街上去杀掉，但现在却突然流下了眼泪。人心可以很善良，但也可以很残忍，关键就在一念之间。
foreignization: 这个回答对听众产生了很好的效果。人们在赞扬这位著名的好医生的同时，欢呼声充满了整个大厅。 人们如此任性地被感动着，以至于几张刚才还怒气冲天地瞪着囚犯的凶恶面孔上，眼泪顿时滚滚而下，就好像刚才他们还急不可耐地想要把他从法庭上拖到街上去杀掉一样。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

This answer had a happy effect upon the audience. Cries in exaltation of the well-known good physician rent the hall. So capriciously were the people moved, that tears immediately rolled down several ferocious countenances which had been glaring at the prisoner a moment before, as if with impatience to pluck him out into the streets and kill him.
观众们听到这句话后，顿时变得欢呼雀跃。赞扬这位著名好医生的喊声响彻整个大厅，人群的情绪忽然发生了转变。刚才那些凶神恶煞的面孔，刚才还在怒目圆睁地盯着囚犯，仿佛迫不及待地想把他拉到街上去杀掉，但现在却突然流下了眼泪。人心可以很善良，但也可以很残忍，关键就在一念之间。

source: The mender of roads looked through rather than at the low ceiling, and pointed as if he saw the gallows somewhere in the sky.
domestication: 修路工的目光穿透了低矮的天花板，仿佛他在远处的天空中看见了什么似的，伸出手指着。他的手指直指苍天，好像在那里有一个绞刑架若隐若现。
foreignization: 修路工人透过而非注视着低矮的天花板，并且指向仿佛他看见绞刑台在某处天空中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The mender of roads looked through rather than at the low ceiling, and pointed as if he saw the gallows somewhere in the sky.
修路工的目光穿透了低矮的天花板，仿佛他在远处的天空中看见了什么似的，伸出手指着。他的手指直指苍天，好像在那里有一个绞刑架若隐若现。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The mender of roads looked through rather than at the low ceiling, and pointed as if he saw the gallows somewhere in the sky.
修路工人透过而非注视着低矮的天花板，并且指向仿佛他看见绞刑台在某处天空中。

source: The drunken patriot had them in his cap, and produced them. Casting his eyes over Gabelle’s letter, the same personage in authority showed some disorder and surprise, and looked at Darnay with a close attention.
domestication: 这个醉醺醺的爱国者从帽子里拿出了文件，拿给那位权威人士看。权威人士扫了一眼盖贝尔的信，神色有些慌乱和惊讶，紧紧地盯着丹雷，看得他浑身发毛。这下子丹雷可就糟了，权威人士的眼睛盯着他，像要把他的心肝都看穿似的。
foreignization: 这个醉酒的爱国者把信拿在帽子里，然后拿出来了。当他扫视着加贝尔的信时，同一个权威人物表现出了一些混乱和惊讶，并且用密切的注意力看着达尼。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The drunken patriot had them in his cap, and produced them. Casting his eyes over Gabelle’s letter, the same personage in authority showed some disorder and surprise, and looked at Darnay with a close attention.
这个醉醺醺的爱国者从帽子里拿出了文件，拿给那位权威人士看。权威人士扫了一眼盖贝尔的信，神色有些慌乱和惊讶，紧紧地盯着丹雷，看得他浑身发毛。这下子丹雷可就糟了，权威人士的眼睛盯着他，像要把他的心肝都看穿似的。

source: Therefore, when Sunday came, the mender of roads was not enchanted (though he said he was) to find that madame was to accompany monsieur and himself to Versailles. It was additionally disconcerting to have madame knitting all the way there, in a public conveyance; it was additionally disconcerting yet, to have madame in the crowd in the afternoon, still with her knitting in her hands as the crowd waited to see the carriage of the King and Queen.
domestication: 到了星期天，这位修路工（虽然他自己说很高兴，但其实并不是那么回事）发现夫人会跟着先生和他一起去凡尔赛宫，心里有点儿不痛快。而且，在公共马车上，夫人一路上都在拼命地织毛衣，这更让他觉得尴尬。到了下午，在人群中等待观看国王和王后的马车经过时，夫人还在手里拿着针线，继续织着，这让他更加感到不安。
foreignization: 因此，当星期日到来时，修路工（尽管他说自己很高兴）却并没有被安排在马德姆陪同先生和他一起去凡尔赛的安排所迷住。这还不够令人不安的是，马德姆在公共交通工具上一直在织毛衣；下午在人群中看到马德姆仍然拿着毛衣等待国王和王后的马车，这更是让人感到不安。

source: In a quarter of an hour Monseigneur was ready, and sat down alone to his sumptuous and choice supper. His chair was opposite to the window, and he had taken his soup, and was raising his glass of Bordeaux to his lips, when he put it down.
domestication: 一刻钟不到，莫尼尼欧先生就准备好了，独自坐下来享用他那丰盛精美的晚餐。他的椅子正对着窗户，他已经吃完了汤，正要把一杯波尔多葡萄酒送到嘴边，却突然停住了。

source: “I know, my dear, I know. I have seen it many times. Don’t be frightened! Not one of them would harm you.”

source: “My dear Manette, I am anxious to have your opinion, in confidence, on a very curious case in which I am deeply interested; that is to say, it is very curious to me; perhaps, to your better information it may be less so.”
domestication: 曼妮特老兄，我有件很奇怪的事想跟你商量，我希望你能坦诚地告诉我你的看法。这件事对我来说很耐人寻味，但也许在你眼里就没那么新鲜了，因为你见过的世面比我多。
foreignization: “亲爱的曼妮特，我渴望得到你的保密的意见，关于一个让我深感兴趣的非常奇怪的案例；也就是说，对我来说它是非常奇怪的；也许，对于你更好的信息来说，它可能就没那么奇怪了。”

source: It was the popular theme for jests; it was the best cure for headache, it infallibly prevented the hair from turning grey, it imparted a peculiar delicacy to the complexion, it was the National Razor which shaved close: who kissed La Guillotine, looked through the little window and sneezed into the sack. It was the sign of the regeneration of the human race. It superseded the Cross. Models of it were worn on breasts from which the Cross was discarded, and it was bowed down to and believed in where the Cross was denied.
domestication: 那时候，人们常拿它开玩笑，说它是头痛的最佳解药，能让人头发永不变白，让皮肤变得特别细腻，这就是国民剃刀——断头台，谁亲过它，谁就从小窗口看出去，然后把灵魂吹进袋子里。它是人类新世代的象征，取代了十字架。人们在胸前戴着它的模型，而十字架却被丢弃，人们在那里拒绝十字架，却又对它顶礼膜拜，深信不疑。
foreignization: 它是笑话的流行主题；它是头痛的最佳治疗方法，它不可避免地防止了头发变灰，给皮肤带来了一种特殊的娇嫩， 它是国民剃刀，它剃得很干净：谁与吉约坦接吻，透过小窗户看着，打喷嚏进袋子里。它是人类再生 的标志。它取代了十字架。在胸前戴着它的模型，十字架被丢弃了，它被跪拜并在那里相信，而十字架被否认。

It was the sign of the regeneration of the human race. It superseded the Cross. Models of it were worn on breasts from which the Cross was discarded, and it was bowed down to and believed in where the Cross was denied.

source: To be confronted with such pity, and such earnest youth and beauty, was far more trying to the accused than to be confronted with all the crowd. Standing, as it were, apart with her on the edge of his grave, not all the staring curiosity that looked on, could, for the moment, nerve him to remain quite still. His hurried right hand parcelled out the herbs before him into imaginary beds of flowers in a garden; and his efforts to control and steady his breathing shook the lips from which the colour rushed to his heart. The buzz of the great flies was loud again.
domestication: 面对这样的同情和如此真挚的年轻与美丽，对被告来说比面对人群更让他难以承受。当他仿佛站在墓边，与她相伴时，所有围观的好奇目光在那一刻，都无法让他完全保持镇定。他的右手快速地将面前的草药分配到一个想象中的花园里，而他努力控制和稳住呼吸的样子，使得他的嘴唇因血液涌动而颤抖。大群苍蝇的嗡嗡声再次响起。
foreignization: 面对这样的怜悯，以及如此真诚的年轻和美丽，对被告来说比面对整个人群更为困难。他仿佛与她站在一起，站在他的坟墓边缘，所有好奇地盯着看的目光在那一刻都无法让他完全保持静止。他的右手急忙将他面前的草药分成花园里的虚拟的花坛；他努力控制并稳定自己的呼吸，这使得从他脸上涌向心脏的血色的嘴唇颤抖。那些大苍蝇的嗡嗡声又响起来了。

His hurried right hand parcelled out the herbs before him into imaginary beds of flowers in a garden; and his efforts to control and steady his breathing shook the lips from which the colour rushed to his heart. The buzz of the great flies was loud again.

source: “All sorts of people have been proposed to me, but I will have nothing to say to any of them. I intend to take Jerry. Jerry has been my bodyguard on Sunday nights for a long time past and I am used to him. Nobody will suspect Jerry of being anything but an English bull-dog, or of having any design in his head but to fly at anybody who touches his master.”
domestication: 有各种人被推荐给我，但是我对他们都不感兴趣。我决定带上杰瑞，杰瑞已经当了我很久的星期日晚上的保镖，我也习惯他了。没有人会怀疑杰瑞是除了英国斗牛犬之外的其他东西，或以为他脑子里除了保护主人不受伤害之外还能有其他想法。
foreignization: 各种各样的人都被提议给我，但是我不会对他们有任何兴趣。我打算带上杰瑞。杰瑞已经是我的保镖有一段时间了，每逢星期天晚上都是他陪伴我，我已经习惯了他。没有人会怀疑杰瑞是除了英国斗牛犬以外的其他东西，或者怀疑他除了对碰他主人的人发起攻击之外还有其他任何想法。

source: That might not be so long as the Marquis supposed. If a picture of the chateau as it was to be a very few years hence, and of fifty like it as they too were to be a very few years hence, could have been shown to him that night, he might have been at a loss to claim his own from the ghastly, fire-charred, plunder-wrecked rains. As for the roof he vaunted, he might have found that shutting out the sky in a new way—to wit, for ever, from the eyes of the bodies into which its lead was fired, out of the barrels of a hundred thousand muskets.
domestication: 马克西的话可能没他想象的那么靠谱。要是有人给他看一幅几年后的城堡图，和其他五十座同样被毁的城堡的图，他当晚就可能分不清哪个是自己的了。那些阴森恐怖、烧得黑乎乎、被洗劫一空的废墟，你说哪一个才是他的呢？至于他夸耀的屋顶，可能最终也没能挡住天空——不过是以另一种方式：永远地遮挡了那些被从十万支步枪的枪口射出的子弹打碎的尸体的视线。
foreignization: 这也许不会像马奎斯想象的那么长。如果有一幅图画能够展现出城堡在几年后将会是什么样子，以及其他五十座类似的城堡在几年后会变成什么样子，那么如果当晚有人给他看了这幅图画，他可能会很难从那些满是火烧残迹、战火肆虐、劫掠破坏的废墟中辨认出自己的城堡。至于他夸耀的屋顶，他可能会发现它以一种新方式阻挡了天空——就是说，永远地从被枪林弹雨射入其铅弹的尸体的眼睛中挡住了天空，从十万支步枪的枪口中射出。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

That might not be so long as the Marquis supposed. If a picture of the chateau as it was to be a very few years hence, and of fifty like it as they too were to be a very few years hence, could have been shown to him that night, he might have been at a loss to claim his own from the ghastly, fire-charred, plunder-wrecked rains. As for the roof he vaunted, he might have found that shutting out the sky in a new way—to wit, for ever, from the eyes of the bodies into which its lead was fired, out of the barrels of a hundred thousand muskets.
马克西的话可能没他想象的那么靠谱。要是有人给他看一幅几年后的城堡图，和其他五十座同样被毁的城堡的图，他当晚就可能分不清哪个是自己的了。那些阴森恐怖、烧得黑乎乎、被洗劫一空的废墟，你说哪一个才是他的呢？至于他夸耀的屋顶，可能最终也没能挡住天空——不过是以另一种方式：永远地遮挡了那些被从十万支步枪的枪口射出的子弹打碎的尸体的视线。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

That might not be so long as the Marquis supposed. If a picture of the chateau as it was to be a very few years hence, and of fifty like it as they too were to be a very few years hence, could have been shown to him that night, he might have been at a loss to claim his own from the ghastly, fire-charred, plunder-wrecked rains. As for the roof he vaunted, he might have found that shutting out the sky in a new way—to wit, for ever, from the eyes of the bodies into which its lead was fired, out of the barrels of a hundred thousand muskets.
这也许不会像马奎斯想象的那么长。如果有一幅图画能够展现出城堡在几年后将会是什么样子，以及其他五十座类似的城堡在几年后会变成什么样子，那么如果当晚有人给他看了这幅图画，他可能会很难从那些满是火烧残迹、战火肆虐、劫掠破坏的废墟中辨认出自己的城堡。至于他夸耀的屋顶，他可能会发现它以一种新方式阻挡了天空——就是说，永远地从被枪林弹雨射入其铅弹的尸体的眼睛中挡住了天空，从十万支步枪的枪口中射出。

source: “It’s plain enough, I should think, why he may be. It’s a dreadful remembrance. Besides that, his loss of himself grew out of it. Not knowing how he lost himself, or how he recovered himself, he may never feel certain of not losing himself again. That alone wouldn’t make the subject pleasant, I should think.”
domestication: 这也很正常啊，他之所以这样可能有他的原因。毕竟这是一件很糟糕的事情。另外，这件事情还导致了他对自己的失去控制。由于不知道自己是怎么失控的，也不知道自己是怎么恢复过来的，他可能永远不会确定自己不会再次失控。光是这一点就足以让人感到不愉快了。
foreignization: “恐怕很明显，他为何会这样子。我想这是一个可怕的回忆。此外，他失去自我的原因也是源于此。不清楚自己是怎么丢失自我，又是如何找回自我的，他也许永远不会感到确定自己不会再次丢失自我。我认为，这一件事实就足以使这个话题变得不愉快。”

source: A moment of profound silence followed. Defarge and his wife looked steadfastly at one another. The Vengeance stooped, and the jar of a drum was heard as she moved it at her feet behind the counter.
domestication: 沉默了一会儿，气氛变得十分诡异。德法尔热和他妻子互相凝视着对方，一言不发。复仇女神弯下腰去，在柜台后面搬动了什么东西，发出了一声鼓的闷响。德法尔热夫妇的目光仍然紧紧地盯着对方，一时间，他们似乎都忘了呼吸。
foreignization: 一阵深沉的寂静接着而来。 德伐日和他的妻子坚定地互相凝视着。 复仇女神弯下腰去，一个鼓在她身后柜台下面被碰动发出了声音。

A moment of profound silence followed. Defarge and his wife looked steadfastly at one another. The Vengeance stooped, and the jar of a drum was heard as she moved it at her feet behind the counter.
沉默了一会儿，气氛变得十分诡异。德法尔热和他妻子互相凝视着对方，一言不发。复仇女神弯下腰去，在柜台后面搬动了什么东西，发出了一声鼓的闷响。德法尔热夫妇的目光仍然紧紧地盯着对方，一时间，他们似乎都忘了呼吸。

一阵深沉的寂静接着而来。 德伐日和他的妻子坚定地互相凝视着。 复仇女神弯下腰去，一个鼓在她身后柜台下面被碰动发出了声音。

source: Thus it had come to pass, that Tellson’s was the triumphant perfection of inconvenience. After bursting open a door of idiotic obstinacy with a weak rattle in its throat, you fell into Tellson’s down two steps, and came to your senses in a miserable little shop, with two little counters, where the oldest of men made your cheque shake as if the wind rustled it, while they examined the signature by the dingiest of windows, which were always under a shower-bath of mud from Fleet-street, and which were made the dingier by their own iron bars proper, and the heavy shadow of Temple Bar. If your business necessitated your seeing “the House,” you were put into a species of Condemned Hold at the back, where you meditated on a misspent life, until the House came with its hands in its pockets, and you could hardly blink at it in the dismal twilight. Your money came out of, or went into, wormy old wooden drawers, particles of which flew up your nose and down your throat when they were opened and shut. Your bank-notes had a musty odour, as if they were fast decomposing into rags again. Your plate was stowed away among the neighbouring cesspools, and evil communications corrupted its good polish in a day or two. Your deeds got into extemporised strong-rooms made of kitchens and sculleries, and fretted all the fat out of their parchments into the banking-house air. Your lighter boxes of family papers went up-stairs into a Barmecide room, that always had a great dining-table in it and never had a dinner, and where, even in the year one thousand seven hundred and eighty, the first letters written to you by your old love, or by your little children, were but newly released from the horror of being ogled through the windows, by the heads exposed on Temple Bar with an insensate brutality and ferocity worthy of Abyssinia or Ashantee.
domestication: 于是，泰尔森银行成为了不便的典范。推开一扇带有傻气的门，发出微弱的叮当声后，你就会发现自己走下两步楼梯，进入一个狭小而破旧的店铺，里面有两个小柜台，柜台后面坐着老态龙钟的老人，他们的手在抖动着，好像被风吹得发抖一样，仔细检查你的签名。窗户很暗，总是被来自弗利特街的泥水淋湿，而且铁栏杆又让它们显得更加肮脏，泰晤士寺庙投下的阴影也让整个地方显得更加阴郁。如果你需要见“董事会”，你会被安排到后面的一个类似监狱的房间里，在那里，你会沉思自己错过的生活，直到董事会悠闲地出现，双手插在口袋里，你几乎无法在昏暗的光线中看清他们。你存入或取出的钱都来自生虫的旧木制抽屉，每次打开和关闭抽屉时，木屑都会飞进你的鼻子和嘴巴。你的钞票有一股霉味，好像它们正在迅速分解成废纸一样。你的银器被放在附近的污水坑中，一两天内，就会被邪恶的东西腐蚀掉它们的光泽。你的契约被存放在临时拼凑的保险库里，这些保险库是由厨房和仆人间改造而成的，它们会使羊皮纸上的所有墨迹都渗入银行里的空气中。你轻一些的家书盒被送到了上面的一个房间，那个房间总有一张大餐桌，但永远没有人在那里吃饭，即使是在1780年，你从老情人或小孩子那里收到的第一封信刚刚从窗户里逃脱了被暴徒们残忍和野蛮地窥视的恐怖。
foreignization: 于是，泰尔森公司成为了最终的不便之地。在你打开一扇顽固的门，门后传来微弱的咔嗒声之后，你会沿着两级台阶走下，跌入泰尔森公司，这是一个狭小的商店，里面有两个小柜台。这里的老者们在检查你的支票时，支票就会像被风吹动一样颤抖，他们靠近脏兮兮的窗户检查签名，这些窗户总是被弗利特街飞来的污泥遮蔽，而且被自己铁质的窗格和泰晤士寺庙大门的浓重阴影弄得更加昏暗。如果你的业务需要你拜访“泰尔森公司”，你会被安排到后面的一个类似监牢的地方，在那里你沉思自己度过的糟糕生活，直到泰尔森公司的人出现，手插在口袋里，你几乎无法在昏暗的黄昏中瞥见他们。你的钱从或存入了生虫的旧木制抽屉，当这些抽屉被打开或关闭时，木屑会飞到你的鼻子和喉咙里。你的纸币有霉味，就好像它们正在迅速分解成破布一样。你的盘子被放在邻近的粪池中，恶劣的环境会在一两天内损害其原本良好的光泽。你的契约被放在临时拼凑成的保险库中，这些保险库由厨房和杂物间改造而成，所有文件上的羊皮纸上的油脂都会因为银行里的空气而蒸发掉。你存放家庭文件的较小的盒子被搬到了楼上一个空荡荡的房间，这个房间永远摆着一张大餐桌，但从未摆过宴席，即使是在1780年，你收到的第一封来自爱人或小孩的信，在刚被从窗户里取出后，还带着被暴力和残忍地从泰晤士寺庙悬挂的头颅中窥视的恐惧，充满了埃塞俄比亚或阿散蒂人的那种无知和野蛮。

source: The wine-shop keeper accordingly rolled his eyes about, until they rested upon an elderly gentleman and a young lady, who were seated in a corner. Other company were there: two playing cards, two playing dominoes, three standing by the counter lengthening out a short supply of wine. As he passed behind the counter, he took notice that the elderly gentleman said in a look to the young lady, “This is our man.”
domestication: 这家酒馆老板的目光在房间里转了一圈，最后落在角落里的一对男女身上，一个是年长的绅士，一个是年轻的小姐。此外，店里还有其他人：两个人在打牌，两个人在下骨牌，还有三个人站在柜台旁边，啜饮着酒。老板从柜台后面经过时，注意到那位年长的绅士对年轻的小姐使了个眼神，说：“这就是我们要找的人。”
foreignization: 酒馆老板顺着这声音转过眼珠来，眼光落在一个老先生和一位年轻的女士身上，他们坐在一个角落里。那里还有其他顾客：两个在打牌，两个在下骨牌，有三个人站在柜台旁边，慢慢地品尝着酒。这家店的酒供应不足，顾客们只能慢慢地喝。 当他从柜台后面走过时，他注意到老先生对那位年轻的女士说了一句话，“这个就是我们要找的人。”

酒馆老板顺着这声音转过眼珠来，眼光落在一个老先生和一位年轻的女士身上，他们坐在一个角落里。那里还有其他顾客：两个在打牌，两个在下骨牌，有三个人站在柜台旁边，慢慢地品尝着酒。这家店的酒供应不足，顾客们只能慢慢地喝。 当他从柜台后面走过时，他注意到老先生对那位年轻的女士说了一句话，“这个就是我们要找的人。”

source: “I speak for myself,” answered Mr. Lorry, “and for Mr. Darnay, and for Miss Lucie, and—Miss Lucie, do you not think I may speak for us all?” He asked her the question pointedly, and with a glance at her father.
domestication: “我可以代表我自己说话，”洛里先生回答道，“也可以代表丹尼先生和卢西小姐...”他转头问卢西小姐，“卢西小姐，你觉得我有权代表我们大家说话吗？”他问这个问题的时候，眼睛一直没离开她的脸，还瞥了一眼坐在她身边的父亲。
foreignization: “我可以代表自己说话，”洛里先生回答说，“也可以代表丹尼先生，和露西小姐，还有——露西小姐，你不认为我可以代表我们所有人说话吗？”他明确地问了她这个问题，并带着凝视看着她的父亲。

“I speak for myself,” answered Mr. Lorry, “and for Mr. Darnay, and for Miss Lucie, and—Miss Lucie, do you not think I may speak for us all?” He asked her the question pointedly, and with a glance at her father.
“我可以代表我自己说话，”洛里先生回答道，“也可以代表丹尼先生和卢西小姐...”他转头问卢西小姐，“卢西小姐，你觉得我有权代表我们大家说话吗？”他问这个问题的时候，眼睛一直没离开她的脸，还瞥了一眼坐在她身边的父亲。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I speak for myself,” answered Mr. Lorry, “and for Mr. Darnay, and for Miss Lucie, and—Miss Lucie, do you not think I may speak for us all?” He asked her the question pointedly, and with a glance at her father.
“我可以代表自己说话，”洛里先生回答说，“也可以代表丹尼先生，和露西小姐，还有——露西小姐，你不认为我可以代表我们所有人说话吗？”他明确地问了她这个问题，并带着凝视看着她的父亲。

source: There was something in its touch that gave Lucie a check. She stopped in the act of putting the note in her bosom, and, with her hands yet at her neck, looked terrified at Madame Defarge. Madame Defarge met the lifted eyebrows and forehead with a cold, impassive stare.
domestication: 当卢西接过那张纸时，却发现自己手底下有一种莫名的感觉，让她突然顿住了。就在她准备把纸塞进胸口的瞬间，她僵住了，手还搭在脖子上，带着恐惧的神情看着德法尔热夫人。德法尔热夫人面对卢西扬起的眉毛和皱起的额头，却是一副冰冷、毫不动摇的表情，两人就这样目光相对。
foreignization: 它的触碰中有一些东西让露西犹豫了。她停止了将便条塞进胸口的动作，双手还放在脖子上，害怕地看着德法尔热夫人。德法尔热夫人以冷漠、面无表情的凝视迎接了露西扬起的眉毛和前额。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There was something in its touch that gave Lucie a check. She stopped in the act of putting the note in her bosom, and, with her hands yet at her neck, looked terrified at Madame Defarge. Madame Defarge met the lifted eyebrows and forehead with a cold, impassive stare.
当卢西接过那张纸时，却发现自己手底下有一种莫名的感觉，让她突然顿住了。就在她准备把纸塞进胸口的瞬间，她僵住了，手还搭在脖子上，带着恐惧的神情看着德法尔热夫人。德法尔热夫人面对卢西扬起的眉毛和皱起的额头，却是一副冰冷、毫不动摇的表情，两人就这样目光相对。

source: “They had conferred together until the last moment, when it was necessary for the French gentlemen to be landed in their boat.”
domestication: 他们一直商议到最后一刻，直到不得不让法国先生们乘船上岸。
foreignization: 他们一直在一起商议，直到最后时刻，当法国绅士们必须乘坐他们的小船下船的时候。

source: Within half an hour from the first starting, they were beyond the winking lamps, and the more than winking watchmen, and were out upon a lonely road. Another fisherman was picked up here—and that so silently, that if Young Jerry had been superstitious, he might have supposed the second follower of the gentle craft to have, all of a sudden, split himself into two.
domestication: 出了城门，没一会儿，他们就把那些闪烁的灯光和比灯光还忙碌的巡夜人抛在了后面，来到了荒凉的郊外公路上。这时又搭上了另一个渔民，动作非常隐秘，如果小杰瑞有迷信的话，他简直会以为这个第二个渔民突然从空气中冒出来了一样。
foreignization: 在出发后的半小时内，他们已经越过了闪烁的路灯和比路灯更加警觉的看守，来到了荒凉的道路上。在这里又捡起了另一个渔夫——并且如此安静地进行着，如果小杰瑞有迷信的话，他可能会认为第二个从事渔业的人突然一下子分裂成了两个。

source: Sydney had been working double tides that night, and the night before, and the night before that, and a good many nights in succession, making a grand clearance among Mr. Stryver’s papers before the setting in of the long vacation. The clearance was effected at last; the Stryver arrears were handsomely fetched up; everything was got rid of until November should come with its fogs atmospheric, and fogs legal, and bring grist to the mill again.
domestication: 那天夜里，悉尼已经连续工作了好几晚，前一晚、前前一晚，乃至更多的夜晚都如此。他正在为斯特莱弗先生处理积压的文件，以便在长假开始前完成大扫除。最后，清理工作终于完成了；斯特莱弗积压的文件也都处理完毕，一切都尘埃落定，直到十一月份带着迷雾和法律上的案件再次来临，给律师事务所带来新的业务。
foreignization: 悉尼那晚、前一晚、前两晚，连续许多个夜晚都在加班加点地工作，以便在长假开始之前处理完史特莱佛先生的文件。最后，他终于完成了清理工作；史特莱佛的积压事情也得以圆满解决；一切都处理完毕，直到十一月份来临，带着大气中的雾和法律上的诉讼，再次给磨坊带来工作量。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Sydney had been working double tides that night, and the night before, and the night before that, and a good many nights in succession, making a grand clearance among Mr. Stryver’s papers before the setting in of the long vacation. The clearance was effected at last; the Stryver arrears were handsomely fetched up; everything was got rid of until November should come with its fogs atmospheric, and fogs legal, and bring grist to the mill again.

source: “Mr. Carton,” she answered, after an agitated pause, “the secret is yours, not mine; and I promise to respect it.”
domestication: “卡顿先生，”她在一阵激动的沉默之后回答道，“这个秘密是你的，而不是我的，我保证会保守它。”可以这样说，她的话分量很重，意思是你交给我，我一定会替你保密的。
foreignization: “卡顿先生，”她在动乱的停顿后回答道，“这个秘密是属于你的，而不是我的；我承诺会尊重它。”

source: “A young lady’s walking shoe,” he muttered, without looking up. “It ought to have been finished long ago. Let it be.”
domestication: "这是个年轻姑娘的行走鞋，”他没抬头就自言自语道：“早该做好了，算了。”
foreignization: “一位年轻女士的行走鞋，”他低声嘟囔着，没有抬起头来，“本应该早就完成了，让它去吧。”

source: After grasping the Doctor’s hand, as he stood victorious and proud before him; after grasping the hand of Mr. Lorry, who came panting in breathless from his struggle against the waterspout of the Carmagnole; after kissing little Lucie, who was lifted up to clasp her arms round his neck; and after embracing the ever zealous and faithful Pross who lifted her; he took his wife in his arms, and carried her up to their rooms.
domestication: 他一把抓住了医生的手，昂首挺胸地站在医生面前，脸上写满了胜利和自豪。接着，他又紧握住劳瑞先生的手，劳瑞先生被卡马尼奥风暴的巨浪折腾得气喘吁吁，浑身湿透。然后，他亲吻了小露西，小露西被高高举起，搂着他的脖子。同时，他也拥抱了永远热情而忠诚的普罗斯，普罗斯是被人抬起来的。在完成了一系列感人的场景后，他终于把妻子抱在怀里，带着她回到他们的房间。
foreignization: 在紧握医生的手之后，他自豪而胜利地站在面前；在紧握来 自卡马尼奥水龙卷的奋斗中气喘吁吁、无力呼吸的洛里先生的手之后；在亲吻被抱起来用双臂抱住他脖子的小露西之后；以及在拥抱总是热心和忠诚的普罗斯——她把露西抱了起来——之后，他把妻子抱在怀里，带着她回到他们的房间。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

After grasping the Doctor’s hand, as he stood victorious and proud before him; after grasping the hand of Mr. Lorry, who came panting in breathless from his struggle against the waterspout of the Carmagnole; after kissing little Lucie, who was lifted up to clasp her arms round his neck; and after embracing the ever zealous and faithful Pross who lifted her; he took his wife in his arms, and carried her up to their rooms.
他一把抓住了医生的手，昂首挺胸地站在医生面前，脸上写满了胜利和自豪。接着，他又紧握住劳瑞先生的手，劳瑞先生被卡马尼奥风暴的巨浪折腾得气喘吁吁，浑身湿透。然后，他亲吻了小露西，小露西被高高举起，搂着他的脖子。同时，他也拥抱了永远热情而忠诚的普罗斯，普罗斯是被人抬起来的。在完成了一系列感人的场景后，他终于把妻子抱在怀里，带着她回到他们的房间。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

After grasping the Doctor’s hand, as he stood victorious and proud before him; after grasping the hand of Mr. Lorry, who came panting in breathless from his struggle against the waterspout of the Carmagnole; after kissing little Lucie, who was lifted up to clasp her arms round his neck; and after embracing the ever zealous and faithful Pross who lifted her; he took his wife in his arms, and carried her up to their rooms.
在紧握医生的手之后，他自豪而胜利地站在面前；在紧握来 自卡马尼奥水龙卷的奋斗中气喘吁吁、无力呼吸的洛里先生的手之后；在亲吻被抱起来用双臂抱住他脖子的小露西之后；以及在拥抱总是热心和忠诚的普罗斯——她把露西抱了起来——之后，他把妻子抱在怀里，带着她回到他们的房间。

source: Dinner-time, and still no Hundreds of people. In the arrangements of the little household, Miss Pross took charge of the lower regions, and always acquitted herself marvellously. Her dinners, of a very modest quality, were so well cooked and so well served, and so neat in their contrivances, half English and half French, that nothing could be better. Miss Pross’s friendship being of the thoroughly practical kind, she had ravaged Soho and the adjacent provinces, in search of impoverished French, who, tempted by shillings and half-crowns, would impart culinary mysteries to her. From these decayed sons and daughters of Gaul, she had acquired such wonderful arts, that the woman and girl who formed the staff of domestics regarded her as quite a Sorceress, or Cinderella’s Godmother: who would send out for a fowl, a rabbit, a vegetable or two from the garden, and change them into anything she pleased.
domestication: 吃饭的时候了，家里仍然没有客人。小家庭的安排中，普洛小姐负责管理下面的那些事务，而且她总是能出色地完成任务。她的饭菜虽然很简单，但做得很好，摆盘也很漂亮，融合了英法两国风格，真是无可挑剔。普洛小姐的友谊是非常实际的，她曾经在苏豪区和附近的地方搜寻贫困的法国人，只要给他们几先令或半克朗，他们就会教她烹饪技巧。从这些落魄的高卢儿女那里，她学到了神奇的烹饪技艺，以至于组成仆人队伍的妇女和姑娘们都把她当作仙女或者灰姑娘的仙女教母一样看待。她可以叫人去买一只鸡、一只兔子或者从花园里拿几种蔬菜，然后把它们变成任何她想要的东西。仆人们对她的厨艺简直是既佩服又害怕。
foreignization: 晚餐时间到了，还是没有看到成百上千的人。在这个小家庭的安排中，普洛丝小姐负责管理下层楼，她总是表现得非常出色。她的晚餐质量很谦逊，但做得很好，也服务得很好，摆设也很整洁，融合了英法风格，没有什么可挑剔的。普洛丝小姐的友谊是彻头彻尾的实际型，她曾搜遍索霍区和邻近省份，寻找那些贫穷的法国人，他们被先令和半克朗吸引，愿意向她传授烹饪秘诀。从这些衰落的高卢儿女那里，她获得了如此精湛的技艺，以至于由女性和少女组成的家政人员都把她看作真正的女巫，或灰姑娘的仙女：她会派人去买一只鸡、一只兔子，或从花园里拿几种蔬菜，然后把它们变成她想要的任何东西。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Dinner-time, and still no Hundreds of people. In the arrangements of the little household, Miss Pross took charge of the lower regions, and always acquitted herself marvellously. Her dinners, of a very modest quality, were so well cooked and so well served, and so neat in their contrivances, half English and half French, that nothing could be better. Miss Pross’s friendship being of the thoroughly practical kind, she had ravaged Soho and the adjacent provinces, in search of impoverished French, who, tempted by shillings and half-crowns, would impart culinary mysteries to her. From these decayed sons and daughters of Gaul, she had acquired such wonderful arts, that the woman and girl who formed the staff of domestics regarded her as quite a Sorceress, or Cinderella’s Godmother: who would send out for a fowl, a rabbit, a vegetable or two from the garden, and change them into anything she pleased.

吃饭的时候了，家里仍然没有客人。小家庭的安排中，普洛小姐负责管理下面的那些事务，而且她总是能出色地完成任务。她的饭菜虽然很简单，但做得很好，摆盘也很漂亮，融合了英法两国风格，真是无可挑剔。普洛小姐的友谊是非常实际的，她曾经在苏豪区和附近的地方搜寻贫困的法国人，只要给他们几先令或半克朗，他们就会教她烹饪技巧。从这些落魄的高卢儿女那里，她学到了神奇的烹饪技艺，以至于组成仆人队伍的妇女和姑娘们都把她当作仙女或者灰姑娘的仙女教母一样看待。她可以叫人去买一只鸡、一只兔子或者从花园里拿几种蔬菜，然后把它们变成任何她想要的东西。仆人们对她的厨艺简直是既佩服又害怕。

Her dinners, of a very modest quality, were so well cooked and so well served, and so neat in their contrivances, half English and half French, that nothing could be better. Miss Pross’s friendship being of the thoroughly practical kind, she had ravaged Soho and the adjacent provinces, in search of impoverished French, who, tempted by shillings and half-crowns, would impart culinary mysteries to her. From these decayed sons and daughters of Gaul, she had acquired such wonderful arts, that the woman and girl who formed the staff of domestics regarded her as quite a Sorceress, or Cinderella’s Godmother: who would send out for a fowl, a rabbit, a vegetable or two from the garden, and change them into anything she pleased.

晚餐时间到了，还是没有看到成百上千的人。在这个小家庭的安排中，普洛丝小姐负责管理下层楼，她总是表现得非常出色。她的晚餐质量很谦逊，但做得很好，也服务得很好，摆设也很整洁，融合了英法风格，没有什么可挑剔的。普洛丝小姐的友谊是彻头彻尾的实际型，她曾搜遍索霍区和邻近省份，寻找那些贫穷的法国人，他们被先令和半克朗吸引，愿意向她传授烹饪秘诀。从这些衰落的高卢儿女那里，她获得了如此精湛的技艺，以至于由女性和少女组成的家政人员都把她看作真正的女巫，或灰姑娘的仙女：她会派人去买一只鸡、一只兔子，或从花园里拿几种蔬菜，然后把它们变成她想要的任何东西。

source: The barrister was keen enough to divine that the banker would not have gone so far in his expression of opinion on any less solid ground than moral certainty. Unprepared as he was for the large pill he had to swallow, he got it down. “And now,” said Mr. Stryver, shaking his forensic forefinger at the Temple in general, when it was down, “my way out of this, is, to put you all in the wrong.”
domestication: 大律师很有心计，他看得出银行家的话并非空穴来风，一定有十足的把握才会那么肯定。他虽然没准备好接受这个事实，但还是硬着头皮吞下了这颗难堪的药丸。“现在，”史特莱佛先生说着，指着手指训斥寺庙的人们，当他把药丸吞下去后，“我想对付你们所有人的办法，就是证明你们都是错的。”
foreignization: 这位律师足够敏锐，能猜测出银行家不会在比道德确定更不牢固的基础上表达如此极端的意见。尽管他没有准备好吞下这么大的药丸，但他还是把它吞了下去。“现在，”斯特赖弗先生说着，他摇晃着他的法庭手指，对整个圣殿一般地说，当（药丸）下去之后，“我脱身的办法，就是让你们所有人都站到错误的一边。”

source: “That my father,” said Lucie, hurriedly taking the paper from her breast, but with her alarmed eyes on her questioner and not on it, “has much influence around him.”

source: “I was saying to madame, with whom I had the pleasure of chatting when you entered, that they tell me there is—and no wonder!—much sympathy and anger in Saint Antoine, touching the unhappy fate of poor Gaspard.”
domestication: “我刚才跟夫人聊天的时候，你进来了，我正在说，圣安托万的百姓听说了可怜的加斯帕德的不幸遭遇，真是有口皆碑，都在替他鸣不平，满腔义愤。”
foreignization: “我刚才跟夫人正在聊天，你进来时我正对她说，有人告诉我——这也难怪！——圣安托万的人们对可怜的加斯帕的不幸命运表示出了很多同情和愤怒。”

source: Of all these cries, and ten thousand incoherences, “The Prisoners!” was the cry most taken up by the sea that rushed in, as if there were an eternity of people, as well as of time and space. When the foremost billows rolled past, bearing the prison officers with them, and threatening them all with instant death if any secret nook remained undisclosed, Defarge laid his strong hand on the breast of one of these men—a man with a grey head, who had a lighted torch in his hand—separated him from the rest, and got him between himself and the wall.
domestication: 在那些哭喊声中，以及数不清的混乱之中，有一句喊声最被汹涌的海浪所传递，那就是“囚犯们”，仿佛有无穷无尽的人群和时空一般。当前面的巨浪滚滚而来，将狱官们卷在其中，威胁着他们，如果任何一个隐秘角落没有被揭露，就会立即面临死亡。在这时，德法尔热伸出他有力的双手，按住了其中一个人的胸口——这个人头发灰白，手里拿着一根点燃的火炬，德法尔热将他与其他人隔开，挡在自己和墙壁之间。
foreignization: 在所有这些呼喊和一万句不连贯的话中，“囚犯！”是海浪涌动时最响亮的呼喊，仿佛有一无穷无尽的人群，以及永恒的时间和空间。当最前面的波浪滚滚而来，带着监狱看守和他们一起奔涌，威胁如果还有任何秘密之处尚未被揭露，他们都会立即死亡的时候，德法尔热把他有力的双手放在其中一个人的胸口——这个人是一个头发灰白的老人，他手里拿着一根火炬——他将老人从人群中分开，夹在自己和墙壁之间。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Of all these cries, and ten thousand incoherences, “The Prisoners!” was the cry most taken up by the sea that rushed in, as if there were an eternity of people, as well as of time and space. When the foremost billows rolled past, bearing the prison officers with them, and threatening them all with instant death if any secret nook remained undisclosed, Defarge laid his strong hand on the breast of one of these men—a man with a grey head, who had a lighted torch in his hand—separated him from the rest, and got him between himself and the wall.

source: This touched Young Jerry on a tender place; who adjured his mother to perform her first duty, and, whatever else she did or neglected, above all things to lay especial stress on the discharge of that maternal function so affectingly and delicately indicated by his other parent.
domestication: 小杰里被这话触到了心底。他催促母亲务必要尽到做母亲的第一份责任，无论她在其他方面怎么样，先要把做母亲这件事放在首位，就像他那位做父親的说的，这是最需要用心去做好的事情。
foreignization: 这触动了小杰瑞敏感的地方；他恳求母亲履行她第一项职责，无论她做或忽略其他事情，尤其要强调他父亲那样感人和微妙地表明的那项母亲职责。

source: Encamped at a quarter before nine, in good time to touch his three-cornered hat to the oldest of men as they passed in to Tellson’s, Jerry took up his station on this windy March morning, with young Jerry standing by him, when not engaged in making forays through the Bar, to inflict bodily and mental injuries of an acute description on passing boys who were small enough for his amiable purpose. Father and son, extremely like each other, looking silently on at the morning traffic in Fleet-street, with their two heads as near to one another as the two eyes of each were, bore a considerable resemblance to a pair of monkeys. The resemblance was not lessened by the accidental circumstance, that the mature Jerry bit and spat out straw, while the twinkling eyes of the youthful Jerry were as restlessly watchful of him as of everything else in Fleet-street.
domestication: 九点不到，杰瑞就已经安顿好了营地，正好有时间向路过的老人敬礼，这些老人正在前往泰尔森公司的大门。这个三月份的早晨，风很大，杰瑞站了起来，他的儿子小杰瑞在他身边，除了偶尔冲进酒吧，去惹是生非，欺负那些个头够小的孩子，给他们造成身体和心理上的伤害。父亲和儿子长得非常像，他们默默地看着费勒特街上的早晨交通，两人的头挨得很近，就像每个人自己的两个眼睛一样。他们俩的样子很像一对猴子，长得非常相似。这种相似之处并没有因为成年杰瑞不小心吐出一口草而减少，小杰瑞的眼里闪着光，带着好奇的目光注视着他的父亲，就像他注视着费勒特街上其他的一切一样。
foreignization: 他们在九点前安营扎寨，很有时间地向Tellson's银行进来的最年长的男人们碰碰他的三角帽。杰瑞在这个三月份的风天早晨占据了他的岗位，他的儿子小杰瑞站在他旁边，当小杰瑞不在法庭上出没，去给足够小的路过男孩施加严重的身体和精神伤害时。父亲和儿子非常相像，他们默默地看着弗利特街的早晨交通，他们的头紧挨着彼此，就像他们每个人的两只眼睛一样。他们俩看起来非常像一对猴子。成熟的杰瑞咬着并吐出稻草，而充满光芒的年轻杰瑞的眼睛同样警觉地注视着他和弗利特街里的其他一切，这种偶然的情况并没有减少他们的相似之处。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Encamped at a quarter before nine, in good time to touch his three-cornered hat to the oldest of men as they passed in to Tellson’s, Jerry took up his station on this windy March morning, with young Jerry standing by him, when not engaged in making forays through the Bar, to inflict bodily and mental injuries of an acute description on passing boys who were small enough for his amiable purpose. Father and son, extremely like each other, looking silently on at the morning traffic in Fleet-street, with their two heads as near to one another as the two eyes of each were, bore a considerable resemblance to a pair of monkeys. The resemblance was not lessened by the accidental circumstance, that the mature Jerry bit and spat out straw, while the twinkling eyes of the youthful Jerry were as restlessly watchful of him as of everything else in Fleet-street.

source: The Marquis took a gentle little pinch of snuff, and shook his head; as elegantly despondent as he could becomingly be of a country still containing himself, that great means of regeneration.
domestication: 马基斯轻轻地拨了一撮鼻烟，然后摇了摇头，显出一副优雅的失望表情。他虽然对这个国家的前途感到绝望，但还是信心十足，因为他认为，只要自己还在，这个国家就还有救。
foreignization: 马奎斯取了一小撮鼻烟，摇了摇头；他优雅地垂头丧气，因为这个国家仍然拥有他这么伟大的复兴力量，所以他能表现出合适的失望。

“That’s a fair young lady to hand to a coach in the dark, Mr. Darnay!” he said, filling his new goblet.
“那是一个公平的年轻女士在黑暗中递给一辆马车，达纳利先生！”他说，填满了他的新酒杯。

source: “The prisoner was as open in his confidence with me—which arose out of my helpless situation—as he was kind, and good, and useful to my father. I hope,” bursting into tears, “I may not repay him by doing him harm to-day.”
domestication: 那囚徒对我倒是坦诚相待，就好像我和他一样无依无靠似的。他不仅对我好，还帮助了我父亲。我现在只希望自己今天不会伤害他，想到这儿，我就忍不住哭出声来。唉，谁让我今朝命途多舛呢，这下可真是“有心栽花花不活，无心插柳柳成荫”了。
foreignization: “囚犯对我也同样坦白——这种信任是因为我的无助处境产生的——就像他对我父亲一样仁慈、善良和有帮助一样。我希望，”突然哭了起来，“我不会今天通过伤害他来报答他。”

source: All this was seen in a moment, as the vision of a drowning man, or of any human creature at any very great pass, could see a world if it were there. They drew back from the window, and the Doctor looked for explanation in his friend’s ashy face.
domestication: 这一切都在瞬间展开，就像一个溺水的人或是任何在生死攸关时刻的人一样，能看到眼前的一切世界。他们从窗户边退了回来，医生看着他朋友惨白的脸，寻找着解释。就好像大限将至，人生百态尽收眼底。而这时，医生的目光落在了朋友苍白的脸上，期待着他开口说些什么。

source: The gaoler’s wife, being provided with no answer to the question, merely replied, “One must have patience, my dear!” Three turnkeys who entered responsive to a bell she rang, echoed the sentiment, and one added, “For the love of Liberty;” which sounded in that place like an inappropriate conclusion.
domestication: 典狱长的妻子没有办法回答这个问题，只能含糊地说：“哎，你就耐心点吧，好姑娘！”话音刚落，三名狱卒应召而入，他们也跟着附和说：“要有耐心。”其中一名狱卒又补充了一句“为了自由的缘故”，这种话在这里听起来有些格格不入，令人啼笑皆非。
foreignization: 狱卒的妻子没有得到这个问题的答案，仅仅回答道，“一必须有耐心，我的亲爱的！”三个狱卒响应她敲的铃声而进来，回应了她的感想，其中一个添加说，“为了自由的爱；”这听起来在那个地方像一个不恰当的结论。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: “It should be very beneficial to a man in your practice at the bar, to be ashamed of anything,” returned Sydney; “you ought to be much obliged to me.”
domestication: 像你这样当律师的，脸皮厚点儿有好处，能红个脸就不错了，我帮了你大忙呢！
foreignization: “对于像您这样的律师来说，能够为某事感到羞耻是非常有益的，”悉尼回答说，“您应该非常感谢我。”

source: When this interchange of Christian name was effected, Madame Defarge, picking her teeth with her toothpick, coughed another grain of cough, and raised her eyebrows by the breadth of another line.
domestication: 当他们交换了名字之后，德法尔热夫人用牙签咬着牙齿，又轻轻地咳嗽了一声，扬起眉毛来，眼神中闪烁着一丝意味深长的光芒。
foreignization: 当这次基督教名字的交换发生时，德法尔热夫人用她的牙签剔着牙齿，又咳出了一粒痰，眉毛又高了另一条线的宽度。

source: In the instinctive association of prisoners with shameful crime and disgrace, the new-comer recoiled from this company. But the crowning unreality of his long unreal ride, was, their all at once rising to receive him, with every refinement of manner known to the time, and with all the engaging graces and courtesies of life.
domestication: 在监狱里，犯人总是和可耻的罪行以及耻辱联系在一起，新来的囚犯对这种陪伴感到反感。但是，最让他觉得不真实的事情发生了：所有的囚犯突然站起来，用当时最高雅的礼仪来迎接他，他们脸上带着迷人的微笑和优雅的风度，彬彬有礼，像在上流社会的客厅里一样热情地欢迎他。这让他觉得自己仿佛是在做梦，完全无法将这些行为和他们的身份联系起来。
foreignization: 在囚犯与可耻罪行和羞辱的直觉联系中，新来的人从这种陪伴中退缩。但他长时间不真实的旅程中，最不真实的是，他们突然站起来迎接他，以当时所知道的一切礼仪，并且以生命中所有吸引人的优雅和礼貌。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In the instinctive association of prisoners with shameful crime and disgrace, the new-comer recoiled from this company. But the crowning unreality of his long unreal ride, was, their all at once rising to receive him, with every refinement of manner known to the time, and with all the engaging graces and courtesies of life.
在监狱里，犯人总是和可耻的罪行以及耻辱联系在一起，新来的囚犯对这种陪伴感到反感。但是，最让他觉得不真实的事情发生了：所有的囚犯突然站起来，用当时最高雅的礼仪来迎接他，他们脸上带着迷人的微笑和优雅的风度，彬彬有礼，像在上流社会的客厅里一样热情地欢迎他。这让他觉得自己仿佛是在做梦，完全无法将这些行为和他们的身份联系起来。

source: “A bad look, you say, my dear Darnay? Yes, but we don’t know what reason there is in it. People are so unreasonable! Some of us at Tellson’s are getting old, and we really can’t be troubled out of the ordinary course without due occasion.”
domestication: 你说这个样子不太好看吧，亲爱的丹雷？是啊，但是我们不知道其中有什么原因呢。人们真的是很难讲理！我们特尔森公司的一些人都快老了，实在懒得没事找事，除非有充分的理由，我们才会破例。
foreignization: “一副糟糕的样子，你这么说，亲爱的达尼？”是的，但是我们不知道这里面有何道理可循。人们如此不讲理！我们泰尔森公司的一些人正在变老，我们确实不能被打扰出于常规之外除非有充分的理由。

source: Not yet trusting the tones of her voice, she sat down on the bench beside him. He recoiled, but she laid her hand upon his arm. A strange thrill struck him when she did so, and visibly passed over his frame; he laid the knife down softly, as he sat staring at her.
domestication: 她还不太放心自己的声音会不会引起他的反感，就坐在他旁边的长椅上。他条件反射般地往后缩了缩，但她及时把手搭在了他的手臂上。奇怪的是，她这一举动让他感到了一种莫名的颤栗，这种感觉清晰地写在了他的脸上；他慢慢地把刀子放下，目不转睛地看着她。
foreignization: 她还不相信自己的声音，于是坐在他旁边的长椅上。他退缩了，但她把手放在他的手臂上。当她这么做的时候，一种奇怪的颤抖击中了他，并且清晰地传遍了他的身体；当他盯着她时，他轻轻地把刀放在了一边。

source: “By his tall figure,” said the mender of roads, softly, and with his finger at his nose. “When Monsieur the Marquis demands that evening, ‘Say, what is he like?’ I make response, ‘Tall as a spectre.’”
domestication: 那修路的人，低声说，“我当时就说他个子很高，”他边说边把手指放在鼻子上。“当马奎斯先生晚上问我，‘那人怎么样？’我回答说，‘他高得跟鬼一样！’”

source: “It’s enough for you,” retorted Mr. Cruncher, “to be the wife of a honest tradesman, and not to occupy your female mind with calculations when he took to his trade or when he didn’t. A honouring and obeying wife would let his trade alone altogether. Call yourself a religious woman? If you’re a religious woman, give me a irreligious one! You have no more nat’ral sense of duty than the bed of this here Thames river has of a pile, and similarly it must be knocked into you.”
domestication: 你就应该满足做个诚实商人的妻子，别把心思放在那些没用的计算上，比如你老公什么时候开始干活儿，什么时候停下来。一个贤惠的妻子根本就不该管这些事情。你自称是有信仰的女人？我倒宁愿找个无信仰的！你对责任的理解比泰晤士河里的河床知道什么叫码头还少，非得敲打着你才行！
foreignization: "对于你来说已经足够了，”克兰切先生反驳道，“你是正直商人的妻子，不应该用你的女性思维去思考他什么时候开始或者停止干他的行业。一个尊敬和服从的妻子会完全不去管他的行业。你自称为一个有宗教信仰的女人吗？如果你是一个有宗教信仰的女人，那么给我一个没有宗教信仰的女人吧！你没有任何天生的职责感，就像这条泰晤士河的河床对码头没有任何感觉一样，同样地，这种观念必须被打入你的脑海中。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“It’s enough for you,” retorted Mr. Cruncher, “to be the wife of a honest tradesman, and not to occupy your female mind with calculations when he took to his trade or when he didn’t. A honouring and obeying wife would let his trade alone altogether. Call yourself a religious woman? If you’re a religious woman, give me a irreligious one! You have no more nat’ral sense of duty than the bed of this here Thames river has of a pile, and similarly it must be knocked into you.”
你就应该满足做个诚实商人的妻子，别把心思放在那些没用的计算上，比如你老公什么时候开始干活儿，什么时候停下来。一个贤惠的妻子根本就不该管这些事情。你自称是有信仰的女人？我倒宁愿找个无信仰的！你对责任的理解比泰晤士河里的河床知道什么叫码头还少，非得敲打着你才行！

“It’s enough for you,” retorted Mr. Cruncher, “to be the wife of a honest tradesman, and not to occupy your female mind with calculations when he took to his trade or when he didn’t. A honouring and obeying wife would let his trade alone altogether. Call yourself a religious woman? If you’re a religious woman, give me a irreligious one! You have no more nat’ral sense of duty than the bed of this here Thames river has of a pile, and similarly it must be knocked into you.”
"对于你来说已经足够了，”克兰切先生反驳道，“你是正直商人的妻子，不应该用你的女性思维去思考他什么时候开始或者停止干他的行业。一个尊敬和服从的妻子会完全不去管他的行业。你自称为一个有宗教信仰的女人吗？如果你是一个有宗教信仰的女人，那么给我一个没有宗教信仰的女人吧！你没有任何天生的职责感，就像这条泰晤士河的河床对码头没有任何感觉一样，同样地，这种观念必须被打入你的脑海中。”

source: “And she showed me her children,” said the Doctor of Beauvais, “and they had heard of me, and had been taught to pity me. When they passed a prison of the State, they kept far from its frowning walls, and looked up at its bars, and spoke in whispers. She could never deliver me; I imagined that she always brought me back after showing me such things. But then, blessed with the relief of tears, I fell upon my knees, and blessed her.”
domestication: 博韦医生说：“她给我看了她的孩子，他们知道我的事，跟他们说我很可怜。他们路过国牢的时候，都会远远地避开那座门卫森严的大墙，抬头看着高墙上铁栏，低声说话。她永远也无法让我脱离苦海，我想她每次给我展示这样的事情，就是在提醒我现实。但是，每当我看到这一切时，我的心里总是感到一阵宽慰，我就会泪流满面，跪下身来，感谢她。”
foreignization: “她给我看了她的孩子，”博韦医生说，“他们听说过我，还被教导要可怜我。当他们路过国家监狱时，他们远离它那令人生畏的墙壁，抬头看着铁栏，并低声说话。她永远也不能让我获得自由；我想象她每次给我看了这样的事情之后，就会把我带回去。但是，感受到眼泪带来的宽慰后，我跪倒在地，谢福于她。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“And she showed me her children,” said the Doctor of Beauvais, “and they had heard of me, and had been taught to pity me. When they passed a prison of the State, they kept far from its frowning walls, and looked up at its bars, and spoke in whispers. She could never deliver me; I imagined that she always brought me back after showing me such things. But then, blessed with the relief of tears, I fell upon my knees, and blessed her.”
博韦医生说：“她给我看了她的孩子，他们知道我的事，跟他们说我很可怜。他们路过国牢的时候，都会远远地避开那座门卫森严的大墙，抬头看着高墙上铁栏，低声说话。她永远也无法让我脱离苦海，我想她每次给我展示这样的事情，就是在提醒我现实。但是，每当我看到这一切时，我的心里总是感到一阵宽慰，我就会泪流满面，跪下身来，感谢她。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“And she showed me her children,” said the Doctor of Beauvais, “and they had heard of me, and had been taught to pity me. When they passed a prison of the State, they kept far from its frowning walls, and looked up at its bars, and spoke in whispers. She could never deliver me; I imagined that she always brought me back after showing me such things. But then, blessed with the relief of tears, I fell upon my knees, and blessed her.”
“她给我看了她的孩子，”博韦医生说，“他们听说过我，还被教导要可怜我。当他们路过国家监狱时，他们远离它那令人生畏的墙壁，抬头看着铁栏，并低声说话。她永远也不能让我获得自由；我想象她每次给我看了这样的事情之后，就会把我带回去。但是，感受到眼泪带来的宽慰后，我跪倒在地，谢福于她。”

source: “Well then, I’ll tell you,” said Stryver, coming slowly into a sitting posture. “Sydney, I rather despair of making myself intelligible to you, because you are such an insensible dog.”
domestication: "那好吧，我就告诉你，”斯特赖弗慢慢地坐直了身子，说：“悉尼，我觉得我很难让你明白我的意思，因为你就是这么个没心没肺的人。”
foreignization: “好吧，我就告诉你，” 斯特赖佛说着，慢慢地坐直了身子。“悉尼，我相当怀疑自己是否能让你明白我的意思，因为你是一个如此迟钝的家伙。”

source: Soon afterwards, the bell at the great gate sounded, and he thought, “They have come back!” and sat listening. But, there was no loud irruption into the courtyard, as he had expected, and he heard the gate clash again, and all was quiet.
domestication: 没过多久，大门上的铃铛响了起来，他想：“他们回来了！”于是静静地坐着倾听。然而，并没有像他预料的那样，人群突然闯入庭院，他只听到大门再次砰然关上，然后一切归于寂静。
foreignization: 不久，大的门口的铃声响了，他想：“他们回来了！”然后坐着听。但是，并没有他预料到的那种闯入庭院的喧嚣，他听到了大门再次关上，一切又变得安静。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Soon afterwards, the bell at the great gate sounded, and he thought, “They have come back!” and sat listening. But, there was no loud irruption into the courtyard, as he had expected, and he heard the gate clash again, and all was quiet.
没过多久，大门上的铃铛响了起来，他想：“他们回来了！”于是静静地坐着倾听。然而，并没有像他预料的那样，人群突然闯入庭院，他只听到大门再次砰然关上，然后一切归于寂静。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Soon afterwards, the bell at the great gate sounded, and he thought, “They have come back!” and sat listening. But, there was no loud irruption into the courtyard, as he had expected, and he heard the gate clash again, and all was quiet.
不久，大的门口的铃声响了，他想：“他们回来了！”然后坐着听。但是，并没有他预料到的那种闯入庭院的喧嚣，他听到了大门再次关上，一切又变得安静。

source: Mr. Stryver shouldered his way through the law, like some great engine forcing itself through turbid water, and dragged his useful friend in his wake, like a boat towed astern. As the boat so favoured is usually in a rough plight, and mostly under water, so, Sydney had a swamped life of it. But, easy and strong custom, unhappily so much easier and stronger in him than any stimulating sense of desert or disgrace, made it the life he was to lead; and he no more thought of emerging from his state of lion’s jackal, than any real jackal may be supposed to think of rising to be a lion. Stryver was rich; had married a florid widow with property and three boys, who had nothing particularly shining about them but the straight hair of their dumpling heads.
domestication: 史特莱弗先生就像一台巨大的引擎，强行在浑浊的水中突进，他的律师事务所也就像一艘船，被他拖在后面。这种被拖在后面的船，通常都很难看，多半是泡在水里。就像这样，赛德尼的生活也很坎坷。但是，习惯的力量实在太强大了，特别是在他身上，它比起对名声和荣誉的渴望来，简直强得多。所以，他就这样过着“狮子的跟班”的日子，从来没想过要改变这种状况。就像真正的豺狼不可能变成雄狮一样。 史特莱弗有钱，娶了一个丰满的寡妇，她带来了财产，还有三个孩子，这三个孩子长得倒是一模一样，头发都直着的，就像馄饨的样子。
foreignization: 斯特赖佛先生像一台巨大的机器一样挤过法律世界，就像一台机器强行推进浑浊的水中，并且他也将他的有用朋友拖在他的后面，就像一艘船被拖在后面。 就像这样的船通常处于困境中，通常在水下一样，悉尼过着艰难的生活。但是，他那轻松而强烈的习惯，遗憾的是比他内心的任何美德或耻辱感更容易和更强烈，使他过上了这种生活。他就像狮子的杂种一样，没有想过改变自己的处境，就像真正的杂种不可能变成狮子一样。 斯特赖佛很富有；他娶了一位丰满的寡妇，她带来了财产和三个儿子，他们的头发是直的，但除了他们肉团般的头上的直发之外，他们没有什么特别突出的地方。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Stryver shouldered his way through the law, like some great engine forcing itself through turbid water, and dragged his useful friend in his wake, like a boat towed astern. As the boat so favoured is usually in a rough plight, and mostly under water, so, Sydney had a swamped life of it. But, easy and strong custom, unhappily so much easier and stronger in him than any stimulating sense of desert or disgrace, made it the life he was to lead; and he no more thought of emerging from his state of lion’s jackal, than any real jackal may be supposed to think of rising to be a lion. Stryver was rich; had married a florid widow with property and three boys, who had nothing particularly shining about them but the straight hair of their dumpling heads.

source: “Quick to see what happened! If a girl, doll or no doll, swoons within a yard or two of a man’s nose, he can see it without a perspective-glass. I pledge you, but I deny the beauty. And now I’ll have no more drink; I’ll get to bed.”
domestication: 这家伙还挺机敏的，能马上看出发生了什么事。如果哪个女孩，在离男人鼻子一两步远的地方晕倒了，不需要望远镜他也能看到。算我输，我不再否认那女孩的美貌。现在我不喝了，我该睡觉了。
foreignization: "真快就看到了发生了什么！如果一个女孩，不管有没有娃娃，在离男人鼻子一两码的地方晕倒，他都可以在没有望远镜的情况下看到它。我保证，但是我否认美丽。而现在我不会再喝了；我会上床睡觉。"

source: At last, the top of the staircase was gained, and they stopped for the third time. There was yet an upper staircase, of a steeper inclination and of contracted dimensions, to be ascended, before the garret story was reached. The keeper of the wine-shop, always going a little in advance, and always going on the side which Mr. Lorry took, as though he dreaded to be asked any question by the young lady, turned himself about here, and, carefully feeling in the pockets of the coat he carried over his shoulder, took out a key.
domestication: 终于，他们爬上了楼梯的顶端，第三次停了下来。前面还有一段更加陡峭、狭窄的楼梯需要攀登，才能到达顶楼。酒馆老板一直在前面带路，而且总是走在罗瑞先生同一边，好像害怕被年轻的女士问及什么问题。这时，他转过身来，小心地在搭在肩膀上的外套口袋里摸索，掏出了一把钥匙。
foreignization: 最后，他们终于登上了楼梯的顶部，并且第三次停了下来。在到达顶层阁楼之前，还有一段楼梯需要攀爬，这段楼梯的坡度更陡，尺寸也更小。酒店的看守者总是走在前面，总是走在路洛先生那一边，就好像他害怕被年轻的女士问及任何问题似的，他在这里转过身来，小心地在他扛在肩上的外套口袋里摸索，拿出了一个钥匙。

At last, the top of the staircase was gained, and they stopped for the third time. There was yet an upper staircase, of a steeper inclination and of contracted dimensions, to be ascended, before the garret story was reached. The keeper of the wine-shop, always going a little in advance, and always going on the side which Mr. Lorry took, as though he dreaded to be asked any question by the young lady, turned himself about here, and, carefully feeling in the pockets of the coat he carried over his shoulder, took out a key.

Translate the following text to Chinese.

At last, the top of the staircase was gained, and they stopped for the third time. There was yet an upper staircase, of a steeper inclination and of contracted dimensions, to be ascended, before the garret story was reached. The keeper of the wine-shop, always going a little in advance, and always going on the side which Mr. Lorry took, as though he dreaded to be asked any question by the young lady, turned himself about here, and, carefully feeling in the pockets of the coat he carried over his shoulder, took out a key.
最后，他们终于登上了楼梯的顶部，并且第三次停了下来。在到达顶层阁楼之前，还有一段楼梯需要攀爬，这段楼梯的坡度更陡，尺寸也更小。酒店的看守者总是走在前面，总是走在路洛先生那一边，就好像他害怕被年轻的女士问及任何问题似的，他在这里转过身来，小心地在他扛在肩上的外套口袋里摸索，拿出了一个钥匙。

source: “See!” said the Doctor of Beauvais, raising his hand towards the moon. “I have looked at her from my prison-window, when I could not bear her light. I have looked at her when it has been such torture to me to think of her shining upon what I had lost, that I have beaten my head against my prison-walls. I have looked at her, in a state so dull and lethargic, that I have thought of nothing but the number of horizontal lines I could draw across her at the full, and the number of perpendicular lines with which I could intersect them.” He added in his inward and pondering manner, as he looked at the moon, “It was twenty either way, I remember, and the twentieth was difficult to squeeze in.”
domestication: "看啊！”博韦医生举起手指向月亮，说，“我曾从监狱窗口凝望过她，当时她的光芒让我无法承受。我也曾在感到极度痛苦时看着她，想到她在我失去的一切上投下光辉，让我忍不住用头撞击监狱的墙壁。我还在一种昏昏欲睡的状态下看着她，只想着在满月的圆面上可以画出多少条横线，以及可以用多少条竖线交叉横线。” 他陷入沉思，内心默默地补充道，“无论是横线还是竖线，每种都有二十条，我记得很清楚，而那第 двадцать 条线特别难以勾勒出来。” 博韦医生说话时，眼睛仍然凝视着月亮，他的思绪似乎又飘回了过去的岁月。
foreignization: "看！”博韦医生举起手指向月亮说，“我曾从我的监狱窗口注视过她，当时她的光芒让我无法忍受。我曾在她照耀的时候注视过她，那时候一想到她正照在我失去的东西上，我就感到极度痛苦，以至于我把头撞在了监狱墙上。我曾在一种如此迟钝和嗜睡的状态下注视过她，以至于我除了思考我可以在满月上画出多少条横线，以及我可以用多少条垂直线交叉它们之外，什么也没想。”他以内省和沉思的方式补充说，当他看着月亮时，“无论哪种方式都是二十，我记得，第二十条很难挤进去。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“See!” said the Doctor of Beauvais, raising his hand towards the moon. “I have looked at her from my prison-window, when I could not bear her light. I have looked at her when it has been such torture to me to think of her shining upon what I had lost, that I have beaten my head against my prison-walls. I have looked at her, in a state so dull and lethargic, that I have thought of nothing but the number of horizontal lines I could draw across her at the full, and the number of perpendicular lines with which I could intersect them.” He added in his inward and pondering manner, as he looked at the moon, “It was twenty either way, I remember, and the twentieth was difficult to squeeze in.”

"看啊！”博韦医生举起手指向月亮，说，“我曾从监狱窗口凝望过她，当时她的光芒让我无法承受。我也曾在感到极度痛苦时看着她，想到她在我失去的一切上投下光辉，让我忍不住用头撞击监狱的墙壁。我还在一种昏昏欲睡的状态下看着她，只想着在满月的圆面上可以画出多少条横线，以及可以用多少条竖线交叉横线。” 他陷入沉思，内心默默地补充道，“无论是横线还是竖线，每种都有二十条，我记得很清楚，而那第 двадцать 条线特别难以勾勒出来。” 博韦医生说话时，眼睛仍然凝视着月亮，他的思绪似乎又飘回了过去的岁月。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“See!” said the Doctor of Beauvais, raising his hand towards the moon. “I have looked at her from my prison-window, when I could not bear her light. I have looked at her when it has been such torture to me to think of her shining upon what I had lost, that I have beaten my head against my prison-walls. I have looked at her, in a state so dull and lethargic, that I have thought of nothing but the number of horizontal lines I could draw across her at the full, and the number of perpendicular lines with which I could intersect them.” He added in his inward and pondering manner, as he looked at the moon, “It was twenty either way, I remember, and the twentieth was difficult to squeeze in.”

"看！”博韦医生举起手指向月亮说，“我曾从我的监狱窗口注视过她，当时她的光芒让我无法忍受。我曾在她照耀的时候注视过她，那时候一想到她正照在我失去的东西上，我就感到极度痛苦，以至于我把头撞在了监狱墙上。我曾在一种如此迟钝和嗜睡的状态下注视过她，以至于我除了思考我可以在满月上画出多少条横线，以及我可以用多少条垂直线交叉它们之外，什么也没想。”他以内省和沉思的方式补充说，当他看着月亮时，“无论哪种方式都是二十，我记得，第二十条很难挤进去。”

source: “The old Sydney Carton of old Shrewsbury School,” said Stryver, nodding his head over him as he reviewed him in the present and the past, “the old seesaw Sydney. Up one minute and down the next; now in spirits and now in despondency!”
domestication: "看那个老同学，肖士伯利学校的老朋友，” 斯崔佛边说边点头，带着怀旧的眼神看着他，“那个不定行止的赛德尼·卡顿，忽高兴忽消沉的家伙！有时高涨，有时低落，前一刻还精神抖擞，转眼间就垂头丧气了。”
foreignization: “老雪伍士比学校的老同学——老雪伍，”司特莱佛说着，点头表示赞同，当他现在和过去回想起这个人时，“老式跷跷板似的雪伍：一分钟高兴，一分钟低落；有时情绪高涨，有时又陷入绝望！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“The old Sydney Carton of old Shrewsbury School,” said Stryver, nodding his head over him as he reviewed him in the present and the past, “the old seesaw Sydney. Up one minute and down the next; now in spirits and now in despondency!”
"看那个老同学，肖士伯利学校的老朋友，” 斯崔佛边说边点头，带着怀旧的眼神看着他，“那个不定行止的赛德尼·卡顿，忽高兴忽消沉的家伙！有时高涨，有时低落，前一刻还精神抖擞，转眼间就垂头丧气了。”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

“The old Sydney Carton of old Shrewsbury School,” said Stryver, nodding his head over him as he reviewed him in the present and the past, “the old seesaw Sydney. Up one minute and down the next; now in spirits and now in despondency!”
“老雪伍士比学校的老同学——老雪伍，”司特莱佛说着，点头表示赞同，当他现在和过去回想起这个人时，“老式跷跷板似的雪伍：一分钟高兴，一分钟低落；有时情绪高涨，有时又陷入绝望！”

Heralded by a courier in advance, and by the cracking of his postilions’ whips, which twined snake-like about their heads in the evening air, as if he came attended by the Furies, Monsieur the Marquis drew up in his travelling carriage at the posting-house gate. 先是由一名信使提前宣告他的到来，接着便是他的驿站马车夫挥舞的鞭子在傍晚的空气中像蛇一样缠绕在他们头上，这就好像他是由复仇女神陪同而来的。马奎斯先生驾着他的旅行马车抵达了驿站门口。它紧靠着喷泉，农民们停下手中的活儿来看他。一时间，他也注视着他们，并且在无意中，看到了法国人的脸和身形在苦难中缓慢而坚定的磨损，这将会使得法国人的瘦弱成为一种英国人的迷信，它将会超过事实的真相，流传近百年。

source: In England, there was scarcely an amount of order and protection to justify much national boasting. Daring burglaries by armed men, and highway robberies, took place in the capital itself every night; families were publicly cautioned not to go out of town without removing their furniture to upholsterers’ warehouses for security; the highwayman in the dark was a City tradesman in the light, and, being recognised and challenged by his fellow-tradesman whom he stopped in his character of “the Captain,” gallantly shot him through the head and rode away; the mail was waylaid by seven robbers, and the guard shot three dead, and then got shot dead himself by the other four, “in consequence of the failure of his ammunition:” after which the mail was robbed in peace; that magnificent potentate, the Lord Mayor of London, was made to stand and deliver on Turnham Green, by one highwayman, who despoiled the illustrious creature in sight of all his retinue; prisoners in London gaols fought battles with their turnkeys, and the majesty of the law fired blunderbusses in among them, loaded with rounds of shot and ball; thieves snipped off diamond crosses from the necks of noble lords at Court drawing-rooms; musketeers went into St. Giles’s, to search for contraband goods, and the mob fired on the musketeers, and the musketeers fired on the mob, and nobody thought any of these occurrences much out of the common way. In the midst of them, the hangman, ever busy and ever worse than useless, was in constant requisition; now, stringing up long rows of miscellaneous criminals; now, hanging a housebreaker on Saturday who had been taken on Tuesday; now, burning people in the hand at Newgate by the dozen, and now burning pamphlets at the door of Westminster Hall; to-day, taking the life of an atrocious murderer, and to-morrow of a wretched pilferer who had robbed a farmer’s boy of sixpence.
domestication: 英格兰简直没什么值得骄傲的，治安混乱，老百姓根本不敢出门。每个夜晚，伦敦城里都有大胆的武装抢劫案发生，人们被明确告诫不要冒然外出，否则就得把家具搬到仓库里保管；白天是做生意，晚上就变成了强盗，一旦被熟人认出，就会被他们当场击毙。邮车也常常被拦劫，警卫开枪打死三个歹徒，但自己也会被其余的歹徒杀死。连伦敦市长在特恩汉姆绿地也被一个强盗当场抢劫，连同他的随从人员都被劫持。监狱里的犯人甚至与看守发生冲突，执法人员不得不使用霰弹枪进行镇压。就连王宫的贵族们也不能幸免，强盗竟然敢在宫廷舞厅里偷窃贵重物品。搜查走私品的士兵在圣吉尔斯区遭到了暴徒的袭击，双方互相开火，没人觉得这些事情有多么奇怪。在这片混乱中，刽子手总是忙个不停，但却收效甚微，有时会同时绞死一批杂乱无章的罪犯，有时会在星期六就处决在星期二刚抓到的窃贼，有时会在纽盖特监狱里烧伤一批人的手，有时会在威斯敏斯特大厅门口烧毁小册子。今天，他们可能会处决一个凶手，明天可能又会处决一个只偷了农夫孩子六便士的小偷。
foreignization: 在英格兰，几乎没有足够的秩序和保护来证明值得夸耀。白天当城里的商人，晚上却成为抢劫者，甚至在光天化日之下公然行凶，骑马逃逸；邮件被七名强盗拦截，卫兵开枪打死三人，自己也被剩下的四个强盗枪杀，原因是子弹用完了，之后他们顺利地抢走了邮件；伦敦市长这位高贵的人物，在特恩汉绿地被一名强盗拦住勒索财物，当着他的侍从们的面，被剥夺了财物；伦敦监狱的囚犯与看守发生冲突，执法者向囚犯开火，子弹中有霰弹和子弹；小偷在宫廷舞厅里从贵族领主的脖子上割下了镶满钻石的十字架；步枪手进入圣吉尔斯地区搜查违禁品，暴徒向步枪手开火，步枪手向暴徒还击，没人认为这些事情有什么异常。在这混乱之中，刽子手忙得不可开交，总是很忙碌但从不有用；有时他会将一排杂乱无章的罪犯吊死，有时他会在星期六绞死星期二被抓到的窃贼；有时他会在新门监狱门口用烙铁烧伤十几个人，有时他会在威斯敏斯特大厅门口焚烧小册子；今天，他夺取一个残忍杀人犯的生命，明天，他又夺取一个只偷了农夫孩子六便士的可怜小偷的生命。

In England, there was scarcely an amount of order and protection to justify much national boasting. Daring burglaries by armed men, and highway robberies, took place in the capital itself every night; families were publicly cautioned not to go out of town without removing their furniture to upholsterers’ warehouses for security; the highwayman in the dark was a City tradesman in the light, and, being recognised and challenged by his fellow-tradesman whom he stopped in his character of “the Captain,” gallantly shot him through the head and rode away; the mail was waylaid by seven robbers, and the guard shot three dead, and then got shot dead himself by the other four, “in consequence of the failure of his ammunition:” after which the mail was robbed in peace; that magnificent potentate, the Lord Mayor of London, was made to stand and deliver on Turnham Green, by one highwayman, who despoiled the illustrious creature in sight of all his retinue; prisoners in London gaols fought battles with their turnkeys, and the majesty of the law fired blunderbusses in among them, loaded with rounds of shot and ball; thieves snipped off diamond crosses from the necks of noble lords at Court drawing-rooms; musketeers went into St. Giles’s, to search for contraband goods, and the mob fired on the musketeers, and the musketeers fired on the mob, and nobody thought any of these occurrences much out of the common way. In the midst of them, the hangman, ever busy and ever worse than useless, was in constant requisition; now, stringing up long rows of miscellaneous criminals; now, hanging a housebreaker on Saturday who had been taken on Tuesday; now, burning people in the hand at Newgate by the dozen, and now burning pamphlets at the door of Westminster Hall; to-day, taking the life of an atrocious murderer, and to-morrow of a wretched pilferer who had robbed a farmer’s boy of sixpence.

source: As Mr. Lorry received these confidences, and as he watched the face of his friend now sixty-two years of age, a misgiving arose within him that such dread experiences would revive the old danger.
domestication: 洛瑞先生听了这些谈话，并看着他这位六十二岁的朋友的脸时，心里开始产生了一种不安的感觉，觉得这样的可怕经历可能会使老问题再次出现。
foreignization: 当洛里先生接受这些秘密的时候， 当他注视着他这位六十二岁的朋友的脸的时候， 一种疑虑在他心中产生：这样的可怕经历将会使旧有的危险重新出现。

source: “No, Jerry, no!” said the messenger, harping on one theme as he rode. “It wouldn’t do for you, Jerry. Jerry, you honest tradesman, it wouldn’t suit your line of business! Recalled—! Bust me if I don’t think he’d been a drinking!”
domestication: “不行，杰瑞，不行！”信使一边骑马一边唠叨着同一件事，“这对你来说不合适，杰瑞。你这个诚实的商人，这事儿不适合你的生意！被召回？我敢说他肯定是喝醉了！”
foreignization: “不，杰里，不！”信使骑着马一边说，一边在一个主题上反复强调。“这不适合你，杰里。杰里，你这个诚实的商人，这与你的生意风格不符！被召回——！我敢发誓，我觉得他一定是喝醉了！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“No, Jerry, no!” said the messenger, harping on one theme as he rode. “It wouldn’t do for you, Jerry. Jerry, you honest tradesman, it wouldn’t suit your line of business! Recalled—! Bust me if I don’t think he’d been a drinking!”
“不行，杰瑞，不行！”信使一边骑马一边唠叨着同一件事，“这对你来说不合适，杰瑞。你这个诚实的商人，这事儿不适合你的生意！被召回？我敢说他肯定是喝醉了！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“No, Jerry, no!” said the messenger, harping on one theme as he rode. “It wouldn’t do for you, Jerry. Jerry, you honest tradesman, it wouldn’t suit your line of business! Recalled—! Bust me if I don’t think he’d been a drinking!”
“不，杰里，不！”信使骑着马一边说，一边在一个主题上反复强调。“这不适合你，杰里。杰里，你这个诚实的商人，这与你的生意风格不符！被召回——！我敢发誓，我觉得他一定是喝醉了！”

source: Here again: Mr. Lorry’s inquiries into Miss Pross’s personal history had established the fact that her brother Solomon was a heartless scoundrel who had stripped her of everything she possessed, as a stake to speculate with, and had abandoned her in her poverty for evermore, with no touch of compunction. Miss Pross’s fidelity of belief in Solomon (deducting a mere trifle for this slight mistake) was quite a serious matter with Mr. Lorry, and had its weight in his good opinion of her.
domestication: 又一件事：洛里先生对普罗斯小姐的私生活进行了调查，证实了她哥哥所罗门是个无情的坏蛋，把她的一切都拿去做投机生意的本钱，之后就把她抛弃了，让她永远陷在贫穷中，连一丝愧疚的感觉都没有。普罗斯小姐对她哥哥那么大的错误竟然依然坚信不疑，这在洛里先生眼里是很严肃的一件事，对他的好感也有一定的分量。
foreignization: 又一次：洛瑞先生对普罗斯小姐个人历史的调查已经证实了这样一个事实：她的哥哥所罗门是一个无情的无赖，他夺走了她所有的一切，把它们当作投机的赌注，并且永远地抛弃了她，让她在贫穷中度日，却没有丝毫愧疚之情。普罗斯小姐对所罗门的坚定信念（除去她犯下的微小错误）对洛瑞先生来说是一个非常严肃的事情，也在他对她的好感中占有一定的份量。

Here again: Mr. Lorry’s inquiries into Miss Pross’s personal history had established the fact that her brother Solomon was a heartless scoundrel who had stripped her of everything she possessed, as a stake to speculate with, and had abandoned her in her poverty for evermore, with no touch of compunction. Miss Pross’s fidelity of belief in Solomon (deducting a mere trifle for this slight mistake) was quite a serious matter with Mr. Lorry, and had its weight in his good opinion of her.

Miss Pross’s fidelity of belief in Solomon (deducting a mere trifle for this slight mistake) was quite a serious matter with Mr. Lorry, and had its weight in his good opinion of her.
又一次：洛瑞先生对普罗斯小姐个人历史的调查已经证实了这样一个事实：她的哥哥所罗门是一个无情的无赖，他夺走了她所有的一切，把它们当作投机的赌注，并且永远地抛弃了她，让她在贫穷中度日，却没有丝毫愧疚之情。普罗斯小姐对所罗门的坚定信念（除去她犯下的微小错误）对洛瑞先生来说是一个非常严肃的事情，也在他对她的好感中占有一定的份量。

source: The shadow of a large high-roofed house, and of many over-hanging trees, was upon Monsieur the Marquis by that time; and the shadow was exchanged for the light of a flambeau, as his carriage stopped, and the great door of his chateau was opened to him.
domestication: 那时，一座高大屋顶的房子和许多垂下的树木已经投下了长长的影子笼罩在马基斯先生身上。不一会儿，这影子就被一根火炬的光芒取代了，因为他的马车停了下来，他的城堡的大门为他敞开。进门的瞬间，火炬的光芒闪耀出来，照亮了一切。
foreignization: 当时，一栋高屋顶的大房子和许多悬挂的树木的影子笼罩在马基斯先生身上；当他的马车停下来，他的城堡的大门为他敞开时，这个影子被火炬的光芒所取代。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The shadow of a large high-roofed house, and of many over-hanging trees, was upon Monsieur the Marquis by that time; and the shadow was exchanged for the light of a flambeau, as his carriage stopped, and the great door of his chateau was opened to him.
那时，一座高大屋顶的房子和许多垂下的树木已经投下了长长的影子笼罩在马基斯先生身上。不一会儿，这影子就被一根火炬的光芒取代了，因为他的马车停了下来，他的城堡的大门为他敞开。进门的瞬间，火炬的光芒闪耀出来，照亮了一切。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The shadow of a large high-roofed house, and of many over-hanging trees, was upon Monsieur the Marquis by that time; and the shadow was exchanged for the light of a flambeau, as his carriage stopped, and the great door of his chateau was opened to him.
当时，一栋高屋顶的大房子和许多悬挂的树木的影子笼罩在马基斯先生身上；当他的马车停下来，他的城堡的大门为他敞开时，这个影子被火炬的光芒所取代。

source: More months, to the number of twelve, had come and gone, and Mr. Charles Darnay was established in England as a higher teacher of the French language who was conversant with French literature. In this age, he would have been a Professor; in that age, he was a Tutor. He read with young men who could find any leisure and interest for the study of a living tongue spoken all over the world, and he cultivated a taste for its stores of knowledge and fancy. He could write of them, besides, in sound English, and render them into sound English. Such masters were not at that time easily found; Princes that had been, and Kings that were to be, were not yet of the Teacher class, and no ruined nobility had dropped out of Tellson’s ledgers, to turn cooks and carpenters. As a tutor, whose attainments made the student’s way unusually pleasant and profitable, and as an elegant translator who brought something to his work besides mere dictionary knowledge, young Mr. Darnay soon became known and encouraged. He was well acquainted, more-over, with the circumstances of his country, and those were of ever-growing interest. So, with great perseverance and untiring industry, he prospered.
domestication: 又过了十二个月，查尔斯·丹雷先生已经在英格兰站稳了脚跟，成为了法国语言和文学的高级教师。当时，他的地位相当于教授，只不过称呼不同，当时管他这种人叫家庭教师。他与有一定空闲时间和兴趣的年轻人一起研读法国文学，为他们打开了通往知识宝藏的大门。除此之外，他还能用流利的英语写作，并将法语作品译成英文。在当时，这样的师资并不容易找，因为真正的贵族还没沦落到做老师的地步，而那些破产的贵族也还没开始改行当厨师或木匠。由于丹雷先生的才华使得学习变得轻松有趣，他很快就成为了知名且备受赞赏的家庭教师和翻译家。另外，他对祖国的情况也非常了解，而这些情况正日益引起人们的兴趣。因此，在他勤奋不懈努力的推动下，他的职业生涯逐渐蓬勃发展。
foreignization: 再过了几个月，总共十二个月过去了，查尔斯·达纳伊先生在英格兰作为一名精通法语文学的法语高级教师站稳了脚跟。在这个时代，他本可以成为一名教授；在那个时代，他是一名家庭教师。他与能够找到时间和兴趣来学习全世界都在使用的活语言的年轻人一起阅读，并培养了他们对这种语言知识和想象力的兴趣。他还能用流利的英语写作，并将法语作品译成流利的英语。当时，这样的师资并不容易找到；曾经或将来的王子还没有加入教师阶层，也没有破产的贵族从Tellson银行的账簿中脱颖而出，去当厨师和木匠。作为一名家庭教师，他的成就让学生的学习变得异常愉快和有收获，而且作为一名优雅的翻译家，他除了字典知识之外，还带来了其他东西，所以年轻的达纳伊先生很快就被人们所了解和赞赏。此外，他还非常熟悉自己国家的情况，而这些情况正在变得越来越令人感兴趣。因此，在经过长时间的坚持不懈和不知疲倦的努力后，他成功了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

More months, to the number of twelve, had come and gone, and Mr. Charles Darnay was established in England as a higher teacher of the French language who was conversant with French literature. In this age, he would have been a Professor; in that age, he was a Tutor. He read with young men who could find any leisure and interest for the study of a living tongue spoken all over the world, and he cultivated a taste for its stores of knowledge and fancy. He could write of them, besides, in sound English, and render them into sound English. Such masters were not at that time easily found; Princes that had been, and Kings that were to be, were not yet of the Teacher class, and no ruined nobility had dropped out of Tellson’s ledgers, to turn cooks and carpenters. As a tutor, whose attainments made the student’s way unusually pleasant and profitable, and as an elegant translator who brought something to his work besides mere dictionary knowledge, young Mr. Darnay soon became known and encouraged. He was well acquainted, more-over, with the circumstances of his country, and those were of ever-growing interest. So, with great perseverance and untiring industry, he prospered.

source: “Ah! So much the worse! A bitter taste it is that such poor cattle always have in their mouths, and hard lives they live, Jacques. Am I right, Jacques?”
domestication: 唉，这下子可糟了！这些可怜的牲口嘴里总是苦涩涩的，过得也很艰难，真的是命苦啊，杰克，你说呢？
foreignization: “啊！这么说更糟糕！嘴里有那么糟糕的味道是那些可怜的牲畜总是有的，而他们过着艰难的生活，雅克。 我说的对吗，雅克？”

“Ah! So much the worse! A bitter taste it is that such poor cattle always have in their mouths, and hard lives they live, Jacques. Am I right, Jacques?”
“啊！这么说更糟糕！嘴里有那么糟糕的味道是那些可怜的牲畜总是有的，而他们过着艰难的生活，雅克。 我说的对吗，雅克？”

source: “I am again at work upon the hill-side, and the sun is again about to go to bed. I am collecting my tools to descend to my cottage down in the village below, where it is already dark, when I raise my eyes, and see coming over the hill six soldiers. In the midst of them is a tall man with his arms bound—tied to his sides—like this!”
domestication: 我又开始在山坡上工作了，太阳也快落下山了，我收拾着工具准备下山回到村子里的小屋，这时天已经黑了，我抬头一看，发现六个士兵正从山上走过来，其中有一个高个子男人双臂被绑着，和身子捆得死死的，就像这样。
foreignization: “我又一次在山坡上工作，太阳又一次即将落下。我正在收拾我的工具，准备下到村子里我那间小屋，已经很暗了，这时候我抬起头，看见六个士兵正从山上过来。在他们中间有一个高个子男人，他的双臂被捆绑着——绑在他身体两侧——就像这样！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I am again at work upon the hill-side, and the sun is again about to go to bed. I am collecting my tools to descend to my cottage down in the village below, where it is already dark, when I raise my eyes, and see coming over the hill six soldiers. In the midst of them is a tall man with his arms bound—tied to his sides—like this!”
我又开始在山坡上工作了，太阳也快落下山了，我收拾着工具准备下山回到村子里的小屋，这时天已经黑了，我抬头一看，发现六个士兵正从山上走过来，其中有一个高个子男人双臂被绑着，和身子捆得死死的，就像这样。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I am again at work upon the hill-side, and the sun is again about to go to bed. I am collecting my tools to descend to my cottage down in the village below, where it is already dark, when I raise my eyes, and see coming over the hill six soldiers. In the midst of them is a tall man with his arms bound—tied to his sides—like this!”
“我又一次在山坡上工作，太阳又一次即将落下。我正在收拾我的工具，准备下到村子里我那间小屋，已经很暗了，这时候我抬起头，看见六个士兵正从山上过来。在他们中间有一个高个子男人，他的双臂被捆绑着——绑在他身体两侧——就像这样！”

source: Awakened by a timid local functionary and three armed patriots in rough red caps and with pipes in their mouths, who sat down on the bed.
domestication:
他被一个害羞的当地官员和三个带武器的爱国者叫醒，后者头戴红色粗糙的帽子，嘴里叼着烟斗，他们坐在他的床上。或者，更通顺的说法是：

他被一阵喧闹吵醒，一开眼便看到一位当地的小官和三位热情的爱国者，头上戴着粗糙的红帽子，嘴里叼着烟斗，他们大模大样地坐在他的床上。
foreignization: 被一个胆小的当地官员和三个穿着粗糙红色帽子、嘴里叼着烟斗的武装爱国者唤醒，他们坐在床上。
