source: The accused, who was (and who knew he was) being mentally hanged, beheaded, and quartered, by everybody there, neither flinched from the situation, nor assumed any theatrical air in it. He was quiet and attentive; watched the opening proceedings with a grave interest; and stood with his hands resting on the slab of wood before him, so composedly, that they had not displaced a leaf of the herbs with which it was strewn. The court was all bestrewn with herbs and sprinkled with vinegar, as a precaution against gaol air and gaol fever.
domestication: 被告知道自己在众人眼中已经是死定了，但他仍然面不改色。整个过程中，他既没有畏缩，也没有做作。他静静地、认真地看着庭审开始，双手安静地放在面前的木板上，就像是在享受一场好戏一样镇定，连木板上的草药都没被他碰乱。法庭里到处都是散落的草药和洒下的醋，为了防止监狱里的瘟疫传播，大家都很小心。
foreignization: 被告者，被所有在场的人们精神上处以绞刑、斩首和肢解，他知道自己就是这样，然而他在这种情形下既没有畏缩，也没有装腔作势。他很安静，很专注；以肃穆的兴趣注视着开庭的进行；双手平放于他面前的木板上，如此镇定自若，以至于他没有打扰到木板上撒的任何一片香草。法庭上满是洒满香草并喷洒着醋作为预防监狱瘴气和监狱热的措施。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The accused, who was (and who knew he was) being mentally hanged, beheaded, and quartered, by everybody there, neither flinched from the situation, nor assumed any theatrical air in it. He was quiet and attentive; watched the opening proceedings with a grave interest; and stood with his hands resting on the slab of wood before him, so composedly, that they had not displaced a leaf of the herbs with which it was strewn. The court was all bestrewn with herbs and sprinkled with vinegar, as a precaution against gaol air and gaol fever.
被告知道自己在众人眼中已经是死定了，但他仍然面不改色。整个过程中，他既没有畏缩，也没有做作。他静静地、认真地看着庭审开始，双手安静地放在面前的木板上，就像是在享受一场好戏一样镇定，连木板上的草药都没被他碰乱。法庭里到处都是散落的草药和洒下的醋，为了防止监狱里的瘟疫传播，大家都很小心。

source: He was to be told (said Monseigneur) that supper awaited him then and there, and that he was prayed to come to it. In a little while he came. He had been known in England as Charles Darnay.
domestication: 蒙西尼尔说，要告诉他，现在有宴席在等他，他该来赴宴了。过了一会儿，他来了。他在英格兰以夏尔·丹雷的名字为人所知。
foreignization: 他被告知（莫尼格尼耶说）晚餐在那时和那地等待着他，并且希望他能够来赴宴。一会儿之后，他来了。他曾以查尔斯·达纳伊的身份为人所知于英格兰。

source: The fellow pointed to his joke with immense significance, as is often the way with his tribe. It missed its mark, and completely failed, as is often the way with his tribe too.
domestication: 这家伙很有意味地指着自己的笑话，就像他的同类常做的那样。他的笑话没有产生预期的效果，完全失败了，这也是他同类常见的情况。
foreignization: 这位先生对他的笑话非常看重， 就像他那类人通常的表现方式一样。 然而它没能起到作用，并且完全失败了，就像他那类人经常做的那样。

“你很疲倦，”夫人说着，抬起眼睛，她系着钱。“只有平常的气味。”

source: Notwithstanding Miss Pross’s denial of her own imagination, there was a perception of the pain of being monotonously haunted by one sad idea, in her repetition of the phrase, walking up and down, which testified to her possessing such a thing.
domestication: 尽管普罗斯小姐拒绝承认自己有想象力，但她的行为却出卖了她：当她不停地重复某句话，走来走去时，流露出了她对某个悲伤念头的执着，这种执着让她感到单调的折磨。这种重复的举动证明，她确实拥有某种难以释怀的情感。换句话说，普罗斯小姐不管是否愿意承认，已经被某个伤心的想法一直纠缠着，这让她不得不反复地诉说出来。

source: “That depends. I may find a use for it one day. If I do—Well,” said madame, drawing a breath and nodding her head with a stern kind of coquetry, “I’ll use it!”
domestication: 这就要看情况了，我说不定哪天会用得上呢。如果我真的用得上的话，那就……麦德姆深吸了一口气，点头时眼神中带着一种严肃的调情意味，“我就会大胆去用！”

source: With an admonitory gesture to keep them back, he stooped, and looked in through the crevice in the wall. Soon raising his head again, he struck twice or thrice upon the door—evidently with no other object than to make a noise there. With the same intention, he drew the key across it, three or four times, before he put it clumsily into the lock, and turned it as heavily as he could.
domestication: 他警告地挥了挥手，让他们退后，然后弯腰从墙上的缝隙往里看。很快，他又抬起头，在门上敲了两三下，显然只是想制造点响声。有同样的用意，他将钥匙在门上刮了三四次，才笨拙地插入锁中，并且使尽力气将它转动。
foreignization: 他用一个告诫的手势让他们退后，弯腰从墙上的缝隙往里面看。 不久，他再次抬起头，在门上敲了两三下——显然其他目的只有制造一些噪音。在同样的意图下，他在把钥匙塞进锁头并转动它之前，用钥匙在门上划过三四次，并且尽可能地使劲转动。

source: Madame Defarge sat observing it, with such suppressed approval as was to be desired in the leader of the Saint Antoine women. One of her sisterhood knitted beside her. The short, rather plump wife of a starved grocer, and the mother of two children withal, this lieutenant had already earned the complimentary name of The Vengeance.
domestication: 德法尔热夫人带着一种很有分寸的赞同态度注视着这一幕，这正是圣安托万妇女的领袖该有的风范。她的一个同伴坐在她旁边，认真地织着毛衣。这位同伴是某位穷饿潦倒的杂货店老板的妻子，也是两个孩子的母亲，已经因为自己勇猛的表现而被人亲切地称作“复仇者”。
foreignization: 德法尔热夫人带着一种像圣安托万妇女领袖所期望的那样被压抑的赞同态度看着他。一位她的姐妹们在她旁边纺织。那个矮壮、有点丰满的面包店老板的妻子，同时也是两个孩子的母亲，这个中尉已经获得了“复仇”这个称号。

Madame Defarge sat observing it, with such suppressed approval as was to be desired in the leader of the Saint Antoine women. One of her sisterhood knitted beside her. The short, rather plump wife of a starved grocer, and the mother of two children withal, this lieutenant had already earned the complimentary name of The Vengeance.

The short, rather plump wife of a starved grocer, and the mother of two children withal, this lieutenant had already earned the complimentary name of The Vengeance.
德法尔热夫人带着一种像圣安托万妇女领袖所期望的那样被压抑的赞同态度看着他。一位她的姐妹们在她旁边纺织。那个矮壮、有点丰满的面包店老板的妻子，同时也是两个孩子的母亲，这个中尉已经获得了“复仇”这个称号。

source: Saint Antoine slept, the Defarges slept: even The Vengeance slept with her starved grocer, and the drum was at rest. The drum’s was the only voice in Saint Antoine that blood and hurry had not changed. The Vengeance, as custodian of the drum, could have wakened him up and had the same speech out of him as before the Bastille fell, or old Foulon was seized; not so with the hoarse tones of the men and women in Saint Antoine’s bosom.
domestication: 圣安托万的人都睡着了，德法尔日一家也睡着了，甚至那位报复女神也和她的饿得皮包骨的店员一起进入了梦乡，战鼓也停止了敲打。在圣安托万，只有战鼓的声音保持不变，没有被血腥和混乱所改变。作为战鼓的守护者，报复女神可以唤醒它，并让它发出与以往一样的呼喊，就像巴士底狱沦陷之前，或老富伦被抓获之前一样。但是圣安托万街道上男人和女人们粗哑的叫嚣声，却再也不会回到从前了。
foreignization: 圣安托万睡了，德法尔日夫人也睡了：甚至复仇女神也和她饥饿的杂货商一起睡着了，鼓也休息了。鼓的声音是圣安托万唯一没有被血腥和匆忙改变的声音。作为鼓的保管人，复仇女神可以唤醒它，并从中得到和巴士底狱沦陷之前，或老富伦被抓获之前相同的声音；但是圣安托万心中的男人和女人们的嘶哑声音就不一样了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Saint Antoine slept, the Defarges slept: even The Vengeance slept with her starved grocer, and the drum was at rest. The drum’s was the only voice in Saint Antoine that blood and hurry had not changed. The Vengeance, as custodian of the drum, could have wakened him up and had the same speech out of him as before the Bastille fell, or old Foulon was seized; not so with the hoarse tones of the men and women in Saint Antoine’s bosom.
圣安托万的人都睡着了，德法尔日一家也睡着了，甚至那位报复女神也和她的饿得皮包骨的店员一起进入了梦乡，战鼓也停止了敲打。在圣安托万，只有战鼓的声音保持不变，没有被血腥和混乱所改变。作为战鼓的守护者，报复女神可以唤醒它，并让它发出与以往一样的呼喊，就像巴士底狱沦陷之前，或老富伦被抓获之前一样。但是圣安托万街道上男人和女人们粗哑的叫嚣声，却再也不会回到从前了。

source: “Yes; for a moment. At first I thought it quite hopeless, but I have unquestionably seen, for a single moment, the face that I once knew so well. Hush! Let us draw further back. Hush!”
domestication: "对，虽然只有片刻。起初我觉得完全没希望，但我确实看到了，我曾经非常熟悉的那张脸，虽然只是一瞬间。嘘！我们退远点儿。嘘！"
foreignization: “是的，只有一瞬间。起初我觉得完全没有希望，但我无疑地看到了，我曾经非常熟悉的那张脸，一瞬间。我想让我们退后一些，安静！让我们退后一些，安静！”

messages_domestication.content:

“Yes; for a moment. At first I thought it quite hopeless, but I have unquestionably seen, for a single moment, the face that I once knew so well. Hush! Let us draw further back. Hush!”

source: “You don’t get on with your account of your matrimonial intentions,” answered Carton, with a careless air; “I wish you would keep to that. As to me—will you never understand that I am incorrigible?”
domestication: “看来你还没说到正题——谈谈你的婚事打算吧，”卡顿漫不经心地回答道，“希望你能专心说这件事。至于我，你难道永远也看不透我这个不可救药的人吗？”
foreignization: “你没能成功地说明你婚姻的打算，”卡顿带着漫不经心的态度回答；“我希望你还是专心谈那件事吧。至于我——你难道永远也不会明白，我是无法改正的吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You don’t get on with your account of your matrimonial intentions,” answered Carton, with a careless air; “I wish you would keep to that. As to me—will you never understand that I am incorrigible?”
“看来你还没说到正题——谈谈你的婚事打算吧，”卡顿漫不经心地回答道，“希望你能专心说这件事。至于我，你难道永远也看不透我这个不可救药的人吗？”

messages_foreignization.content:

“You don’t get on with your account of your matrimonial intentions,” answered Carton, with a careless air; “I wish you would keep to that. As to me—will you never understand that I am incorrigible?”
“你没能成功地说明你婚姻的打算，”卡顿带着漫不经心的态度回答；“我希望你还是专心谈那件事吧。至于我——你难道永远也不会明白，我是无法改正的吗？”

source: “Which I entreated you to believe, again and again, most fervently, with all my heart, was capable of better things, Mr. Carton!”
domestication: "我曾经多次恳求你相信，我是有出息的，卡顿先生！"
foreignization: “先生，我曾再三、最热切地恳求你相信，我有能力做出更好的事情，卡顿先生！”

“Which I entreated you to believe, again and again, most fervently, with all my heart, was capable of better things, Mr. Carton!”
"我曾经多次恳求你相信，我是有出息的，卡顿先生！"

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Which I entreated you to believe, again and again, most fervently, with all my heart, was capable of better things, Mr. Carton!”
“先生，我曾再三、最热切地恳求你相信，我有能力做出更好的事情，卡顿先生！”

source: “Monseigneur, the good God knows; but I don’t ask it. My petition is, that a morsel of stone or wood, with my husband’s name, may be placed over him to show where he lies. Otherwise, the place will be quickly forgotten, it will never be found when I am dead of the same malady, I shall be laid under some other heap of poor grass. Monseigneur, they are so many, they increase so fast, there is so much want. Monseigneur! Monseigneur!”
domestication: “大人，老天有眼；但我不求什么了。我只求能在我丈夫的坟上放块石头或一根木头，上面刻上他的名字，以免他的埋葬之处被忘记。要不然，等我也死于同样的病痛时，就会被随便扔在另一堆杂草下。大人，穷人太多了，他们死得太快，贫困的人实在太需要帮助了。大人！大人！”
foreignization: “大人，仁慈的上帝知道；但是我不要求这件事。我的请求是，把一块刻有我丈夫名字的石头或木头放在他身上，以表明他所在的位置。否则，这个地方很快就会被遗忘，当我死于同样的病症时，我将被埋葬在另一堆贫瘠的草丛下。大人，大人！他们太多了，增加得太快，有太多的匮乏。大人，大人！”

Monseigneur, they are so many, they increase so fast, there is so much want. Monseigneur! Monseigneur!”

source: Cannon, muskets, fire and smoke; but, still the deep ditch, the single drawbridge, the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers. Slight displacements of the raging sea, made by the falling wounded. Flashing weapons, blazing torches, smoking waggonloads of wet straw, hard work at neighbouring barricades in all directions, shrieks, volleys, execrations, bravery without stint, boom smash and rattle, and the furious sounding of the living sea; but, still the deep ditch, and the single drawbridge, and the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers, and still Defarge of the wine-shop at his gun, grown doubly hot by the service of Four fierce hours.
domestication: 大炮轰鸣，火枪爆炸，火焰熊熊，浓烟滚滚。但是，深深的护城河，独一无二的吊桥，厚重的石墙，以及八座高耸的塔楼仍然屹立不动。海浪般的激烈战斗中，受伤的人倒下，导致波涛稍有波动。闪烁的刀剑，熊熊的火炬，大车载着湿透的干草冒着烟，四面八方的街垒正在加紧修筑，尖叫声，枪声，咒骂声，勇敢的士兵毫不畏惧，战鼓雷鸣，金属碰撞，愤怒的人潮澎湃然。但是，深深的护城河，独一无二的吊桥，厚重的石墙，以及八座高耸的塔楼仍然巍然屹立。同时，酒馆老板德法尔热还在他的炮位上，经过四个小时的激烈战斗，他变得更加凶猛。
foreignization: 大炮、火枪、火焰和烟雾；但是，依然是深深的护城河、独一无二的吊桥、巨大的石墙和八座伟大的塔楼。狂暴的大海由于倒下的伤者而产生的轻微波动。闪烁的武器、熊熊的火炬、冒烟的湿草满载的马车、邻近的街垒到处都在辛苦地工作、尖叫、齐射、咒骂、毫无保留的勇敢、轰鸣、碰撞、叮当声，以及狂怒的响声的活生生的大海；但是，依然是深深的护城河、独一无二的吊桥、巨大的石墙、八座伟大的塔楼，依然是酒馆里的德法尔热站在他的大炮旁，由四个小时的激烈战斗而变得更加炽热。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Cannon, muskets, fire and smoke; but, still the deep ditch, the single drawbridge, the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers. Slight displacements of the raging sea, made by the falling wounded. Flashing weapons, blazing torches, smoking waggonloads of wet straw, hard work at neighbouring barricades in all directions, shrieks, volleys, execrations, bravery without stint, boom smash and rattle, and the furious sounding of the living sea; but, still the deep ditch, and the single drawbridge, and the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers, and still Defarge of the wine-shop at his gun, grown doubly hot by the service of Four fierce hours.

大炮轰鸣，火枪爆炸，火焰熊熊，浓烟滚滚。但是，深深的护城河，独一无二的吊桥，厚重的石墙，以及八座高耸的塔楼仍然屹立不动。海浪般的激烈战斗中，受伤的人倒下，导致波涛稍有波动。闪烁的刀剑，熊熊的火炬，大车载着湿透的干草冒着烟，四面八方的街垒正在加紧修筑，尖叫声，枪声，咒骂声，勇敢的士兵毫不畏惧，战鼓雷鸣，金属碰撞，愤怒的人潮澎湃然。但是，深深的护城河，独一无二的吊桥，厚重的石墙，以及八座高耸的塔楼仍然巍然屹立。同时，酒馆老板德法尔热还在他的炮位上，经过四个小时的激烈战斗，他变得更加凶猛。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Cannon, muskets, fire and smoke; but, still the deep ditch, the single drawbridge, the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers. Slight displacements of the raging sea, made by the falling wounded. Flashing weapons, blazing torches, smoking waggonloads of wet straw, hard work at neighbouring barricades in all directions, shrieks, volleys, execrations, bravery without stint, boom smash and rattle, and the furious sounding of the living sea; but, still the deep ditch, and the single drawbridge, and the massive stone walls, and the eight great towers, and still Defarge of the wine-shop at his gun, grown doubly hot by the service of Four fierce hours.

大炮、火枪、火焰和烟雾；但是，依然是深深的护城河、独一无二的吊桥、巨大的石墙和八座伟大的塔楼。狂暴的大海由于倒下的伤者而产生的轻微波动。闪烁的武器、熊熊的火炬、冒烟的湿草满载的马车、邻近的街垒到处都在辛苦地工作、尖叫、齐射、咒骂、毫无保留的勇敢、轰鸣、碰撞、叮当声，以及狂怒的响声的活生生的大海；但是，依然是深深的护城河、独一无二的吊桥、巨大的石墙、八座伟大的塔楼，依然是酒馆里的德法尔热站在他的大炮旁，由四个小时的激烈战斗而变得更加炽热。

source: At length the sun rose so high that it struck a kindly ray as of hope or protection, directly down upon the old prisoner’s head. The favour was too much to bear; in an instant the barrier of dust and chaff that had stood surprisingly long, went to the winds, and Saint Antoine had got him!
domestication: 终于，太阳升到了高处，一道温暖的光线照在老囚犯的头上，这光线仿佛带来了希望和保护的信息。这份恩惠实在让人受不了，转眼之间，长久以来一直站立的尘土和谷壳的屏障就被风吹散了，圣安托万的人们已经控制了局面！
foreignization: 终于，太阳升得这么高，以至于它发出了一道充满希望或保护的仁慈光线，直接照在了老囚犯的头上。这份恩惠实在难以承受；转瞬之间，那一直奇迹般地屹立不倒的尘土和谷壳的屏障，被吹散到四面八方，圣安托万的人们已经抓住了他！

source: “Genuine truth, Mr. Darnay, trust me! I have gone aside from my purpose; I was speaking about our being friends. Now, you know me; you know I am incapable of all the higher and better flights of men. If you doubt it, ask Stryver, and he’ll tell you so.”
domestication: "说真的，达尔内先生，请相信我！我刚才跑题了，我当时想谈的是我们的友谊。现在，你已经了解我了，你知道我不是那种有远大理想和高尚情怀的人。如果你有疑虑，就去问斯特莱佛，他会告诉你一样的。"
foreignization: “真正的真相，达纳里先生，请相信我！我已经偏离了我的目的；我刚才在谈论我们之间的友谊。现在，你了解我；你知道我不具备那些高尚而优异的品质。如果你怀疑这一点，请问斯垂佛，他会告诉你。”

source: Mr. Lorry, who had gone out when the young lady and her father went out, now reappeared, and beckoned to Jerry: who, in the slackened interest, could easily get near him.
domestication: 洛里先生在年轻的小姐和她老头子出门的时候也出去了，现在他重新出现了，并向杰瑞招手。在大家注意力都放松的时候，杰瑞轻而易举地凑到了洛里先生身边。
foreignization: 洛里先生在那位年轻的小姐和她的父亲出去的时候也出去了，现在他又出现了，并示意杰瑞过去：而此时由于大家的兴趣已经减退，杰瑞很容易地走到他身边。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Lorry, who had gone out when the young lady and her father went out, now reappeared, and beckoned to Jerry: who, in the slackened interest, could easily get near him.
洛里先生在年轻的小姐和她老头子出门的时候也出去了，现在他重新出现了，并向杰瑞招手。在大家注意力都放松的时候，杰瑞轻而易举地凑到了洛里先生身边。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Lorry, who had gone out when the young lady and her father went out, now reappeared, and beckoned to Jerry: who, in the slackened interest, could easily get near him.
洛里先生在那位年轻的小姐和她的父亲出去的时候也出去了，现在他又出现了，并示意杰瑞过去：而此时由于大家的兴趣已经减退，杰瑞很容易地走到他身边。

source: “I know this messenger, guard,” said Mr. Lorry, getting down into the road—assisted from behind more swiftly than politely by the other two passengers, who immediately scrambled into the coach, shut the door, and pulled up the window. “He may come close; there’s nothing wrong.”
domestication: 洛里先生边说边从马车上下来，还没等他站稳，另外两个人就已经从后面把他推下来，动作快的很，礼貌倒是不怎么讲了。他们急忙爬进马车，砰地关上门，拉起窗户。洛里先生接着说：“这个送信人我认识，没问题，让他近点来吧。”
foreignization: “我认识这个传信的人，警卫，”劳瑞先生说着走下到了路上——在他身后的另外两位乘客的帮助下，他动作很快，然而却不太讲究礼貌，他们马上爬进了马车，关上了门，并且拉上了窗户。“他可以靠近过来；没有什么问题。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I know this messenger, guard,” said Mr. Lorry, getting down into the road—assisted from behind more swiftly than politely by the other two passengers, who immediately scrambled into the coach, shut the door, and pulled up the window. “He may come close; there’s nothing wrong.”
洛里先生边说边从马车上下来，还没等他站稳，另外两个人就已经从后面把他推下来，动作快的很，礼貌倒是不怎么讲了。他们急忙爬进马车，砰地关上门，拉起窗户。洛里先生接着说：“这个送信人我认识，没问题，让他近点来吧。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I know this messenger, guard,” said Mr. Lorry, getting down into the road—assisted from behind more swiftly than politely by the other two passengers, who immediately scrambled into the coach, shut the door, and pulled up the window. “He may come close; there’s nothing wrong.”
“我认识这个传信的人，警卫，”劳瑞先生说着走下到了路上——在他身后的另外两位乘客的帮助下，他动作很快，然而却不太讲究礼貌，他们马上爬进了马车，关上了门，并且拉上了窗户。“他可以靠近过来；没有什么问题。”

source: “Father,” said Young Jerry, as they walked along: taking care to keep at arm’s length and to have the stool well between them: “what’s a Resurrection-Man?”
domestication: “爸爸，”小杰瑞边走边问道，同时小心地保持着安全距离，让凳子隔在他们中间：“什么是复活人？”他歪着头，眼里满是好奇。其实，小杰瑞心里已经有些打鼓，因为他听说过一些关于复活人的传言，觉得很恐怖。但他还是忍不住问出了这个问题，他想弄清楚这到底是什么意思。
foreignization: "父亲，”小杰瑞一边走着说，同时注意保持与他之间的距离，并且让凳子放在他们中间：“什么是复活人？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Father,” said Young Jerry, as they walked along: taking care to keep at arm’s length and to have the stool well between them: “what’s a Resurrection-Man?”
“爸爸，”小杰瑞边走边问道，同时小心地保持着安全距离，让凳子隔在他们中间：“什么是复活人？”他歪着头，眼里满是好奇。其实，小杰瑞心里已经有些打鼓，因为他听说过一些关于复活人的传言，觉得很恐怖。但他还是忍不住问出了这个问题，他想弄清楚这到底是什么意思。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Father,” said Young Jerry, as they walked along: taking care to keep at arm’s length and to have the stool well between them: “what’s a Resurrection-Man?”
"父亲，”小杰瑞一边走着说，同时注意保持与他之间的距离，并且让凳子放在他们中间：“什么是复活人？”

source: Avoiding the larger rooms, which were dark and made fast for the night, Monsieur the Marquis, with his flambeau-bearer going on before, went up the staircase to a door in a corridor. This thrown open, admitted him to his own private apartment of three rooms: his bed-chamber and two others. High vaulted rooms with cool uncarpeted floors, great dogs upon the hearths for the burning of wood in winter time, and all luxuries befitting the state of a marquis in a luxurious age and country. The fashion of the last Louis but one, of the line that was never to break—the fourteenth Louis—was conspicuous in their rich furniture; but, it was diversified by many objects that were illustrations of old pages in the history of France.
domestication: 马奎斯先生避开了那些已经为夜晚做好准备的黑暗大房间，带着前面提灯笼的人，一起走上了楼梯，来到一条走廊上的一个门前。这扇门打开后，是他的私人住所，有三个房间：卧室和另外两个房间。这些高高的拱形天花板的房间，地板很凉爽，没有铺地毯，在冬天里，壁炉上还有大狗，所有的豪华设施都与奢靡时代和国家中的侯爵身份相符。家具的款式是路易十四前的那位路易，即将要断绝王位继承权的路易十四时期的风格；但同时，也融入了许多物品，反映了法国历史旧篇章的内容。整个空间装饰豪华，令人惊艳。
foreignization: 避开较大的房间，那些房间很暗，并且已经为夜晚做好了准备，马奎斯先生在他的火炬手的带领下，走上了楼梯，来到走廊里的一个门前。这扇门打开后，通向了他自己的私人三室套房：他的卧室和另外两间房。这些房间有着高高的拱形天花板，地板凉爽，没有铺地毯，在冬季里，壁炉上有大狗，所有豪华设施都与马奎斯在豪奢时代和国家中的地位相符。路易十四前的路易，即永远不会被打破的路易王朝的风格，在他们豪华的家具中非常突出；但是，它们被许多物品所丰富，这些物品是法国历史上古老篇章的插图。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Avoiding the larger rooms, which were dark and made fast for the night, Monsieur the Marquis, with his flambeau-bearer going on before, went up the staircase to a door in a corridor. This thrown open, admitted him to his own private apartment of three rooms: his bed-chamber and two others. High vaulted rooms with cool uncarpeted floors, great dogs upon the hearths for the burning of wood in winter time, and all luxuries befitting the state of a marquis in a luxurious age and country. The fashion of the last Louis but one, of the line that was never to break—the fourteenth Louis—was conspicuous in their rich furniture; but, it was diversified by many objects that were illustrations of old pages in the history of France.

马奎斯先生避开了那些已经为夜晚做好准备的黑暗大房间，带着前面提灯笼的人，一起走上了楼梯，来到一条走廊上的一个门前。这扇门打开后，是他的私人住所，有三个房间：卧室和另外两个房间。这些高高的拱形天花板的房间，地板很凉爽，没有铺地毯，在冬天里，壁炉上还有大狗，所有的豪华设施都与奢靡时代和国家中的侯爵身份相符。家具的款式是路易十四前的那位路易，即将要断绝王位继承权的路易十四时期的风格；但同时，也融入了许多物品，反映了法国历史旧篇章的内容。整个空间装饰豪华，令人惊艳。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Avoiding the larger rooms, which were dark and made fast for the night, Monsieur the Marquis, with his flambeau-bearer going on before, went up the staircase to a door in a corridor. This thrown open, admitted him to his own private apartment of three rooms: his bed-chamber and two others. High vaulted rooms with cool uncarpeted floors, great dogs upon the hearths for the burning of wood in winter time, and all luxuries befitting the state of a marquis in a luxurious age and country. The fashion of the last Louis but one, of the line that was never to break—the fourteenth Louis—was conspicuous in their rich furniture; but, it was diversified by many objects that were illustrations of old pages in the history of France.

避开较大的房间，那些房间很暗，并且已经为夜晚做好了准备，马奎斯先生在他的火炬手的带领下，走上了楼梯，来到走廊里的一个门前。这扇门打开后，通向了他自己的私人三室套房：他的卧室和另外两间房。这些房间有着高高的拱形天花板，地板凉爽，没有铺地毯，在冬季里，壁炉上有大狗，所有豪华设施都与马奎斯在豪奢时代和国家中的地位相符。路易十四前的路易，即永远不会被打破的路易王朝的风格，在他们豪华的家具中非常突出；但是，它们被许多物品所丰富，这些物品是法国历史上古老篇章的插图。

source: “Is it being a good wife to oppose your husband’s business? Is it honouring your husband to dishonour his business? Is it obeying your husband to disobey him on the wital subject of his business?”
domestication: 难道反对丈夫的生意就是做个好妻子吗？难道破坏他的事业就是尊敬他吗？难道在他最关心的生意问题上违背他，就是听从他吗？说来说去，这些问题其实是在问：你到底是支持丈夫还是在拆他的台？
foreignization: “反对丈夫的生意是做个好妻子吗？毁坏他生意是尊敬丈夫吗？在他生意这件最重要的事上违背他的意愿是听从丈夫吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Is it being a good wife to oppose your husband’s business? Is it honouring your husband to dishonour his business? Is it obeying your husband to disobey him on the wital subject of his business?”
难道反对丈夫的生意就是做个好妻子吗？难道破坏他的事业就是尊敬他吗？难道在他最关心的生意问题上违背他，就是听从他吗？说来说去，这些问题其实是在问：你到底是支持丈夫还是在拆他的台？

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Is it being a good wife to oppose your husband’s business? Is it honouring your husband to dishonour his business? Is it obeying your husband to disobey him on the wital subject of his business?”
“反对丈夫的生意是做个好妻子吗？毁坏他生意是尊敬丈夫吗？在他生意这件最重要的事上违背他的意愿是听从丈夫吗？”

source: He studied much, slept little, sustained a great deal of fatigue with ease, and was equably cheerful. To him, now entered Charles Darnay, at sight of whom he laid aside his book and held out his hand.
domestication: 他学习非常勤奋，很少睡觉，但却能轻松地承受大量的劳累，而且总是保持着愉快的心情。这时候，夏尔查·当纳走了进来，他一见到当纳，就把书放在一边，伸出了手。
foreignization: 他学习非常努力，睡眠很少，轻松地承受了大量的疲劳，并且始终保持着愉快的心情。这时，夏尔·丹雷进入了他的房间，他一看到对方，就放下书，伸出了手。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He studied much, slept little, sustained a great deal of fatigue with ease, and was equably cheerful. To him, now entered Charles Darnay, at sight of whom he laid aside his book and held out his hand.
他学习非常勤奋，很少睡觉，但却能轻松地承受大量的劳累，而且总是保持着愉快的心情。这时候，夏尔查·当纳走了进来，他一见到当纳，就把书放在一边，伸出了手。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He studied much, slept little, sustained a great deal of fatigue with ease, and was equably cheerful. To him, now entered Charles Darnay, at sight of whom he laid aside his book and held out his hand.
他学习非常努力，睡眠很少，轻松地承受了大量的疲劳，并且始终保持着愉快的心情。这时，夏尔·丹雷进入了他的房间，他一看到对方，就放下书，伸出了手。

source: At first, there were times, though she was a perfectly happy young wife, when her work would slowly fall from her hands, and her eyes would be dimmed. For, there was something coming in the echoes, something light, afar off, and scarcely audible yet, that stirred her heart too much. Fluttering hopes and doubts—hopes, of a love as yet unknown to her: doubts, of her remaining upon earth, to enjoy that new delight—divided her breast. Among the echoes then, there would arise the sound of footsteps at her own early grave; and thoughts of the husband who would be left so desolate, and who would mourn for her so much, swelled to her eyes, and broke like waves.
domestication: 一开始，有时候，她会感到手里的事情慢慢放下，眼前变得模糊。因为，在回声中，有些东西正在悄悄地靠近，这些东西是轻微的，遥远的，几乎听不到，但却激动得她心跳加速。她心里充满了对未知的爱的希望和对自己能否享受到这种新乐趣的疑虑。这些希望和疑虑让她的心思绪万千。在那些回声中，她会听到自己早逝的墓地上回荡的脚步声，想到如果自己离开了人世，将会留下一个如此伤心的丈夫，他会为她悲痛欲绝，这些想法涌上她的心头，如同波浪般击打着她，使她的眼泪夺眶而出。
foreignization: 起初，有时候，尽管她是一个完全幸福的年轻妻子，但她的工作会慢慢从她的手中滑落，她的眼睛也会变得昏暗。因为，在回声中，有某种东西正在靠近，某种微弱的、遥远的、几乎听不见的声音，它太过激动了她的心。闪烁的希望和疑虑——对她来说尚未知晓的爱的希望；对她还能留在地球上去享受那种新乐趣的疑虑——使她的心胸变得矛盾。在那时，在回声中，她自己的早逝坟墓上就会出现脚步声；还有对将要留下来的丈夫，他将如此孤独地思念她，会让她的眼里充满泪水，像海浪般溢出。

source: “Charles Darnay! I rejoice to see you. We have been counting on your return these three or four days past. Mr. Stryver and Sydney Carton were both here yesterday, and both made you out to be more than due.”
domestication: 查尔斯·丹尼回来得正好！这几天我们都在指望你回来呢。史特莱佛先生和悉尼·卡顿昨天都来了，他们两个都说你该回来了。
foreignization: “查尔斯·丹尼！我很高兴见到你，我们这三四天一直在等你的归来，斯特赖弗先生和悉尼·卡顿昨天都来过这里，他们两个人都说你已经超期了。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Charles Darnay! I rejoice to see you. We have been counting on your return these three or four days past. Mr. Stryver and Sydney Carton were both here yesterday, and both made you out to be more than due.”
“查尔斯·丹尼！我很高兴见到你，我们这三四天一直在等你的归来，斯特赖弗先生和悉尼·卡顿昨天都来过这里，他们两个人都说你已经超期了。”

source: Had he ever been a spy himself? No, he scorned the base insinuation. What did he live upon? His property. Where was his property? He didn’t precisely remember where it was. What was it? No business of anybody’s. Had he inherited it? Yes, he had. From whom? Distant relation. Very distant? Rather. Ever been in prison? Certainly not. Never in a debtors’ prison? Didn’t see what that had to do with it. Never in a debtors’ prison?—Come, once again. Never? Yes. How many times? Two or three times. Not five or six? Perhaps. Of what profession? Gentleman. Ever been kicked? Might have been. Frequently? No. Ever kicked downstairs? Decidedly not; once received a kick on the top of a staircase, and fell downstairs of his own accord. Kicked on that occasion for cheating at dice? Something to that effect was said by the intoxicated liar who committed the assault, but it was not true. Swear it was not true? Positively. Ever live by cheating at play? Never. Ever live by play? Not more than other gentlemen do. Ever borrow money of the prisoner? Yes. Ever pay him? No. Was not this intimacy with the prisoner, in reality a very slight one, forced upon the prisoner in coaches, inns, and packets? No. Sure he saw the prisoner with these lists? Certain. Knew no more about the lists? No. Had not procured them himself, for instance? No. Expect to get anything by this evidence? No. Not in regular government pay and employment, to lay traps? Oh dear no. Or to do anything? Oh dear no. Swear that? Over and over again. No motives but motives of sheer patriotism? None whatever.
domestication: 他自己有没有当过间谍？不，他鄙视这种无聊的暗示。那么，他靠什么生活呢？靠他的财产。他的财产在哪里呢？他不太记得了。到底是什么样的财产呢？那与任何人无关。他是继承来的吗？是的，是从一个远亲那里继承来的。很远的亲戚吗？算是吧。在监狱里待过吗？绝对没有。从来没有被关进债务人监狱吗？他不认为这与事情有关。从来没有被关进债务人监狱吗？再问一次。没有吗？是的。几次呢？两三次吧。不是五六次吗？也许吧。你是做什么的？绅士。被踢过吗？可能被踢过。经常被踢吗？不会。被踢下楼吗？绝对没有；有一次是在楼梯顶上被踢了一脚，然后他自行滚下楼去。那次是因为他在掷骰子时作弊被踢的吗？那个醉醺醺的混蛋这么说，但是不真实。他能发誓这是不真实的吗？绝对的。靠作弊赢钱生活过吗？从来没有。靠赌博生活过吗？不比其他绅士多。曾经向被告借过钱吗？是的。还过钱吗？没有。这跟被告的关系，其实只是在马车、客栈和船上才这么熟悉吧？不是的。他肯定看到被告拿着这些名单吗？肯定的。除了这些之外什么也不知道吗？不知道。他自己没有为此提供这些名单吧？没有。他指望通过这个证词得到什么好处吗？没有。他不是受雇于政府，负责设下陷阱吧？哦，不会的。或者为了做其他事情吗？哦，不会的。他能发誓这一点吗？再三发誓。不仅仅是出于爱国主义的动机吗？完全没有。
foreignization: 他自己是否曾经是间谍？不，他鄙视这种卑劣的暗示。他靠什么生活？他的财产。在哪里？他不太记得它在哪里。是什么？这与任何人无关。他是否继承了它？是的，他继承了。从谁那里？远亲。非常远亲？相当于这样。曾经被监禁过吗？绝对没有。从来没有在债务人监狱里吗？不知道这与当前有什么关系。从来没有在债务人监狱里吗？——再问一次。没有吗？是的。几次？两三次。不是五六次吗？也许。是什么职业？绅士。曾经被踢过吗？可能曾经被踢过。经常被踢吗？没有。曾经被踢下楼吗？绝对没有；曾经在楼梯顶端被踢了一脚，然后自己摔下楼去。当时是因为骰子游戏作弊而被踢的吗？发起袭击的醉酒骗子这么说过，但那是不正确的。可以发誓那是不正确的吗？肯定。曾经靠骰子游戏作弊为生吗？永远不会。曾经靠玩游戏为生吗？不比其他绅士多。曾经向被告借钱吗？是的。曾经还过钱吗？没有。这和被告的交往，实际上是一种非常微小的交往，在马车里、客栈里和邮件包裹里强加于被告吗？不。确定他看到被告拿着这些名单？肯定的。除了这些名单外不知道的其他事情吗？没有。例如他自己没有为此获取这些名单吗？没有。指望通过这个证词得到什么吗？没有。不是在政府正式的薪水和雇佣中，设下陷阱？哦，天哪，不。或者做任何事情？哦，天哪，不。可以发誓吗？一次又一次。除了纯粹爱国主义的动机之外没有其他动机吗？根本没有。

source: Miss Pross was a pleasant sight, albeit wild, and red, and grim, taking off her darling’s bonnet when she came up-stairs, and touching it up with the ends of her handkerchief, and blowing the dust off it, and folding her mantle ready for laying by, and smoothing her rich hair with as much pride as she could possibly have taken in her own hair if she had been the vainest and handsomest of women. Her darling was a pleasant sight too, embracing her and thanking her, and protesting against her taking so much trouble for her—which last she only dared to do playfully, or Miss Pross, sorely hurt, would have retired to her own chamber and cried. The Doctor was a pleasant sight too, looking on at them, and telling Miss Pross how she spoilt Lucie, in accents and with eyes that had as much spoiling in them as Miss Pross had, and would have had more if it were possible. Mr. Lorry was a pleasant sight too, beaming at all this in his little wig, and thanking his bachelor stars for having lighted him in his declining years to a Home. But, no Hundreds of people came to see the sights, and Mr. Lorry looked in vain for the fulfilment of Miss Pross’s prediction.
domestication: 普罗小姐真是一道风景，虽然她有点狂野，脸红得像苹果一样，面目也很严肃，但她一上楼就开始给她的亲爱的脱帽子，用手帕边缘轻轻理着，吹去帽子上的灰尘，然后把自己的披肩折好准备收起来，还自豪地抚平头发，就像她是世界上最爱美和最漂亮的女人在打扮自己一样。她的亲爱的也是个美景，一边抱着她，一边感谢她，嘴里说着不要操这么多心——当然，这些话都是开玩笑的，如果她认真的话，普罗小姐会伤心得回到自己房间里哭一场。医生也是个美景，他看着他们俩，告诉普罗小姐她怎么宠坏了露西，他的声音和眼神里都充满了溺爱，就像普罗小姐对待露西一样，甚至更甚。如果可能的话，他还会更加宠溺。拉里先生也是个美景，他戴着小假发，对这一切都报以满面的笑容，感谢命运在他的晚年为他指引了一份家园。但是，没有成百上千的人来看这场景，拉里先生等待普罗小姐预言的实现却等来了失望。
foreignization: 普罗丝小姐是一道令人愉快的景象，尽管她看起来很狂野、满脸通红、表情严肃。当她上楼时，她脱下她心爱的女儿的帽子，用手帕的角擦拭干净，吹去灰尘，然后把她的斗篷折好准备收起来，并且以一种只有最虚荣和最漂亮的女人对自己头发的自豪感才能相比的方式梳理着她那浓密的头发。她的心爱的女儿也是一个令人愉快的景象，拥抱着她并感谢她，并抗议她为自己付出这么多的麻烦——而且她只能开玩笑地这样做，否则普罗丝小姐会因伤心而退回到自己的房间里哭泣。医生也是一个令人愉快的景象，从旁观察着她们，并以充满溺爱的口吻和眼神告诉普罗丝小姐，她是如何宠坏露西的——他的口吻和眼神中有和普罗丝小姐一样多的溺爱之情，如果可能的话，他会更加溺爱。洛瑞先生也是一个令人愉快的景象，他戴着小假发，对这一切都报以微笑，并感谢他单身汉的命运，在他的晚年将他带到了一个家。但是，没有成百上千的人来看这些景象，洛瑞先生徒劳地寻找着普罗丝小姐的预言的实现。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Miss Pross was a pleasant sight, albeit wild, and red, and grim, taking off her darling’s bonnet when she came up-stairs, and touching it up with the ends of her handkerchief, and blowing the dust off it, and folding her mantle ready for laying by, and smoothing her rich hair with as much pride as she could possibly have taken in her own hair if she had been the vainest and handsomest of women. Her darling was a pleasant sight too, embracing her and thanking her, and protesting against her taking so much trouble for her—which last she only dared to do playfully, or Miss Pross, sorely hurt, would have retired to her own chamber and cried. The Doctor was a pleasant sight too, looking on at them, and telling Miss Pross how she spoilt Lucie, in accents and with eyes that had as much spoiling in them as Miss Pross had, and would have had more if it were possible. Mr. Lorry was a pleasant sight too, beaming at all this in his little wig, and thanking his bachelor stars for having lighted him in his declining years to a Home. But, no Hundreds of people came to see the sights, and Mr. Lorry looked in vain for the fulfilment of Miss Pross’s prediction.

普罗小姐真是一道风景，虽然她有点狂野，脸红得像苹果一样，面目也很严肃，但她一上楼就开始给她的亲爱的脱帽子，用手帕边缘轻轻理着，吹去帽子上的灰尘，然后把自己的披肩折好准备收起来，还自豪地抚平头发，就像她是世界上最爱美和最漂亮的女人在打扮自己一样。她的亲爱的也是个美景，一边抱着她，一边感谢她，嘴里说着不要操这么多心——当然，这些话都是开玩笑的，如果她认真的话，普罗小姐会伤心得回到自己房间里哭一场。医生也是个美景，他看着他们俩，告诉普罗小姐她怎么宠坏了露西，他的声音和眼神里都充满了溺爱，就像普罗小姐对待露西一样，甚至更甚。如果可能的话，他还会更加宠溺。拉里先生也是个美景，他戴着小假发，对这一切都报以满面的笑容，感谢命运在他的晚年为他指引了一份家园。但是，没有成百上千的人来看这场景，拉里先生等待普罗小姐预言的实现却等来了失望。

Miss Pross was a pleasant sight, albeit wild, and red, and grim, taking off her darling’s bonnet when she came up-stairs, and touching it up with the ends of her handkerchief, and blowing the dust off it, and folding her mantle ready for laying by, and smoothing her rich hair with as much pride as she could possibly have taken in her own hair if she had been the vainest and handsomest of women. Her darling was a pleasant sight too, embracing her and thanking her, and protesting against her taking so much trouble for her—which last she only dared to do playfully, or Miss Pross, sorely hurt, would have retired to her own chamber and cried. The Doctor was a pleasant sight too, looking on at them, and telling Miss Pross how she spoilt Lucie, in accents and with eyes that had as much spoiling in them as Miss Pross had, and would have had more if it were possible. Mr. Lorry was a pleasant sight too, beaming at all this in his little wig, and thanking his bachelor stars for having lighted him in his declining years to a Home. But, no Hundreds of people came to see the sights, and Mr. Lorry looked in vain for the fulfilment of Miss Pross’s prediction.

普罗丝小姐是一道令人愉快的景象，尽管她看起来很狂野、满脸通红、表情严肃。当她上楼时，她脱下她心爱的女儿的帽子，用手帕的角擦拭干净，吹去灰尘，然后把她的斗篷折好准备收起来，并且以一种只有最虚荣和最漂亮的女人对自己头发的自豪感才能相比的方式梳理着她那浓密的头发。她的心爱的女儿也是一个令人愉快的景象，拥抱着她并感谢她，并抗议她为自己付出这么多的麻烦——而且她只能开玩笑地这样做，否则普罗丝小姐会因伤心而退回到自己的房间里哭泣。医生也是一个令人愉快的景象，从旁观察着她们，并以充满溺爱的口吻和眼神告诉普罗丝小姐，她是如何宠坏露西的——他的口吻和眼神中有和普罗丝小姐一样多的溺爱之情，如果可能的话，他会更加溺爱。洛瑞先生也是一个令人愉快的景象，他戴着小假发，对这一切都报以微笑，并感谢他单身汉的命运，在他的晚年将他带到了一个家。但是，没有成百上千的人来看这些景象，洛瑞先生徒劳地寻找着普罗丝小姐的预言的实现。

source: “I don’t think I do like, if I may speak my mind. I am not fit to be pitted against you to-night. Is the teaboard still there, Lucie? I can’t see.”
domestication: 老实说，我感觉自己不是对手。你看我现在这状态，根本不适合跟你较量。露西，那个茶盘还在吗？我看不太清楚。
foreignization: “我不这么认为，如果我可以说出我的想法的话，我不适合在今晚和你作对。茶盘还在那里吗，露西？我看不到。”

source: He had been talking all day, on many subjects, and with unusual vivacity. “Pray, Doctor Manette,” said Mr. Darnay, as they sat under the plane-tree—and he said it in the natural pursuit of the topic in hand, which happened to be the old buildings of London—“have you seen much of the Tower?”
domestication: 他一整天都在侃侃而谈，话题五花八门，神采飞扬。他们坐在梧桐树下时，丹雷先生漫不经心地问道，“曼妮特医生，你去过伦敦塔吗？”其实他只是顺着话题问问，刚好他们正在讨论伦敦的老建筑。
foreignization: 他整天都在说话，讨论了很多话题，并且表现出非同寻常的生气勃勃。“请问，曼尼特博士，”当他们坐在梧桐树下时，达尔奈先生说，他这样说是自然而然地接着刚才的话题——碰巧刚才的话题是伦敦的旧建筑——“您是否看过很多伦敦塔？”

source: These occupations brought her round to the December month, wherein her father walked among the terrors with a steady head. On a lightly-snowing afternoon she arrived at the usual corner. It was a day of some wild rejoicing, and a festival. She had seen the houses, as she came along, decorated with little pikes, and with little red caps stuck upon them; also, with tricoloured ribbons; also, with the standard inscription (tricoloured letters were the favourite), Republic One and Indivisible. Liberty, Equality, Fraternity, or Death!
domestication: 这些日子很快就过去了，转眼就到了十二月份。她的父亲在那段艰难时期仍然表现得很坚强。有一天下午，雪花轻轻地飘落下来，她来到了平常的那个街角。今天是个狂欢的日子，大家都在庆祝节日。当她走过来的路上，看见很多房子都装饰着小小的旗帜和戴着红帽子的布娃娃，还有三色丝带，以及用三种颜色的字写的口号“共和国万岁，一个不可分割的国家”；或者是“自由、平等、博爱，否则就死吧！”
foreignization: 这些事情让她度过了十二月，这个月份里，她的父亲在恐怖统治中保持着镇定。有一天下午，雪花轻轻飘落，她来到了通常的拐角处。那是一个狂欢和节日的日子。当她走过时，她看到房子上装饰着小尖桩和小红帽；还有三色丝带；以及标语（三色字母是人们最喜欢的），不可分割的共和国！自由，平等，博爱，或者死亡！

source: He resorted to his pint of wine for consolation, drank it all in a few minutes, and fell asleep on his arms, with his hair straggling over the table, and a long winding-sheet in the candle dripping down upon him.
domestication: 他一时没了主意，就拿起酒瓶来解闷，一会儿工夫就把一瓶酒全喝完了，然后趴在桌子上睡着了，头发散乱地搭在桌上，蜡烛上滴下的烛泪像是一条白色的绞索慢慢地垂下来。
foreignization: 他求助于他的酒来安慰自己，几分钟之内就喝完了所有的酒，然后在桌子上睡着了，他的头发散乱地垂在桌子上，一根长长的蜡烛在滴落在他身上。

source: “Nevertheless,” pursued Darnay, rising to ring the bell, “there is nothing in that, I hope, to prevent my calling the reckoning, and our parting without ill-blood on either side.”
domestication: 尽管如此，达尼还是站起来去按门铃，他说：“我希望这不会妨碍我结账离开，你我也就此别过，两清了。”
foreignization: “然而，”达尼继续说着，站起来按铃，“但是我希望没有什么能够阻止我算账，并且我们双方没有恶意地分开。”

“Nevertheless,” pursued Darnay, rising to ring the bell, “there is nothing in that, I hope, to prevent my calling the reckoning, and our parting without ill-blood on either side.”
“然而，”达尼继续说着，站起来按铃，“但是我希望没有什么能够阻止我算账，并且我们双方没有恶意地分开。”

source: “I believe,” returned Doctor Manette, “that there had been a strong and extraordinary revival of the train of thought and remembrance that was the first cause of the malady. Some intense associations of a most distressing nature were vividly recalled, I think. It is probable that there had long been a dread lurking in his mind, that those associations would be recalled—say, under certain circumstances—say, on a particular occasion. He tried to prepare himself in vain; perhaps the effort to prepare himself made him less able to bear it.”
domestication: 曼尼特医生回答说：“我相信，这是因为那些旧事的记忆又重新浮现了，他以前的病情很可能就是因为这些记忆引起的。某些非常痛苦的回忆又重新变得鲜活起来了。他心里可能早就有个潜在的恐惧，就是怕这些痛苦的回忆会重新出现，比如在某种情况下或是在某个特定的场合。他想要做好准备，但可能是越想准备，反而越无法承受。”
foreignization: “我相信，”曼尼特医生回答说，“曾经有一次思绪和记忆的强烈和异常的复苏，这是疾病的第一个原因。我想，一些最痛苦性质的强烈联想被生动地回忆起来。可能他长期以来一直在他的脑海中潜伏着一种恐惧，害怕那些联想会被回忆起来——比如，在某种情况下——比如，在某个特定的场合。他徒劳地试图让自己做好准备；也许他试图让自己做好准备的努力使他变得更无法承受它。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I believe,” returned Doctor Manette, “that there had been a strong and extraordinary revival of the train of thought and remembrance that was the first cause of the malady. Some intense associations of a most distressing nature were vividly recalled, I think. It is probable that there had long been a dread lurking in his mind, that those associations would be recalled—say, under certain circumstances—say, on a particular occasion. He tried to prepare himself in vain; perhaps the effort to prepare himself made him less able to bear it.”
曼尼特医生回答说：“我相信，这是因为那些旧事的记忆又重新浮现了，他以前的病情很可能就是因为这些记忆引起的。某些非常痛苦的回忆又重新变得鲜活起来了。他心里可能早就有个潜在的恐惧，就是怕这些痛苦的回忆会重新出现，比如在某种情况下或是在某个特定的场合。他想要做好准备，但可能是越想准备，反而越无法承受。”

“I believe,” returned Doctor Manette, “that there had been a strong and extraordinary revival of the train of thought and remembrance that was the first cause of the malady. Some intense associations of a most distressing nature were vividly recalled, I think. It is probable that there had long been a dread lurking in his mind, that those associations would be recalled—say, under certain circumstances—say, on a particular occasion. He tried to prepare himself in vain; perhaps the effort to prepare himself made him less able to bear it.”
“我相信，”曼尼特医生回答说，“曾经有一次思绪和记忆的强烈和异常的复苏，这是疾病的第一个原因。我想，一些最痛苦性质的强烈联想被生动地回忆起来。可能他长期以来一直在他的脑海中潜伏着一种恐惧，害怕那些联想会被回忆起来——比如，在某种情况下——比如，在某个特定的场合。他徒劳地试图让自己做好准备；也许他试图让自己做好准备的努力使他变得更无法承受它。”

source: As the rider rattled down the hill again and through the street, the village was illuminating. The mender of roads, and the two hundred and fifty particular friends, inspired as one man and woman by the idea of lighting up, had darted into their houses, and were putting candles in every dull little pane of glass. The general scarcity of everything, occasioned candles to be borrowed in a rather peremptory manner of Monsieur Gabelle; and in a moment of reluctance and hesitation on that functionary’s part, the mender of roads, once so submissive to authority, had remarked that carriages were good to make bonfires with, and that post-horses would roast.
domestication: 骑手又一次从山上疾驰而下，穿过街道时，村庄里开始亮起了灯光。路边的修路工和其他两百五十个热心的朋友，被点亮灯笼的想法激发，立即冲进各自的家中，把蜡烛放在每一块灰暗的玻璃窗前。由于物资稀缺，大家都借来了蜡烛，有点强迫地让收税官加贝先生也借出了蜡烛。在加贝先生犹豫不决时，平日对权威很恭敬的修路工说了一句话：马车可以用来生火，邮政马也可以用来烤肉。
foreignization: 当骑手再次沿着山坡下来，穿过街道时，村庄被照亮了。路工和另外两百五十个朋友被点燃的想法所启发，像一个人和一个女人一样，冲进了他们的房子，并在每一块灰暗的小玻璃窗上点燃蜡烛。由于东西普遍匮乏，蜡烛是以一种相当命令的方式从伽贝先生那里借来的。在那位官员犹豫不决的时候，曾经对权威很奴颜卑膝的路工曾经说过，马车很适合做火堆，而且邮政马可以烤着吃。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As the rider rattled down the hill again and through the street, the village was illuminating. The mender of roads, and the two hundred and fifty particular friends, inspired as one man and woman by the idea of lighting up, had darted into their houses, and were putting candles in every dull little pane of glass. The general scarcity of everything, occasioned candles to be borrowed in a rather peremptory manner of Monsieur Gabelle; and in a moment of reluctance and hesitation on that functionary’s part, the mender of roads, once so submissive to authority, had remarked that carriages were good to make bonfires with, and that post-horses would roast.

The general scarcity of everything, occasioned candles to be borrowed in a rather peremptory manner of Monsieur Gabelle; and in a moment of reluctance and hesitation on that functionary’s part, the mender of roads, once so submissive to authority, had remarked that carriages were good to make bonfires with, and that post-horses would roast.
当骑手再次沿着山坡下来，穿过街道时，村庄被照亮了。路工和另外两百五十个朋友被点燃的想法所启发，像一个人和一个女人一样，冲进了他们的房子，并在每一块灰暗的小玻璃窗上点燃蜡烛。由于东西普遍匮乏，蜡烛是以一种相当命令的方式从伽贝先生那里借来的。在那位官员犹豫不决的时候，曾经对权威很奴颜卑膝的路工曾经说过，马车很适合做火堆，而且邮政马可以烤着吃。

source: “Well! Some whisper this, some whisper that; they speak of nothing else; even the fountain appears to fall to that tune. At length, on Sunday night when all the village is asleep, come soldiers, winding down from the prison, and their guns ring on the stones of the little street. Workmen dig, workmen hammer, soldiers laugh and sing; in the morning, by the fountain, there is raised a gallows forty feet high, poisoning the water.”
domestication: 唉，人们纷纷议论，有的传说这个，有的传说那个，没人再谈别的事儿，似乎连喷泉的水声也是在跟着这件事儿的调子走。终于，在星期天晚上，整个村庄都睡着了之后，士兵们从监狱里出来，沿着小路下来，他们的枪支碰撞在石头路面上发出叮当声。工人挖掘，工人敲打，士兵们欢笑歌唱；第二天早晨，在喷泉旁边，竟然搭起了一座四十英尺高的绞刑架，把喷泉的水都弄污染了。
foreignization: “好吧！ 有些人窃窃私语这样说，有些人窃窃私语那样说；他们除了这件事什么也不谈；甚至喷泉似乎也随着这种说法有节奏地流淌。终于，在星期日的晚上，当整个村庄都睡着的时候，士兵们来了，他们从监狱里蜿蜒而下，他们的枪支撞击着小街上的石头。工人们挖掘，工人们敲打，士兵们大笑和唱歌；在早晨，在喷泉旁边，竖起了一座四十英尺高的绞架，污染了水。”

source: A beautiful landscape, with the corn bright in it, but not abundant. Patches of poor rye where corn should have been, patches of poor peas and beans, patches of most coarse vegetable substitutes for wheat. On inanimate nature, as on the men and women who cultivated it, a prevalent tendency towards an appearance of vegetating unwillingly—a dejected disposition to give up, and wither away.
domestication: 这是一片风景如画的乡村，玉米田金黄一片，但是收成却不佳。在本该是玉米地的地方，长出了杂乱的黑麦；原本应该种豌豆和豆子的地方，却长出了最粗糙的替代品——土豆和萝卜等杂蔬。在静止的自然界中，就像在耕种土地的人们身上一样，到处都有一种无可奈何的萧条气息。仿佛大地和人都有一种想要放弃、枯萎的心态，随风而去，消失不见。
foreignization: 一个美丽的风景，里面有金黄色的玉米，但是并不丰富。那里应该种植玉米的地方却长满了贫瘠的黑麦，那里应该种植豌豆和豆子的地方同样长满了贫瘠的豌豆和豆子，还有大量最粗糙的蔬菜替代小麦。在无生命的自然界和在那些耕种它的人们身上，都有一种普遍的趋势，那就是呈现出一种不情愿生长的样子——一种沮丧的情绪，想要放弃并枯萎。

source: He opened it in the light of the coach-lamp on that side, and read—first to himself and then aloud: “‘Wait at Dover for Mam’selle.’ It’s not long, you see, guard. Jerry, say that my answer was, Recalled to life.”
domestication: 他拉开了车厢旁的灯，借着微弱的光线开始读信，先是自言自语，后来提高声音朗读：“‘在多佛等候小姐’。你看，警官，这条信息很简短。杰瑞，请转告他们，我回复的内容是——被召回人世。”

source: She looked so beautiful in the purity of her faith in this lost man, that her husband could have looked at her as she was for hours.
domestication: 她对这个失去方向的男人如此信任和坚信，这份信念让她显得非常美丽，以至于她的丈夫可以盯着她看上几个小时。

source: “Why, yes, of Beauvais. Like Monsieur Manette, your father, the gentleman was of Beauvais. Like Monsieur Manette, your father, the gentleman was of repute in Paris. I had the honour of knowing him there. Our relations were business relations, but confidential. I was at that time in our French House, and had been—oh! twenty years.”
domestication: “啊，对了，是从博韦来的人，就像你的父亲曼内先生一样，这位先生也是博韦人。他在巴黎也很有名，我曾经在那里认识他。我们之间的关系是生意上的，但又很亲密。我那时候在我们法国的分公司工作，已经干了二十年了。”
foreignization: “为什么，是的，来自博韦。像曼内先生，你的父亲，这位绅士来自博韦。像曼内先生，你的父亲，这位绅士在巴黎享有盛誉。我曾经有幸认识他，我们的关系是商务关系，但又很亲密。我当时在我们的法国分公司工作，并且已经——哦！二十年了。”

source: “And you in brown!” she said, indignantly turning to Mr. Lorry; “couldn’t you tell her what you had to tell her, without frightening her to death? Look at her, with her pretty pale face and her cold hands. Do you call that being a Banker?”
domestication: "你这个穿着棕色衣服的人，”她气呼呼地说，同时转向了洛里先生，“你非要吓唬她成这样吗？不能温和点告诉她吗？看看她，脸色这么苍白，手都凉得发抖，你这叫银行家吗？像你这样的大人物就不能有点涵养吗？”
foreignization: “你这身棕色的衣服！”她说着，愤怒地转向了洛里先生，“你不能告诉她你必须告诉她的事情，而不至于把她吓死吗？看看她，拥有那张漂亮的苍白脸庞和冰冷的手。你把这种样子叫做银行家？”

source: “To none. No, Miss Manette, to none. If you will hear me through a very little more, all you can ever do for me is done. I wish you to know that you have been the last dream of my soul. In my degradation I have not been so degraded but that the sight of you with your father, and of this home made such a home by you, has stirred old shadows that I thought had died out of me. Since I knew you, I have been troubled by a remorse that I thought would never reproach me again, and have heard whispers from old voices impelling me upward, that I thought were silent for ever. I have had unformed ideas of striving afresh, beginning anew, shaking off sloth and sensuality, and fighting out the abandoned fight. A dream, all a dream, that ends in nothing, and leaves the sleeper where he lay down, but I wish you to know that you inspired it.”
domestication: 这些话不是对任何人的，也不指望得到什么回报，曼妮特小姐。只要您肯听我说完，我就没有什么可求的了。我想让您知道，您一直是我灵魂深处最后的梦想。在我堕落的状态中，即使我已经到了最低谷，您与父亲在一起，以及您亲手打造的这个家的温暖场景，都让我心中那些以为已经熄灭的旧情怀重新燃起。自从遇见您后，我被一种以为永远不会再困扰我的愧疚感所困扰，并且听到来自过去的呼唤，让我想要奋发向上，而那些声音我以为早已沉寂。我的脑海里产生了一些想要重新开始，想要抖落懒惰和感官享受，想要继续我曾经放弃的战斗的念头。虽然这只是一场梦，最后还是什么也没有改变，只是让我回归到原点，但是我还是想让您知道，这一切都是您激发的。
foreignization: “对于没有人， 不，曼妮特小姐，对于没有人。如果你愿意再听我说一会儿，你已经为我做了你能做的一切。我希望你知道，你曾是我灵魂的最后一个梦想。在我的堕落中，我并没有堕落到如此地步，以至于你和你的父亲的身影，以及你让这个家成为家的景象，没有唤起我以为早已在我身上死去的旧影子。自从我认识你以来，我一直被一种我以为永远不会再责备我的愧疚感困扰，并且听到了我以为永远沉默了的旧声音在敦促我向上奋进。我有着重新努力、重新开始、摆脱懒惰和感官享乐、继续已经放弃的斗争的模糊想法。这是一个梦，全部都是梦，它以什么都没有结束，并且让睡眠的人躺在他躺下的地方，但我希望你知道，你激发了这个梦。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“To none. No, Miss Manette, to none. If you will hear me through a very little more, all you can ever do for me is done. I wish you to know that you have been the last dream of my soul. In my degradation I have not been so degraded but that the sight of you with your father, and of this home made such a home by you, has stirred old shadows that I thought had died out of me. Since I knew you, I have been troubled by a remorse that I thought would never reproach me again, and have heard whispers from old voices impelling me upward, that I thought were silent for ever. I have had unformed ideas of striving afresh, beginning anew, shaking off sloth and sensuality, and fighting out the abandoned fight. A dream, all a dream, that ends in nothing, and leaves the sleeper where he lay down, but I wish you to know that you inspired it.”

这些话不是对任何人的，也不指望得到什么回报，曼妮特小姐。只要您肯听我说完，我就没有什么可求的了。我想让您知道，您一直是我灵魂深处最后的梦想。在我堕落的状态中，即使我已经到了最低谷，您与父亲在一起，以及您亲手打造的这个家的温暖场景，都让我心中那些以为已经熄灭的旧情怀重新燃起。自从遇见您后，我被一种以为永远不会再困扰我的愧疚感所困扰，并且听到来自过去的呼唤，让我想要奋发向上，而那些声音我以为早已沉寂。我的脑海里产生了一些想要重新开始，想要抖落懒惰和感官享受，想要继续我曾经放弃的战斗的念头。虽然这只是一场梦，最后还是什么也没有改变，只是让我回归到原点，但是我还是想让您知道，这一切都是您激发的。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“To none. No, Miss Manette, to none. If you will hear me through a very little more, all you can ever do for me is done. I wish you to know that you have been the last dream of my soul. In my degradation I have not been so degraded but that the sight of you with your father, and of this home made such a home by you, has stirred old shadows that I thought had died out of me. Since I knew you, I have been troubled by a remorse that I thought would never reproach me again, and have heard whispers from old voices impelling me upward, that I thought were silent for ever. I have had unformed ideas of striving afresh, beginning anew, shaking off sloth and sensuality, and fighting out the abandoned fight. A dream, all a dream, that ends in nothing, and leaves the sleeper where he lay down, but I wish you to know that you inspired it.”

“对于没有人， 不，曼妮特小姐，对于没有人。如果你愿意再听我说一会儿，你已经为我做了你能做的一切。我希望你知道，你曾是我灵魂的最后一个梦想。在我的堕落中，我并没有堕落到如此地步，以至于你和你的父亲的身影，以及你让这个家成为家的景象，没有唤起我以为早已在我身上死去的旧影子。自从我认识你以来，我一直被一种我以为永远不会再责备我的愧疚感困扰，并且听到了我以为永远沉默了的旧声音在敦促我向上奋进。我有着重新努力、重新开始、摆脱懒惰和感官享乐、继续已经放弃的斗争的模糊想法。这是一个梦，全部都是梦，它以什么都没有结束，并且让睡眠的人躺在他躺下的地方，但我希望你知道，你激发了这个梦。”

source: “These fools know nothing. While they despise your breath, and would stop it for ever and ever, in you or in a hundred like you rather than in one of their own horses or dogs, they only know what your breath tells them. Let it deceive them, then, a little longer; it cannot deceive them too much.”
domestication: 这些傻瓜什么都不懂，他们鄙视你的生命，宁愿你和跟你一样的百个人都死掉，也不愿让他们一匹马或一只狗受到伤害。他们只知道听你说些什么，那就让他们再蒙在鼓里一些时候吧，反正他们也不会醒悟过来。
foreignization: “这些傻瓜什么都不懂。他们鄙视你的气息，并且愿意永远、永远地阻止它，无论是在你身上还是在一百个像你的人身上，而不是在他们自己的马或狗身上，但他们只知道你的气息告诉他们的东西。那么，就让它再欺骗他们一会儿吧；它不能欺骗他们太多。”

source: With a weary sound that was not a sigh, nor a groan, he bent to work again, until the silence was again broken.
domestication: 他发出了一种疲惫的声音，不像是叹息，也不像呻吟，重新开始工作，直到寂静再次被打破。或者说，他又恢复了工作，发出一种难以形容的闷声，不是叹息，也不是呻吟，反正那声音听起来挺累的。
foreignization: 他发出了一种疲惫的声响，这声音不是叹息，也不是呻吟，他再次弯下腰回到工作中，直到寂静再次被打破。

source: He was an inquisitive fellow, and sometimes when she had quite forgotten him in gazing at the prison roof and grates, and in lifting her heart up to her husband, she would come to herself to find him looking at her, with his knee on his bench and his saw stopped in its work. “But it’s not my business!” he would generally say at those times, and would briskly fall to his sawing again.
domestication: 这个家伙很爱打听，有时她忘了他的存在，眼睛盯着监狱的屋顶和铁栏，心中想着她的丈夫。这时她突然回过神来，发现他正盯着她看，他的膝盖抵着长凳，锯子停在半空中。“反正都与我无关！”他通常会这样说完，就会继续他的工作，专心地锯着。
foreignization: 他是一个好奇的人，有时当她完全忘记了他，凝视着监狱的屋顶和格子窗，并将她的心情提升到她的丈夫时，她会突然发现他正在看着她，他的膝盖放在他的长凳上，他的锯子停止了工作。“但这不关我的事！”他通常会在那些时候说，并会很快地回到他的锯木工作中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: Defarge looked gloomily at his wife, and gave no other answer than a gruff sound of acquiescence.
domestication: 德法尔热阴着脸看了妻子一眼，除了一个粗暴的表示同意的声音之外，什么也没说。
foreignization: 德法尔热阴郁地看着他的妻子，并且除了一个粗鲁的应允声之外没有做出其他回答。

source: The prisoner had got into a coach, and his daughter had followed him, when Mr. Lorry’s feet were arrested on the step by his asking, miserably, for his shoemaking tools and the unfinished shoes. Madame Defarge immediately called to her husband that she would get them, and went, knitting, out of the lamplight, through the courtyard. She quickly brought them down and handed them in;—and immediately afterwards leaned against the door-post, knitting, and saw nothing.
domestication: 囚犯已经坐进了一辆马车，他的女儿也跟着上了车，就在这时，劳瑞先生的脚刚刚踏上门阶，却被他自己的问题阻止了。他很可怜地向人打听他的鞋匠工具和未完成的鞋子在哪里。德法尔热夫人立即招呼她的丈夫说她去取来，于是她拎着针织活儿走出了灯光照不到的地方，穿过院子。接着她快速地拿来了工具和鞋子，并且递给了他们；就在这之后，她靠在门柱上继续她的针织活儿，看起来完全不知道发生了什么事。
foreignization: 那犯人已经上了马车，他的女儿也跟着他上了车，当时劳瑞先生的脚正踏在车门上，犯人可怜地请求取回他的鞋匠工具和未完成的鞋子。德法尔热夫人立即呼唤她的丈夫，说她会去取来这些东西，然后带着织针走出了灯光照亮的地方，穿过庭院。她很快就拿来了这些东西并递了进去；——然后马上倚靠在门柱上，织着毛衣，看不到任何东西。

She quickly brought them down and handed them in;—and immediately afterwards leaned against the door-post, knitting, and saw nothing.

source: “Courage, dear miss! Courage! Business! The worst will be over in a moment; it is but passing the room-door, and the worst is over. Then, all the good you bring to him, all the relief, all the happiness you bring to him, begin. Let our good friend here, assist you on that side. That’s well, friend Defarge. Come, now. Business, business!”
domestication: “小姐，您要勇敢啊！事情很快就结束了，光是穿过门口就 xong了，最难的部分就过去了。到了那边，您能带给他的安慰、幸福，才正是刚刚开始嘛。让我们的好朋友在那边协助您吧，没问题的，德法尔热先生。来吧，现在该办正事了，大家都要一起努力啊！”
foreignization: “鼓起勇气吧，小姐！鼓起勇气！办正事吧！最糟糕的部分马上就会过去；只是穿过房间的门， 最糟糕的部分就会过去。然后，你带给他的所有好处，你带给他的所有安慰，你带给他的所有幸福，就会开始。让我们的好朋友在另一边帮助你。那很好，德法尔热先生。来吧，现在。办正事，办正事！”

source: “As to me, the greatest desire I have, is to forget that I belong to it. It has no good in it for me—except wine like this—nor I for it. So we are not much alike in that particular. Indeed, I begin to think we are not much alike in any particular, you and I.”
domestication: 说到我自己，我最大的心愿就是忘掉我和它的关系。因为和它扯上关系对我来说没有任何好处，除了像这样的美酒之外——当然，它也没什么需要我做的。我觉得我们俩在这方面挺不一样的。事实上，我越来越觉得你我之间根本就没什么共同点。
foreignization: 至于我，我最大的愿望是忘记我属于它。 它对我没有任何好处——除了像这样的美酒——我也没有什么可以给予它。 因此，在这一点上，我们并没有太多的相似之处。 的确，我开始认为我们在任何方面都没有太多的相似之处，你和我。

source: Rounding his mouth and both his eyes, as he stepped backward from the table, the waiter shifted his napkin from his right arm to his left, dropped into a comfortable attitude, and stood surveying the guest while he ate and drank, as from an observatory or watchtower. According to the immemorial usage of waiters in all ages.
domestication: 他把嘴巴和眼睛都圆了起来，退离餐桌的同时，把手臂上的餐巾从右边移到左边，轻松地站好，开始观察客人吃喝的样子，就像站在瞭望台上监视一样。这种做法是各个时代服务员的不成文规则。
foreignization: 当他从桌子旁退后一步时，侍者把嘴巴和两只眼睛都凸了出来，他把餐巾从右臂移到左臂，放松地站着，带着一种舒适的姿态，观察客人吃喝的样子，就像是在一个观测台或是望楼上一样。按照所有时代以来服务员的不变惯例。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Rounding his mouth and both his eyes, as he stepped backward from the table, the waiter shifted his napkin from his right arm to his left, dropped into a comfortable attitude, and stood surveying the guest while he ate and drank, as from an observatory or watchtower. According to the immemorial usage of waiters in all ages.
他把嘴巴和眼睛都圆了起来，退离餐桌的同时，把手臂上的餐巾从右边移到左边，轻松地站好，开始观察客人吃喝的样子，就像站在瞭望台上监视一样。这种做法是各个时代服务员的不成文规则。

Rounding his mouth and both his eyes, as he stepped backward from the table, the waiter shifted his napkin from his right arm to his left, dropped into a comfortable attitude, and stood surveying the guest while he ate and drank, as from an observatory or watchtower. According to the immemorial usage of waiters in all ages.
当他从桌子旁退后一步时，侍者把嘴巴和两只眼睛都凸了出来，他把餐巾从右臂移到左臂，放松地站着，带着一种舒适的姿态，观察客人吃喝的样子，就像是在一个观测台或是望楼上一样。按照所有时代以来服务员的不变惯例。

source: They turned into the wine-shop, which was closed (for it was midnight), and where Madame Defarge immediately took her post at her desk, counted the small moneys that had been taken during her absence, examined the stock, went through the entries in the book, made other entries of her own, checked the serving man in every possible way, and finally dismissed him to bed. Then she turned out the contents of the bowl of money for the second time, and began knotting them up in her handkerchief, in a chain of separate knots, for safe keeping through the night. All this while, Defarge, with his pipe in his mouth, walked up and down, complacently admiring, but never interfering; in which condition, indeed, as to the business and his domestic affairs, he walked up and down through life.
domestication: 他们走进了酒馆，门已关上，因为已是午夜。德法盖夫人立即回到她在柜台后面的位置，清点她离开期间收到的钱，检查存货，查看账目，记下新的交易，彻底检查服务员的工作，最后让他去睡觉。这时，她第二次倒出碗里的钱，然后开始用手帕将钱一一打结，形成一串独立的结，以确保夜间的安全。与此同时，德法盖先生嘴里叼着烟斗，在房间里来回踱步，心满意足地看着妻子忙碌，却从不插手干预，这其实也算是他一贯的作风，不论对待生意还是家庭事务，他总是这样自得其乐地过日子。
foreignization: 他们转身进入酒馆，这时酒馆已经关门了（因为是午夜），在那里德法尔热夫人立即坐在她的桌子旁，清点她不在期间收取的零钱，检查库存，查看账簿上的记录，添加自己的记录，用各种方法核对服务员的工作，最后让他回床睡觉。接着，她第二次倒出碗里的钱，开始用手帕将它们打成一系列单独的结，以便在夜间安全保管。与此同时，戴法尔热把烟斗放在嘴里，上下踱步，心满意足地看着，但从不干预；实际上，对于他的生意和家务，他就是这样在人生中漫步的状态。

Then she turned out the contents of the bowl of money for the second time, and began knotting them up in her handkerchief, in a chain of separate knots, for safe keeping through the night. All this while, Defarge, with his pipe in his mouth, walked up and down, complacently admiring, but never interfering; in which condition, indeed, as to the business and his domestic affairs, he walked up and down through life.

source: “Well! To the best of my understanding, and bad’s the best, you’ll tell me,” said Miss Pross, softened by the tone of the apology, “he is afraid of the whole subject.”
domestication: “好吧！据我所知，不過這已經是最好了，你就告诉我吧，”普洛小姐说，她被那语气亲切的道歉话语所感动，“他其实是害怕整个事情。”她接着又說：“这下子我總算明白了，他對這件事很緊張，其實是害怕得要命！”

source: “If it was a light answer,” returned Darnay, “I beg your forgiveness for it. I had no other object than to turn a slight thing, which, to my surprise, seems to trouble you too much, aside. I declare to you, on the faith of a gentleman, that I have long dismissed it from my mind. Good Heaven, what was there to dismiss! Have I had nothing more important to remember, in the great service you rendered me that day?”
domestication: “如果我的回答太轻率了，”丹雷说，“我在这里向你道歉。我当时只是想转移一下话题，因为这件事看起来对你来说很重要，而我事先并没有意识到这一点。我以一个gentleman的名誉起誓，这件事情我早就抛之脑后了。老天爷啊，哪里有什么值得记住的呢？难道我那天忘了更重要的事情，比如你给我帮的忙？”
foreignization: “如果这是一个轻率的回答，”丹雷 trả lời说，“我为此请求你的原谅。我没有其他目的，只是想把这个微不足道的事情，令我惊讶的是，它似乎也太过困扰你，放在一边。我以绅士的信誉向你发誓，我早已把它从我的脑海中抹去。天啊，到底有什么值得抹去的呢？在你那天给我提供的大恩大德中，我难道没有更重要的事情需要记住吗？”

messages_domestication.content:

“If it was a light answer,” returned Darnay, “I beg your forgiveness for it. I had no other object than to turn a slight thing, which, to my surprise, seems to trouble you too much, aside. Have I had nothing more important to remember, in the great service you rendered me that day?”

source: “It wasn’t ending it, I suppose? I say, when you began it, it was hard enough; not that I have any fault to find with Doctor Manette, except that he is not worthy of such a daughter, which is no imputation on him, for it was not to be expected that anybody should be, under any circumstances. But it really is doubly and trebly hard to have crowds and multitudes of people turning up after him (I could have forgiven him), to take Ladybird’s affections away from me.”
domestication: 不会是结束了吧？我想，当你开始的时候就已经很难了。不算我对曼尼特医生有什么意见，不过他配不上这么好的女儿，这也不能怪他，因为没有人能配得上她。但真正让我难受的是，像他这样的男人竟然能吸引那么多人的注意力，抢走了Ladybird的感情。如果只有他一个人，我或许还能容忍，但实在是让一大群人都来跟我争夺她的心，这让我感到十分沮丧。
foreignization: “这并不是结束吧？我说，当你开始的时候已经很难了；并非我对曼内特博士有任何怨言，只是他配不上这样的一个女儿，不过这也不能怪他，因为在任何情况下都不会有人配得上她。但真正让人难过的是，在他之后还有成群的人（我本可以原谅他），却来抢走Ladybird对我的感情。”

source: “Rot the admiration of the whole Court! Who made the Old Bailey a judge of beauty? She was a golden-haired doll!”
domestication: 算了吧，满朝的称赞！谁让老贝利法庭来当评美的仲裁者？她只不过是个金发美人娃娃罢了！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Rot the admiration of the whole Court! Who made the Old Bailey a judge of beauty? She was a golden-haired doll!”
算了吧，满朝的称赞！谁让老贝利法庭来当评美的仲裁者？她只不过是个金发美人娃娃罢了！

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Rot the admiration of the whole Court! Who made the Old Bailey a judge of beauty? She was a golden-haired doll!”
“全 courtroom 的仰慕都枯萎吧！谁让老贝利成为美丽的裁判？她是一个金发的娃娃！ ”

source: From that time, in all weathers, she waited there two hours. As the clock struck two, she was there, and at four she turned resignedly away. When it was not too wet or inclement for her child to be with her, they went together; at other times she was alone; but, she never missed a single day.
domestication: 从那以后，无论刮风下雨还是烈日当头，她每天都会在那里等待两个小时。只要钟声一敲两点，她准时出现，四点钟她便悄然离去。天气好的时候，她会带上孩子一起去，如果下雨或者风太大，她就独自前去，但有一点可以肯定：她绝不会缺席。
foreignization: 从那时起，无论天气如何，她都会在那里等待两个小时。钟声敲响两点时，她就在那里，四点时她带着无奈转身离开。当天气不是太湿或者不适宜孩子陪伴她时，他们会一起去；其他时候，她一个人去；但是，她没有错过一天。

source: Rather wishing his modest adjective away, Mr. Lorry replied, “No, no, no. Surely not. To return to business:—Is it not remarkable that Doctor Manette, unquestionably innocent of any crime as we are all well assured he is, should never touch upon that question? I will not say with me, though he had business relations with me many years ago, and we are now intimate; I will say with the fair daughter to whom he is so devotedly attached, and who is so devotedly attached to him? Believe me, Miss Pross, I don’t approach the topic with you, out of curiosity, but out of zealous interest.”
domestication: 洛里先生倒是巴不得把这些不必要的客套话免掉，他赶忙接口说：“不，不，不。绝对不会的。还是谈正事吧：马内特医生无疑是无辜的，我们都很清楚这一点，他怎么从来都不提起这件事呢？我不说跟我，因为他很多年前和我有过生意来往，现在我们关系很密切；我说的是跟他非常疼爱的漂亮女儿，还有她对他的深情。相信我，普罗斯小姐，我不是出于好奇心跟你讨论这个问题，而是出于热切的关心。”
foreignization: 宁愿让他自己的谦逊的形容词消失，罗瑞先生回答道：“不，不，不。一定不是这样。回到正题：曼尼特博士无疑是无辜的，我们都非常清楚这一点，他从来没有涉及过那个问题，这不是很值得注意吗？我不会说我和他有关系，尽管他多年前曾经和我有商业往来，现在我们关系亲密；我会说他和他的亲爱的女儿有关系，她非常专心地与他相连，他也非常专心地与她相连？相信我，普罗斯小姐，我不是出于好奇心，而是出于热情的兴趣来和你讨论这个话题。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Rather wishing his modest adjective away, Mr. Lorry replied, “No, no, no. Surely not. To return to business:—Is it not remarkable that Doctor Manette, unquestionably innocent of any crime as we are all well assured he is, should never touch upon that question? I will not say with me, though he had business relations with me many years ago, and we are now intimate; I will say with the fair daughter to whom he is so devotedly attached, and who is so devotedly attached to him? Believe me, Miss Pross, I don’t approach the topic with you, out of curiosity, but out of zealous interest.”
洛里先生倒是巴不得把这些不必要的客套话免掉，他赶忙接口说：“不，不，不。绝对不会的。还是谈正事吧：马内特医生无疑是无辜的，我们都很清楚这一点，他怎么从来都不提起这件事呢？我不说跟我，因为他很多年前和我有过生意来往，现在我们关系很密切；我说的是跟他非常疼爱的漂亮女儿，还有她对他的深情。相信我，普罗斯小姐，我不是出于好奇心跟你讨论这个问题，而是出于热切的关心。”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Rather wishing his modest adjective away, Mr. Lorry replied, “No, no, no. Surely not. To return to business:—Is it not remarkable that Doctor Manette, unquestionably innocent of any crime as we are all well assured he is, should never touch upon that question? I will not say with me, though he had business relations with me many years ago, and we are now intimate; I will say with the fair daughter to whom he is so devotedly attached, and who is so devotedly attached to him? Believe me, Miss Pross, I don’t approach the topic with you, out of curiosity, but out of zealous interest.”

宁愿让他自己的谦逊的形容词消失，罗瑞先生回答道：“不，不，不。一定不是这样。回到正题：曼尼特博士无疑是无辜的，我们都非常清楚这一点，他从来没有涉及过那个问题，这不是很值得注意吗？我不会说我和他有关系，尽管他多年前曾经和我有商业往来，现在我们关系亲密；我会说他和他的亲爱的女儿有关系，她非常专心地与他相连，他也非常专心地与她相连？相信我，普罗斯小姐，我不是出于好奇心，而是出于热情的兴趣来和你讨论这个话题。

source: “But it is very strange—now, at least, is it not very strange”—said Defarge, rather pleading with his wife to induce her to admit it, “that, after all our sympathy for Monsieur her father, and herself, her husband’s name should be proscribed under your hand at this moment, by the side of that infernal dog’s who has just left us?”
domestication: "但是这事儿真的是很奇怪——至少现在是这样，不是吗？”德法尔热说，他几乎是在央求妻子承认这一点，“毕竟我们一直同情她的父亲和她自己，她丈夫的名字居然在这时被你亲手写上黑名单，而这个名单上还有刚刚离开我们的那个糟糕透顶的小子的名字？”
foreignization: “但这很奇怪——至少现在是这样，不是吗？”——德法尔热带着恳求的眼神对他的妻子说，想要诱使她承认这一点，“尽管我们一直同情她父亲和她自己，以及她的丈夫，但她的丈夫的名字却在这个时候被你亲手写下，被写在我们刚刚离开的那个该死的家伙旁边，这难道不是很奇怪吗？”

source: “I am that child, I hope, my father. O my dear, my dear, will you bless me as fervently to-morrow?”
domestication: “我就是那个孩子，我希望，我的父亲啊。哦，我的亲爱的，我亲爱的，你会不会明天也同样地给我祝福呢？”
foreignization: “我就是那孩子，我希望，我的父亲。噢，我的亲爱的，我的亲爱的，你明天会那样热切地祝福我吗？”

source: “I think so. But it is, as I have told you, next to impossible. I even believe it—in some cases—to be quite impossible.”
domestication: “我想应该是这样。但是，就像我之前跟你说的，这种事情基本上是不可能发生的。我甚至觉得有些情况下根本就不可能。”
foreignization: “我想是这样的。但是，正如我告诉你的，它简直是几乎不可能的。我甚至相信它——在某些情况下——根本是不可能的。”

source: The fountain was a little removed; for the street opened, where it was, into a space some ten or twelve yards square. As the tall man suddenly got up from the ground, and came running at the carriage, Monsieur the Marquis clapped his hand for an instant on his sword-hilt.
domestication: 喷泉位于一片开阔地上，大约十几平方码大小，原本在那里的街道一下子就拓宽了。当那个高个子男人突然从地上跳起来，朝马车跑来的时候，马尔基斯先生手一挥，瞬间握住了剑柄。
foreignization: 喷泉离此稍远了一点；因为街道在那里开阔成一个大约十到十二码见方的广场。当高个子男人突然从地上站起来，朝马车奔来时，马基斯先生马上把手按在剑柄上一瞬间。

source: “I am not frightened for myself, my father. But when I think of my husband, and the mercies of these people—”
domestication: “我自己不怕，父亲，但是我一想到我的丈夫，以及这些人的行为——”
foreignization: “我并不为自己担心，父亲。但当我想到我的丈夫，以及这些人可能施加的……”

source: So cowed was their condition, and so long and hard their experience of what such a man could do to them, within the law and beyond it, that not a voice, or a hand, or even an eye was raised. Among the men, not one. But the woman who stood knitting looked up steadily, and looked the Marquis in the face. It was not for his dignity to notice it; his contemptuous eyes passed over her, and over all the other rats; and he leaned back in his seat again, and gave the word “Go on!”
domestication: 他们的处境太过悲惨了，长期以来一直在忍受着这样的人的欺凌，无论是在法律范围内还是之外，所以没有一个人敢吱声，连眼神都不敢接触。但是，那位拿着针织活的女人，却抬起头来，直视着侯爵的脸。当然，这不在他的注意之内，他带有鄙视的眼睛扫过她和其他人，然后又靠回座位上，挥手说：“继续吧！”
foreignization: 他们的处境如此低下，且长期以来一直体验着这样一个人在法律之内和之外可以对他们做什么， 以至于没有一个人发出声音，或举起手，甚至没有人抬眼看他。在这些男人中，没有一个人。但是，那个站在那里纺织的女人，却始终抬头看着，凝视着马基斯的脸。这不是他注意她的时候；他轻蔑的眼睛扫过她和其他所有的人，然后他又靠回座位，并说出“继续！”这个词。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

So cowed was their condition, and so long and hard their experience of what such a man could do to them, within the law and beyond it, that not a voice, or a hand, or even an eye was raised. Among the men, not one. But the woman who stood knitting looked up steadily, and looked the Marquis in the face. It was not for his dignity to notice it; his contemptuous eyes passed over her, and over all the other rats; and he leaned back in his seat again, and gave the word “Go on!”
他们的处境太过悲惨了，长期以来一直在忍受着这样的人的欺凌，无论是在法律范围内还是之外，所以没有一个人敢吱声，连眼神都不敢接触。但是，那位拿着针织活的女人，却抬起头来，直视着侯爵的脸。当然，这不在他的注意之内，他带有鄙视的眼睛扫过她和其他人，然后又靠回座位上，挥手说：“继续吧！”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Among the men, not one. But the woman who stood knitting looked up steadily, and looked the Marquis in the face. It was not for his dignity to notice it; his contemptuous eyes passed over her, and over all the other rats; and he leaned back in his seat again, and gave the word “Go on!”

source: One year and three months. During all that time Lucie was never sure, from hour to hour, but that the Guillotine would strike off her husband’s head next day. Every day, through the stony streets, the tumbrils now jolted heavily, filled with Condemned. Lovely girls; bright women, brown-haired, black-haired, and grey; youths; stalwart men and old; gentle born and peasant born; all red wine for La Guillotine, all daily brought into light from the dark cellars of the loathsome prisons, and carried to her through the streets to slake her devouring thirst. Liberty, equality, fraternity, or death;—the last, much the easiest to bestow, O Guillotine!
domestication: 一年又三个月，卢西每时每刻都在担心丈夫会不会明天就被断头台砍掉脑袋。当时巴黎街头到处都是运载囚犯的马车，日日夜夜都有漂亮的姑娘，美丽的女人，不管是棕发还是黑发还是灰发，年轻人，壮汉，老人，不管是贵族出身还是平民出身，都被送上断头台，如同美酒一般献给了断头台。口号喊的是自由，平等，博爱，但最容易做到的却是死，哦，断头台，你真是一个轻易赐予死亡的凶器啊！
foreignization: 一年零三个月。在这段时间里，露西从来没有一刻确定，法兰西式断头台不会在第二天砍下她丈夫的头。每一天，从石头街道上，刑车现在隆隆地行驶，里面装满了被判刑的人。美丽的姑娘们；明艳的女人，有棕色头发、黑色头发和灰色头发的；年轻人；强壮的男人和老人；出身贵族的和农民出身的，都成了断头台上的祭品，每天从可憎的监狱黑暗的地下室里被带到光亮中，然后通过街道运给断头台，以解除它的渴望。自由，平等，博爱，或者死亡；——最后一个，对你来说，哦，断头台，是最容易赐予的！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

One year and three months. During all that time Lucie was never sure, from hour to hour, but that the Guillotine would strike off her husband’s head next day. Every day, through the stony streets, the tumbrils now jolted heavily, filled with Condemned. Lovely girls; bright women, brown-haired, black-haired, and grey; youths; stalwart men and old; gentle born and peasant born; all red wine for La Guillotine, all daily brought into light from the dark cellars of the loathsome prisons, and carried to her through the streets to slake her devouring thirst. Liberty, equality, fraternity, or death;—the last, much the easiest to bestow, O Guillotine!

Translate the following text to Chinese.

One year and three months. During all that time Lucie was never sure, from hour to hour, but that the Guillotine would strike off her husband’s head next day. Every day, through the stony streets, the tumbrils now jolted heavily, filled with Condemned. Lovely girls; bright women, brown-haired, black-haired, and grey; youths; stalwart men and old; gentle born and peasant born; all red wine for La Guillotine, all daily brought into light from the dark cellars of the loathsome prisons, and carried to her through the streets to slake her devouring thirst. Liberty, equality, fraternity, or death;—the last, much the easiest to bestow, O Guillotine!

一年零三个月。在这段时间里，露西从来没有一刻确定，法兰西式断头台不会在第二天砍下她丈夫的头。每一天，从石头街道上，刑车现在隆隆地行驶，里面装满了被判刑的人。美丽的姑娘们；明艳的女人，有棕色头发、黑色头发和灰色头发的；年轻人；强壮的男人和老人；出身贵族的和农民出身的，都成了断头台上的祭品，每天从可憎的监狱黑暗的地下室里被带到光亮中，然后通过街道运给断头台，以解除它的渴望。自由，平等，博爱，或者死亡；——最后一个，对你来说，哦，断头台，是最容易赐予的！

source: “No. That was another thing. It stood before my disturbed sense of sight, but it never moved. The phantom that my mind pursued, was another and more real child. Of her outward appearance I know no more than that she was like her mother. The other had that likeness too—as you have—but was not the same. Can you follow me, Lucie? Hardly, I think? I doubt you must have been a solitary prisoner to understand these perplexed distinctions.”
domestication: "不，那是另一回事。它一直站在我混乱的视线前，但从未移动。我的脑海中一直在追寻的那个幽灵，是另一个更真实的孩子。至于她的外貌，我只知道她长得像她母亲。另一个孩子也长得像，但却不是同一个人。你能明白我说的吗，露西？我觉得你很难理解。要想明白这些令人困惑的区别，我想你必须曾经是一个孤独的囚徒才行。”
foreignization: “不。那是另一回事了。在我的视觉混乱之中，它就站在我的面前，但它从未移动。我的心思所追求的幽灵，是另一个更加真实的孩子。至于她的外貌，我所知道的不比她与她母亲相像更多。而那个孩子也具有这种相似之处——就像你一样——但并非同一个人。你能明白我在说什么，露西吗？恐怕不能吧，我想你也许得像我一样成为孤独的囚徒，才能理解这些混乱的区别。”

source: “I doubt, sir,” returned the nephew, “whether, if it had carried me to the utmost brink of death, you would have cared to stop me there.”
domestication: “我怀疑，即使它会让我死到临头，你也不会在乎，更别说出手阻止了，”侄子反驳道。

source: He stared at her with a fearful look, and after a while his lips began to form some words, though no sound proceeded from them. By degrees, in the pauses of his quick and laboured breathing, he was heard to say:

source: The night was so very sultry, that although they sat with doors and windows open, they were overpowered by heat. When the tea-table was done with, they all moved to one of the windows, and looked out into the heavy twilight. Lucie sat by her father; Darnay sat beside her; Carton leaned against a window. The curtains were long and white, and some of the thunder-gusts that whirled into the corner, caught them up to the ceiling, and waved them like spectral wings.
domestication: 那是一个闷热的夜晚，尽管他们打开了门窗，仍然被热浪所笼罩。当茶点结束后，他们都走到一扇窗前，凝视着浓重的黄昏。露西坐在她父亲旁边，丹尼坐到了她的身边，卡顿则靠在窗户上。长长的白色窗帘在风中飘动，有时甚至被阵风卷起到天花板上，像幽灵般的翅膀般舞动。
foreignization: 那晚的天气非常闷热，尽管他们打开门窗而坐，还是被热浪所笼罩。当茶桌上的一切都结束以后，他们都走到窗前，朝着浓厚的暮色望出去。露西坐在她父亲身边；丹雷坐在她旁边；卡顿斜靠在一扇窗户上。窗帘又长又白，有些卷入窗角的雷暴风，把它们卷到了天花板上，在那里像幽灵般的翅膀一样飘荡。

source: It sheared off heads so many, that it, and the ground it most polluted, were a rotten red. It was taken to pieces, like a toy-puzzle for a young Devil, and was put together again when the occasion wanted it. It hushed the eloquent, struck down the powerful, abolished the beautiful and good. Twenty-two friends of high public mark, twenty-one living and one dead, it had lopped the heads off, in one morning, in as many minutes. The name of the strong man of Old Scripture had descended to the chief functionary who worked it; but, so armed, he was stronger than his namesake, and blinder, and tore away the gates of God’s own Temple every day.
domestication: 这东西砍下了无数人的头，连带着它经常“光顾”的地方，都变得血红一片。就好像一个给小鬼玩的拼图一样，它被拆开又被重新拼凑起来，只等待着时机。它让那些能言善辩的人闭嘴，让强大的统治者倒台，毁灭了一切美好的事物。一天早晨的时间里，就有二十二个社会名流，二十一个活的，一个死的，全部被它砍掉了头。这东西的主要操作者，被人称为圣经中那个力量强大的家伙，但他却比原版更有力，也更盲目，每天都在拆毁上帝庙里的门。
foreignization: 它砍掉了这么多头，以至于它和它最严重污染的地面，都变成了腐烂的红色。它被拆开了，就像一个年轻的魔鬼的玩具谜题，并在需要的时候再重新组装起来。它使有口才的人沉默，击倒了强大的人，消灭了美好的事物。二十二个备受瞩目的朋友，二十一个活着的一个死了，它在一分钟内，就在一个早晨，砍掉了他们所有人的头。《旧约》中强人名字的传承到了操作它的首席官员，但他虽然武装精良，却比他的同名人更强大，也更盲目，每天都撕裂着上帝自己神庙的大门。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It sheared off heads so many, that it, and the ground it most polluted, were a rotten red. It was taken to pieces, like a toy-puzzle for a young Devil, and was put together again when the occasion wanted it. It hushed the eloquent, struck down the powerful, abolished the beautiful and good. Twenty-two friends of high public mark, twenty-one living and one dead, it had lopped the heads off, in one morning, in as many minutes. The name of the strong man of Old Scripture had descended to the chief functionary who worked it; but, so armed, he was stronger than his namesake, and blinder, and tore away the gates of God’s own Temple every day.

source: The happiness of his own chosen English home, the necessity of being always actively employed, the swift changes and troubles of the time which had followed on one another so fast, that the events of this week annihilated the immature plans of last week, and the events of the week following made all new again; he knew very well, that to the force of these circumstances he had yielded:—not without disquiet, but still without continuous and accumulating resistance. That he had watched the times for a time of action, and that they had shifted and struggled until the time had gone by, and the nobility were trooping from France by every highway and byway, and their property was in course of confiscation and destruction, and their very names were blotting out, was as well known to himself as it could be to any new authority in France that might impeach him for it.
domestication: 他自己选择的英格兰家园的幸福，总是需要积极工作的必要性，以及时间的迅速变化和困扰，这些都让他应接不暇。每周的事件都能抹去上周的计划，而下周的事件又会让一切重新开始。他很清楚，他已经向这些情况屈服了，尽管心中有些不安，但还没有到持续抵抗的地步。他也知道自己曾经等待过行动的时机，但那些时机却像沙子般从指间溜走。法国贵族们纷纷逃离，财产被没收和破坏，名字也几乎被抹去。这一切，他自己比任何人都清楚，就像新上任的法国当局可能会因此而起诉他一样。
foreignization: 他自己的英国家园的幸福，总是积极工作的必要性，时间的迅速变化和接踵而来的麻烦，这些麻烦紧随其后，以至于本周的事件抹去了上周不成熟的计划，而下一周的事件使一切变得新的；他很清楚，他已经向这些情况的力量屈服了：—不免感到不安，但仍然没有持续和积累的抵抗。他曾观望时机，等待采取行动的时候，可是时机却在变化和挣扎中流逝，直到时机已经消失，贵族们从法国各条大路小道纷纷逃离，他们的财产正在被没收和毁灭，甚至他们的名字也被抹去。这一切，他自己都很清楚，就像任何可能因为这件事而指控他的新法国当局一样清楚。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The happiness of his own chosen English home, the necessity of being always actively employed, the swift changes and troubles of the time which had followed on one another so fast, that the events of this week annihilated the immature plans of last week, and the events of the week following made all new again; he knew very well, that to the force of these circumstances he had yielded:—not without disquiet, but still without continuous and accumulating resistance. That he had watched the times for a time of action, and that they had shifted and struggled until the time had gone by, and the nobility were trooping from France by every highway and byway, and their property was in course of confiscation and destruction, and their very names were blotting out, was as well known to himself as it could be to any new authority in France that might impeach him for it.

source: “As we happen to be alone for the moment, and are both people of business,” he said, when they had got back to the drawing-room and had sat down there in friendly relations, “let me ask you—does the Doctor, in talking with Lucie, never refer to the shoemaking time, yet?”
domestication: "说来也巧，我们现在巧遇无人打扰，而且我们都是办事的人，”当他们回到客厅，坐下后，大家都很融洽，他开口问道，“我就趁机请教一下，你有没有注意到，杜尔医生跟露西聊天的时候，是否曾经提起过他以前当鞋匠的事？”
foreignization: “正好我们现在是独处，而且我们都是务实的人，”他在他们回到客厅并坐下后，处于友好的关系中，说，“让我问你——医生和卢西说话时，从不提起那段制作鞋子的时光吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“As we happen to be alone for the moment, and are both people of business,” he said, when they had got back to the drawing-room and had sat down there in friendly relations, “let me ask you—does the Doctor, in talking with Lucie, never refer to the shoemaking time, yet?”
"说来也巧，我们现在巧遇无人打扰，而且我们都是办事的人，”当他们回到客厅，坐下后，大家都很融洽，他开口问道，“我就趁机请教一下，你有没有注意到，杜尔医生跟露西聊天的时候，是否曾经提起过他以前当鞋匠的事？”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“As we happen to be alone for the moment, and are both people of business,” he said, when they had got back to the drawing-room and had sat down there in friendly relations, “let me ask you—does the Doctor, in talking with Lucie, never refer to the shoemaking time, yet?”
“正好我们现在是独处，而且我们都是务实的人，”他在他们回到客厅并坐下后，处于友好的关系中，说，“让我问你——医生和卢西说话时，从不提起那段制作鞋子的时光吗？”

source: “Monseigneur, he was whiter than the miller. All covered with dust, white as a spectre, tall as a spectre!”
domestication: 大人，我跟您说，他比磨坊主还白！满身灰尘，白得像个鬼魂，高得像个鬼魂！
foreignization: “大人，他比磨坊主还白，全身被灰尘覆盖着，像鬼魂一样白，像鬼魂一样高！”

messages_domestication.content:

“Monseigneur, he was whiter than the miller. All covered with dust, white as a spectre, tall as a spectre!”
大人，我跟您说，他比磨坊主还白！满身灰尘，白得像个鬼魂，高得像个鬼魂！

messages_foreignization.content:

source: “I have been there, as you remember,” said Darnay, with a smile, though reddening a little angrily, “in another character, and not in a character that gives facilities for seeing much of it. They told me a curious thing when I was there.”
domestication: 达尼还记得，他曾经去过那里，不过那次他扮演的角色不同，也不是能让他看清事物真相的角色，当时有人跟他说了一件很有趣的事。达尼边说边笑了笑，脸颊也微微泛红，带着一丝恼怒。
foreignization: “我去过那里，如你所记得，”当纳利带着微笑说，尽管他有点生气地脸红了，“但那时候我是以另一种身份去的，并不是那种能让我有机会看清很多东西的角色。在我在那里的时候，他们告诉我一件奇怪的事情。”

source: “A bad truth for you,” said Defarge, speaking with knitted brows, and looking straight before him.
domestication: "这对你来说是个坏消息，”德法尔热皱着眉头，眼睛直视前方，说道。“算你倒霉”，他咆哮着说。他的眉头紧皱，目光直刺前方。

source: The half-dozen who were peering at the chain were still among the wheels, like sheep; the wheels turned so suddenly that they were lucky to save their skins and bones; they had very little else to save, or they might not have been so fortunate.
domestication: 那几个围着链条东瞅西看的人还在车轮旁转悠，像几只懵懵懂懂的绵羊。可车轮一下子就猛地一转，他们能保住皮肉算是运气好；要是他们身上还有其他值钱的东西，也许就没那么好运了。
foreignization: 观察链条的六个人仍然呆在车轮之间，就像绵羊一样；车轮转得如此突然，他们很幸运能够保住皮肤和骨头；他们几乎没有什么可保留的，否则他们可能不会这么幸运。

source: The passenger booked by this history, was on the coach-step, getting in; the two other passengers were close behind him, and about to follow. He remained on the step, half in the coach and half out of; they remained in the road below him. They all looked from the coachman to the guard, and from the guard to the coachman, and listened. The coachman looked back and the guard looked back, and even the emphatic leader pricked up his ears and looked back, without contradicting.
domestication: 这趟车根据历史记载，有一位乘客站在登车台阶上，正准备登车；另外两位乘客紧随其后，也准备跟着上车。登车台阶上的乘客，一半身子在车内，一半身子在车外；而另外两位乘客则停留在他下面的路边。他们都把目光从马车夫移到了警卫，又从警卫移到马车夫，专心地倾听。马车夫回头看了看，警卫也回头看了看，就连那个表情夸张的带头人也竖起耳朵回头看去，没有一个人表示反对。
foreignization: 根据这段历史，乘客正踏在马车的踏板上准备登车；另外两名乘客紧随其后，正准备跟着上车。他停留在踏板上，身体的上半部分在马车内，下半部分在马车外；他们站在他下面的路面上。他们都从车夫看向乘务员，又从乘务员看向车夫，静听着。车夫回头看了看，乘务员也回头看了看，甚至那匹带头的马竖起耳朵回头看，也没有表示反对。

The coachman looked back and the guard looked back, and even the emphatic leader pricked up his ears and looked back, without contradicting.

根据这段历史，乘客正踏在马车的踏板上准备登车；另外两名乘客紧随其后，正准备跟着上车。他停留在踏板上，身体的上半部分在马车内，下半部分在马车外；他们站在他下面的路面上。他们都从车夫看向乘务员，又从乘务员看向车夫，静听着。车夫回头看了看，乘务员也回头看了看，甚至那匹带头的马竖起耳朵回头看，也没有表示反对。

source: “As they descend the hill like madmen running a race, he falls. They laugh and pick him up again. His face is bleeding and covered with dust, but he cannot touch it; thereupon they laugh again. They bring him into the village; all the village runs to look; they take him past the mill, and up to the prison; all the village sees the prison gate open in the darkness of the night, and swallow him—like this!”
domestication: 他们像疯子一样从山坡上冲下来，跑得太快了，他摔倒在地。大家都哈哈大笑，然后把他扶起来。他脸上满是血和灰尘，但他无法碰自己的脸，这让他们又一次爆发出笑声。他们拖着他穿过村庄，整个村庄的人都出来围观；他们带着他经过磨坊，直到监狱；在夜晚的黑暗中，整个村庄都看到了监狱的大门打开，然后他就消失在里面——就这样！
foreignization: “当他们像疯子一样比赛般下山时，他摔倒了。他们大笑并再次把他扶起来。他的脸在流血，满是尘土，但他却无法碰触它；于是他们再次大笑。他们把他带进村庄；整个村庄都跑来围观；他们把他从磨坊旁边带过，并一直带到监狱；整个村庄看到夜幕中的监狱大门敞开，然后把他吞没——就像这样！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“As they descend the hill like madmen running a race, he falls. They laugh and pick him up again. His face is bleeding and covered with dust, but he cannot touch it; thereupon they laugh again. They bring him into the village; all the village runs to look; they take him past the mill, and up to the prison; all the village sees the prison gate open in the darkness of the night, and swallow him—like this!”
